title: Database doesn't import and causes a crash
body:
### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Export db from ViTune 1.0.3
2. Import to latest nightly build
3. Nightly build crashes and db is not imported (and everything that was there is wiped)

### Expected behavior

Database imports songs and playlists from stable to nightly build. 

### Actual behavior

App crashes, db not imported

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Logs

FATAL EXCEPTION: arch_disk_io_2
Process: app.vitune.android.nightly, PID: 26244

title: An unknown playback error has occurred
body:
### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Play a song that has not been downloaded yet
2. After 30s (524kb) or 59s (1.0MB), it stops and shows "An unknown playback error has occurred" regardless of the internet speed

### Expected behavior

The song should play and download seamlessly without the error showing

### Actual behavior

The song cached size will be stuck to either 524kb or 1.0MB, leading to playback error

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

title: Пропали  все скачанные песни и кэш
body:
### Steps to reproduce the bug

Я использовал самую последнюю версию приложения Vi tune 1.1.5 В один прекрасный день я был в автобусе у не было интернета я решил зайти в приложение и послушать музыку и оказывается все песни мною сказаные почти на 9 гигабайт беследно пропали вместе с кешом но прикол в том то что я не чистил память приложения и это повторилось еще один раз я был в шоке когда об этом узнал потому что в этом приложении скачивать песни это просто сущий гемор когда я увидел то что всех песен скачанных нет но само все остальное осталось по прежнему все альбомы сохраненые треки понравившиеся и даже история но все треки и кэш удалились 

### Expected behavior

Я был конечно в шоке и злой был на разработчиков данного приложения и это я щас пишу

### Actual behavior

То что я офигел с этого конечно щяс в приложении есть такая фигня то что псни просто не включаются и каждый раз мне приходится очищять кэш для нормального прослушивания музыки я эту проблему заметил на версии приложения 1.1.3 но разрабы зделали обновление и ничиго не поменялось и я тогда был в ярости

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

Я не скинуть фотку потому что потом удалил это приложение но поверте мне на слово моё событие которое со мной произошло реально так оно еще было не один раз а два так что я нормально объяснил надеюсь разработчики поймут меня потому что я говорю по русски надеюсь проблему решат

### Logs

_No response_

### ViTune version

1.1.5

### What kind of build are you using?

Release (GitHub / F-Droid)

### Android version

Андроид 12

### Device info

Я использую Redmi 9T на MIUI 14.0.3

### Upstream reproducibility

_No response_

### Additional information

Короче исправляйте проблему она мне надоела 
И исправьте проблему которая на версии 1.1.3 еще была то что музыка нормально не производится часто нужно чистить кэш это надоедает и песни нормально не скачиваются

title: [BUG] Tablets found bugs
body: TabA9 using latest nightly build Dont know other tablet users if they have issues as well Anyway, Found 3: ****** In any channels tab at landscape mode, these sections are not properly wrapped in tablet's layout which make it difficult to click ****** Related Video cards and player are overlapping at portrait mode ******* Pop up miniplayer is not respecting its edges as it only fills ~55% i guess. See image. But, when i test again, it now fills the edges right. Honest thought, Table layout of Flow App is not really good especially handling using back and forth portrait and landscape mode, just see those video cards in related videos,

title: [BUG] Pop-up UI elements have broken swipes
body:
## Describe the Bug
Any pop-up element, for example comments section or video chapter viewer, have very buggy swipes. It happens on all the elements that have the small white bar at the top and can be "put down".



## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open Flow and navigate to any video
3. Tap on comments section/chapter view/anything with the little bar separator thing
4. Perform swipes
5. Observe the issue
## Expected Behavior
The UI elements swipe up and down


## Actual Behavior
The whole section wants to close itself



## Screenshots or Screen Recordings
https://gofile.io/d/ANNws3



## Environment

|-------|-------|
| **Device** | e.g. Pixel 8 Pro |

title: [BUG] Autoplay does not continue in background or screen-off mode
body:
Describe the Bug

Autoplay works correctly when I stay inside the app with the screen on. However, when I leave the app (background mode) or turn the screen off, playback stops after one video and does not continue to the next.

Expected Behavior

Autoplay / continuous playback should also work in background mode and when the screen is off, similar to YouTube.

Steps to Reproduce

1. Play any video
2. Stay inside the app → next video plays automatically
3. Leave the app or turn off the screen
4. Playback stops after one video (no continuous play)

Device Info

- Device: TECNO CL6
- Android Version: (write your version here)

Additional Context

Background playback is supported, but continuous autoplay does not work in background/screen-off mode. This creates an inconsistent experience.

@viharm Could you please check this?

title: [BUG] skipping video with screen off, then turning on screen will restart video
body: On the nightly build, when the screen is off and if I skip the video to the next one then turn the screen on, the video will reload and start all over again. It doesn't matter how long I let the video play with the screen off before I turn it back on. It'll have the same effect.

title: [BUG] When I open a YouTube video link directly in Flow, the video goes black / when I exit the app and return, it takes a while for the video to start playing again.
body:
<!-- 
  Thanks for taking the time to report a bug! 
  Please fill out as much detail as possible — the more we know, 
  the faster we can fix it.
-->

## Describe the Bug
<!-- A clear, concise summary of what's going wrong. -->
when I open a link directly in Flow the video starts black leaving the sound of the video and I need to close the video and open it again to work / When I exit the app and come back the video takes a while and returns to resume normally 


## Steps to Reproduce
<!-- Walk us through exactly how to trigger the bug. Be specific. -->

BUG 1
1. open any YouTube link to open directly in Flow
2. Will starts with black screen and play sound

BUG 2
1. Open any video from Flow
2. Exit the app and come back


## Expected Behavior
<!-- What SHOULD have happened instead? -->
BUG 1
Open and reproduce the video normally 

BUG 2
returns to where it was in the background


## Actual Behavior
<!-- What ACTUALLY happened? Include any error messages if visible. -->

BUG 1
opens video link from another platform in flow it starts black 

BUG 2
The video crashes like a time and then goes back to normal 


## Screenshots or Screen Recordings

  If possible, attach a screenshot or screen recording showing the issue.
  This helps enormously — especially for UI bugs.

  Drag and drop files here or paste from clipboard.

title: [BUG] Add English in app language
body: Just add English in app language I want choose English but cannot find it

title: [BUG] Subscribing from feed replaces the channel image with the video thumbnail
body:
## Describe the Bug
If you subscribe to a channel straight from the feed or search, the channel’s profile picture gets replaced by the thumbnail of that video. And it stays like that everywhere—in your feed and subscriptions.
Only way for it to not happen, open the channel page and subscribe from there.



## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Flow and find any video from an unsubscribed channel
2. Tap on the three dots on the video card 
3. And from the selections choose 'Subscribe'
4. Then everywhere in the app the channel image with the thumbnail from the video

## Expected Behavior
The channel image should be the same one in main page, throughout the app

## Actual Behavior
The channel image is actually the thumbnail of the video.



## Environment
<!-- Please complete ALL of the following. -->

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Device** | Moto G52 |
| **Android Version** | Android 13 |
| **Flow Version** | 2.1.0 |
| **Installation Source** | GitHub APK
| **ROM** | Stock  |

## Frequency
<!-- How often does this happen? -->

- [x] Every time (100% reproducible)
- [ ] Often (happens most of the time)
- [ ] Sometimes (happens intermittently)
- [ ] Rarely (happened once or twice)

## Affected Area
<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] Video playback
- [ ] Music player
- [x] Home feed / Recommendations
- [x] Search
- [ ] Downloads
- [x] Subscriptions
- [ ] Playlists
- [ ] Picture-in-Picture / Background playback
- [ ] Notifications
- [ ] Settings / Preferences
- [ ] Import / Export
- [ ] Casting
- [ ] SponsorBlock / DeArrow
- [ ] Lyrics / Subtitles
- [ ] UI / Theming
- [ ] Performance / Battery
- [ ] Other (describe below)

title: Email field doesn't verify email format
body:
Device: Nexus 6P
Android Version: 8.0.0
Commit: 1ce0e98

Bug: Input field allows for multiple `@` symbols to be used in the field for email, most likely not verifying it's a valid email format. 

Reproduce: Fresh install of commit, entering in multiple `@` symbols during email entry

title: e-mail entry validation
body:
Issue: e-mail input allows for any wildcard entry so long as it contains an "@" symbol, should conform to a rigid email standard like:

`username`@`subdomain`.`domain`.`TLD`

Where there are `0..*` `subdomains` and `1` `domain` and `1` `TLD` 

Reproduction Steps:
Reproducible on all branches.

title: Crash on New Course creation
body:
Device: Nexus 6P
Android Version: 8.0.0
Commit: 1ce0e98

Bug: Application crashes on fresh install when entering a new course

Reproduce: Fresh install of commit, select a new course, enter in the field and submit.

title: [Bug] Pictures coming from folders other than local ones are not correctly previewed
body:
**Describe the bug**
I selected some wallpapers from a remote location instead of a local folder: the file manager in my device lets me pick images from applications like Google Drive and Nextcloud. I can successfully select pictures, but they don't get a preview in the album, although I can select them and see their outline when I hold on one of them.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Library
2. Create an album
3. Tap on '+' then 'Add images'
4. Select a remote source of files like Google Drive or Nextcloud
5. Select an image

**Expected behavior**
Images should be previewed regardless of the source

**Screenshots**

<!-- Failed to upload "screen-20260228-095700.mp4" -->

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Motorola Moto g 5G Plus
 - OS: Android 11
 - Version: 2.1.0

title: [Bug] <RANDOM WALLPAPER CROP>
body:
**Describe the bug**
Please update, fix this bug where the wallpaper will zoom in extremely every 5 or so wallpapers when on shuffle. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Put some wallpaper images on a shuffle
2. wait

**Expected behavior**
expected behavior is it doesn't randomly zoom in

**Screenshots**
[here is an imgur.com album with 2 examples](https://imgur.com/a/o3PSJY6) Apologies, I can't embed an image on mobile.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: samsung tab s7+
 - OS: oneui 5
 - app version: latest

**Additional context**
Only way to fix it is to completely restart the app, or go in and switch modes (eg from fit to fill), then switch back

title: [Bug] "Select folder" option isn't properly visible
body:
**Describe the bug**
On my device, tapping on the "+" button inside an album opens the options to load images but doesn't fully extend, therefore the second option ("Select folder") stays hidden behind the "-" button.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to Library
2. Click or create an album
3. Tap on the "+" button

**Expected behavior**
All the options in the list should be properly visible

**Screenshots**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/584c5fad-d1a2-4c30-bd64-10c7a9791692

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Motorola Moto g 5G Plus
 - OS: Android 11
 - Version: 2.1.0

**Additional context**
I don't think it should matter, but this device has a ratio of 21:9, being much taller than other devices.

title: [Bug] Separate albums , deselecting albums not working properly
body:
**Describe the bug**
Selecting/deselecting albums for wallpapers not working properly. Also, changing selection under lock screen changes it for home screen as well.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to 'Wallpaper' tab
2. Enable individual scheduling
3. Click on wallpaper album selection for home screen or lock screen.
4. Click on an album name to select/deselect.
5. Error: Either doesn't change anything at all or deselects the opposite album instead.

**Expected behavior**
Separate album selection for lock screen and home screen. Clicking on a selected album deselects it.

**Smartphone:**
 - Device: Redmi Note 10T 
 - OS: Android 13 (MIUI global 14)

**Additional Information**
Current app version 3.2.1

title: [Bug] The theme is being overridden for no reason
body:
**Describe the bug**
The app overrides the current theme instead of simply changing it's background on lockscreen/background.
Additionally an option that would change the background every time you turn the phone on would be nice (so not every minute, but simply every time it wakes up)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Just turn it the setting on, and boom it changed the theme.

**Expected behavior**
It should not change the theme and simply change the background, and if it really wants to change the theme then at least clone the current one, and then switch to it. Additionally every time a power button is clicked to turn on the phone the wallpaper should change. Simillarly to how Lockscreen Magazine works in OnePlus

**Screenshots**
How it looks like (the theme was overriden, thus the font is different)
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cbb30e7e-23f7-49ad-bd15-df1f190723e8)
How I want it to look
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ce09c7b-ee70-4f42-b7e0-51bfe71770dd)

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: OnePlus 15
 - OS: OxygenOS 16.0.3.503
 - Version v3.2.1

title: [Bug] Perfectly cropped wallpaper zoom in
body: Despite cropping and resizing a picture to the exact size of the screen (1080x2424), when wallpaper is set trough Paperize it is slightly zoomed in. Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Create a wallpaper collection containing one picture that has exact dimensions of the screen; 2. Set it for both home and lock screen using any fitting mode; 3. Go to the home screen and see zoomed in wallpaper. Perfectly sized pictures should fit perfectly. Screenshot: Wallpaper: Smartphone: - Device: Pixel 9a; - OS: GrapheneOS 2025120400; - Version v3.2.1. Does not appear to be related to #399

title: [Bug] Wallpaper zoom issue
body:
so, basically whenever i use an app which uses miniplayer(for eg-youtube, chrome when someone is watching a video on the browser etc.) the wallpaper on my homescreen zooms out infinitly 

note-1.) i use yt revanced (which enables me the miniplayer feature even if i leave yt)
2.) this happened with me with yt and chrome (both having miniplayer option)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to yt or chrome
2. start a video 
3. click the home button or come out of your app
4. see your wallpaper zoomed out


Theory- i feel like this maybe happening because miniplayer also uses draw over other apps which paperize uses as well so it might be causing problems

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/976bea91-941d-45e3-ae8c-8ee5654656e1)

one is the normal perfect one and other is the one after using yt

**Smartphone 
 - Device: samsung galaxy m32 5g
 - OS: One ui 5.1
 - Version 3.2.1

title: [Bug] Privacy Notice Page Returns to Welcome Screen After Rotation
body: **Describe the bug** In portrait mode, tapping the Privacy Notice button opens the Privacy Notice page. After rotating to landscape mode, it returns to the Welcome screen. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Tap the Privacy Notice button 2. Rotate to landscape mode. 3. See error, returns to the Welcome screen. **Expected behavior** After rotating to landscape, the Privacy Notice page should remain open, consistent with the portrait mode. **Screenshots** **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: Pixel 6a - Android version：Android 13 (API 33) - Version 2.1.0 (31)

title: [Bug] the Privacy Notice button disappears in landscape mode
body: **Describe the bug** After rotation, the Privacy Notice button disappears. In landscape mode, scrolling is not possible, so it cannot be found by scrolling. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Rotate to landscape mode. 2. See error. The **Privacy Notice** button disappears, and scrolling is not possible. **Expected behavior** The Privacy Notice button should remain visible after rotation, or it should be accessible by scrolling. **Screenshots** **Smartphone (please complete the following information):** - Device: Pixel 6a - Android version：Android 13 (API 33) - Version 2.1.0 (31)

title: Detection of Silent SMS (part 2)
body:
The [original thread](https://github.com/SecUpwN/Android-IMSI-Catcher-Detector/issues/69) (#69) was getting far too long, so this is the continuation of that thread. 

**Please use old thread as reference only!**

title: Broken AT Command Interface
body:
I am always using the latest version compiled from our `development` branch and my [testing device](https://github.com/SecUpwN/Android-IMSI-Catcher-Detector/wiki/Testing-Devices) - it seems as if the `AT Command Interface` does not react to any command on `/dev/smd0`, especially not those described in the article [Get Kc key and TMSI number](https://ferrancasanovas.wordpress.com/2014/01/28/get-kc-key-and-tmsi-number/). @larsgrefer, are you able to confirm this?
## <bountysource-plugin>

Want to back this issue? **[Post a bounty on it!](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/30008585-broken-at-command-interface?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F565750&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github)** We accept bounties via [Bountysource](https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F565750&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github).
</bountysource-plugin>

title: Bountysource does not update GitHub Issues
body:
We currently experience an Issue with @Bountysource: When creating or updating a `bounty` for our project on [![Donations](https://www.bountysource.com/badge/team?team_id=40338&style=bounties_received)](https://www.bountysource.com/teams/android-imsi-catcher-detector/issues?utm_source=Android%20IMSI-Catcher%20Detector&utm_medium=shield&utm_campaign=bounties_received), the GitHub Issue titles are **not** being updated with e.g. `[$50]`, even though all permissions have been granted. This Issue has been first noticed by @Nordlenning and seems to appear ever since we moved our project into the new organization.

I am currently manually updating the Issue titles. @rappo, please help me investigating this here!

title: F-Droid builds need special workarounds
body:
> Thank you for your feedback! Please add below details so that we can help you a bit faster.

---
#### Agreements

> Please check these boxes to ensure you've actually read and understood these important rules!
- [x] **I have reviewed and accepted the [guidelines for contributing](https://github.com/SecUpwN/Android-IMSI-Catcher-Detector/blob/development/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) to this project.**
- [x] **I have searched the [open Issues](https://github.com/SecUpwN/Android-IMSI-Catcher-Detector/issues) and made sure I am not filing a duplicate.**
- [x] **I have read the notes on [debugging](https://github.com/SecUpwN/Android-IMSI-Catcher-Detector/blob/development/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#debugging) and will properly maintain this Issue.**

---
#### Overview

> Please add a short and easy to understand description of the Issue you've experienced here.

First introduced with `0.1.40-alpha-normal`, the [metedata of our app on F-Droid](https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroiddata/blob/master/metadata/com.SecUpwN.AIMSICD.txt) seems to need a [special workaround](https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroiddata/commit/9b6634cc1f0fd4def7196f04a682da5f0d61c25f) and a forced version to be able to be built by F-Droid. We need to fix this to prevent nagging @krt and other F-Droid members. Thanks for digging a bit deeper into this, @larsgrefer!
- `AIMSICD` version (see the `About` screen): Bleeding edge
- Phone and ROM running _current_ release: HTC One M7 / not relevant for this Issue
- Link to logfile without identifyable data: [F-Droid Buildlog of version 0.1.41-alpha-normal](https://f-droid.org/wiki/index.php?title=com.SecUpwN.AIMSICD/lastbuild_41&action=edit&oldid=99769)

```
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
A problem occurred configuring root project 'com.SecUpwN.AIMSICD'.
> No signature of method: java.util.ArrayList.toSorted() is applicable for argument types: (org.codehaus.groovy.runtime.NumberAwareComparator) values: [org.codehaus.groovy.runtime.NumberAwareComparator@1160497]
  Possible solutions: toSet(), toString(), toString(), toString(), toString(), sort()
```

---
#### Reproduction

> Please tell us the detailed steps on how to reproduce your Issue here.

Please see the attached logfile of the F-Droid build server. @f-droid, can you point us to what is wrong?

---
#### References

> If your Issue is related to any other existing Issues or pull requests, please link them here.

https://gitlab.com/fdroid/fdroiddata/issues/423
## <bountysource-plugin>

Want to back this issue? **[Post a bounty on it!](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/32072120-f-droid-builds-need-special-workarounds?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F565750&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github)** We accept bounties via [Bountysource](https://www.bountysource.com/?utm_campaign=plugin&utm_content=tracker%2F565750&utm_medium=issues&utm_source=github).
</bountysource-plugin>

title: OpenCellID API key is not immediately displayed
body:
**EDIT by SecUpwN:** @agilob, we have implemented `Issue Templates` for a reason! Please use it!

---

Description of Issue: After saving the new OCID API key, it does not show immediately in the field `OpenCellID API Key`. It is empty. Only after exiting the `Settings` and re-entering that field the key is shown. The way it should work is that it is immmediately shown when pressing that button after fetching it.

title: App crashes when importing settings backup
body:
The app crashes when importing a settings backup that contains modified values, whereas importing the default settings works fine.
 
1. Created a backup of the app's default settings (fresh install state).
2. Closed the app completely and restarted it.
3. Imported the default backup successfully.
4. Repeated the process, but modified a setting beforehand to create a custom configuration.
5. Upon importing this custom backup, the app crashed.

7.11.1

_No response_

title: Search bar text is not centered vertically (UI issue)
body:
### What happened? It may be due to my system font settings, but the search bar label is not centered vertically on my device. It would be better to have the text of label even trimmed from the top and the bottom a bit, if the text were too big, but having it this way makes it really ugly. That's the first thing you notice after opening the app.

I would also be glad to have an option to change this search bar to search icon in the top right edge, but I can see that there has been already such request ( #241 ). For me it would be the perfect workaround, even better than fixing this visual bug. ### App Version 7.4.2 ### Android Version (API Level) _No response_ ### (Optional) Relevant log output ```shell ```

title: [BUG] Possible bug with the 'Update All' button
body:

- [x] I have searched the issue tracker for [open](https://github.com/Droid-ify/client/issues) and [closed](https://github.com/Droid-ify/client/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) issues that are similar to the feature request I want to file, without success.

### What's going wrong?

I’ve checked and haven’t found anything in the reports that matches exactly what I’m about to describe.
When you click on the ‘Updates’ tab and then on the ‘Update All’ button, I notice that the apps in the update list download without any issues, but the selection window to confirm the installation doesn’t appear. I am forced to click on each app and, once in the details window, click ‘Update’ again to bring up the window that allows the installation. I’m not sure if this is a bug or if it’s designed that way.

https://streamable.com/rwp0d3

When you watch the video, make sure you watch it right to the end, as it might seem very short, but that’s because I’m waiting for the update to finish downloading; however, the progress bar isn’t displayed, which might be confusing. Once the download is complete, you’ll see that I tapped on the app I want to update, as the window to confirm the installation doesn’t appear automatically.

### How do we reproduce it?

_No response_

### What did you expect?

I hope that once the updates have been downloaded, clicking the ‘Update All’ button will bring up a window asking me to confirm the update for each app individually.

Oneplus 12

Android 16

title: [BUG] [0.7.1] This version is signed with a different signature than the one installed on your device
body:

- [x] This issue contains only one bug.

### What's going wrong?

I just upgraded from 0.6.6 to 0.7.1, and now every app is telling me the signature is wrong with the error message:

"This version is signed with a different signature than the one installed on your device".

if I reinstall 0.6.6, this is not a problem.



### How do we reproduce it?

1. open the app (0.7.1)
2. go to any installed app info page.
3. error is shown.

### What did you expect?

Normally it would... not show this error? 0.6.6 and 0.6.8 do not show this error, but 0.6.8 has a bug that crashes on android 7.1 so I am forced to use an older version.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Your phone

Droid 4

### Android version

Android 7.1

### Droid-ify version

0.7.1

### Anything else?

"Ignore signature verification" is not checked in settings when using droid-ify 0.6.6 or 0.6.8 and it does not show the error, so 0.7.1 should not require "Ignore signature verification" to be checked in settings to realize that the apps were installed by previous versions of droid-ify

title: Birthday Adapter incompatibility
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:calendar) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:calendar) - to which I could contribute my information.

- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.9.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Any

### Affected device model

Any

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Which calendar service or provider do you use, if any?

None

### Which sync adapter app do you use for calendar synchronization, if any?

Birthday Adapter

### Steps to reproduce the bug

"Sync is not broken, only delayed.
Unfortunately this is an issue which I cannot fix in the scope of this project." - [Birthday Adapter dev](https://github.com/SufficientlySecure/birthday-calendar/issues/132#issuecomment-3694770162)

* Birthday Adapter
  - Follow the instructions (step 1-3 in the Main tab)
    - Enable Birthday Adapter
      - Select "ALLOW" in the "Allow Birthday Adapter to access your calendar?" prompt.
      - Select "ALLOW" in the "Allow Birthday Adapter to access your contacts?" prompt.
* Contacts: Create a contact with birthday 1 January this year
* Fossify Calendar
  - Settings -> Enable "CalDAV sync"
  - "Allow Calendar to access your calendar?": ALLOW
  - Select calendars to sync: BIRTHDAY ADAPTER [section], enable Birthdays
* Birthday Adapter
  - Navigate to Preferences
  - Click on "Synchronize now!"
* Fossify Calendar, the contact birthday will now be visible in the calendar
* Contacts: Change the birthday to 3 January
* Birthday Adapter
  - Navigate to Preferences
  - Click on "Synchronize now!"

# Software
* Fossify Calendar 3.7
* Multiple android versions

### Expected behavior

The date of the contact birthday should be updated to 3rd January in Fossify Calendar.

### Actual behavior

The date of the contact birthday is not updated to 3rd January in Fossify Calendar.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

title: [Accessibility] Increasing font and display sizes in the OS is making the monthly views impossible to use
body: ### Checklist - [x] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/releases/latest). - [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information. - [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:calendar) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:calendar) - to which I could contribute my information. - [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed. - [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.** - [x] This issue contains only one bug. - [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md). ### Affected app version 1.10.3 ### Affected Android/Custom ROM version /e/OS 3.7.1-a14 - Android 14 ### Affected device model Fairphone 4 ### How did you install the app? F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid ### Which calendar service or provider do you use, if any? _No response_ ### Which sync adapter app do you use for calendar synchronization, if any? _No response_ ### Steps to reproduce the bug 1. Go to Android settings to increase font size to at least 6/7 and display size to at least to 4/6. 2. Open Fossify Calendar in the classic monthly view. 3. Click on the dates of the first line: everything is OK. 4. Click on the dates of the second line: it doesn't open the right date but the following date. For example, for May 2026: the Monday 4th opens the 5th, the 5th opens the 6th, etc. 5. Click on the dates of the third line: the 11th opens the 13th, the 12th opens the 14th, etc. 6. Click on the dates of the fourth line: the 18th opens the 21th, the 19th opens the 22th, etc. 7. Check the "Monthly and daily" view: it's the same and you can see the wrong day focused and guess that the buttons are overlapping. So these monthly views are not usable because a lot of days can't be selected at all… ### Expected behavior Clicking on a day opens the right day even if the font and display sizes are increased. Visually impaired people should be able to use the app. ### Actual behavior Clicking on a day opens the wrong day from the second line of a monthly view when font and display sizes are increased. ### Screenshots/Screen recordings _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

### Affected app version

1.3.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 15 (GrapheneOS Build 2025060200)

### Affected device model

Pixel 6

### How did you install the app?

GitHub releases

### Which calendar service or provider do you use, if any?

Baikal

### Which sync adapter app do you use for calendar synchronization, if any?

DAVx5

### Steps to reproduce the bug

**For setup:**

1. Install Fossify Calendar, DAVx5 and configure it to sync with a Selfhosted Baikal CalDav Server.
2. Disable battery optimization for both Fossify Calendar and DAVx5.
3. Make sure CalDAV sync is enabled within Fossify Calendar's settings.

**Scenario 1: Create Event in Fossify Calendar:**

1. Open Fossify Calendar.
2. Create a new event.
3. Try to manually refresh the calendar within Fossify Calendar (e.g., pulldown gesture).
4. Notice that changes on the server (Baikal) or another synced client are not showing up.
5. Go into DAVx5 app and manually refresh.
6. Notice the changes are immediately reflected on the server side

**Scenario 2: Creating an event elsewhere:**

1. Create an from some other synchronized client, and make sure it shows up on the Baikal server (it will).
2. Open Fossify Calendar.
3. Try to manually refresh the calendar within Fossify Calendar (e.g., pulldown gesture).
4. Notice the new event will not show up.
5. Go into DAVx5 app and manually refresh.
6. Notice that changes are immediately reflected in the Fossify calendar app.

### Expected behavior

The event created in Fossify Calendar should synchronize with the CalDAV server (Baikal) either immediately or upon manual refresh (pulldown gesture). Other synced clients should then reflect this new event.
Changes made on the CalDAV server (Baikal) or another synced client should be retrieved and reflected in the Fossify Calendar app either automatically (based on sync intervals) or upon manual refresh (pulldown gesture).

### Actual behavior

Fossify Calendar is failing to synchronize calendar events with DAVx5, both for events created within Fossify Calendar and for events created externally. Manual refreshing within Fossify Calendar (e.g., via pulldown gesture) and automatic synchronization intervals are not triggering the sync. Synchronization only occurs when manually initiated from the DAVx5 application.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Calendar/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

Trunk

### Steps to reproduce the bug

Hi, I'm working on a research prototype static analyzer targeting Kotlin-based Android apps, and running it against this repo. **It detected a small threading issue on shortcut setup in `MainActivity`.**

## Checked file:

```text
app/src/main/kotlin/org/fossify/calendar/activities/MainActivity.kt
```

## What I found

In `MainActivity`, the app updates dynamic shortcuts from the Activity path:

```kotlin
shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = shortcuts
```

This is a Kotlin property call, but underneath it maps to:

```kotlin
ShortcutManager.setDynamicShortcuts(shortcuts)
```

Android documents `ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(...)` as a potentially slow API that should only be called from a worker thread. Since this code is reached from `MainActivity.onResume()` and there is no visible worker-thread dispatch around the shortcut publishing call, this appears to run on the main thread.

## Verified bug trace

```text
org.fossify.calendar.activities.MainActivity.onResume()
  -> prepares/updates dynamic launcher shortcuts
  -> MainActivity.kt, around line 480:
       shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = shortcuts
  -> Kotlin property setter calls:
       ShortcutManager.setDynamicShortcuts(List<ShortcutInfo>)
  -> platform shortcut service / launcher-facing shortcut state update
  -> documented as potentially taking several seconds
  -> executed from the Activity main-thread path
```

TLDR: The slow platform call is made from the Activity lifecycle path, without an obvious `Dispatchers.IO`, executor, or other worker-thread boundary around the `dynamicShortcuts` assignment. If the launcher/system side is slow, this can block the main Looper and show up as jank, delayed startup, or, in the worst case, an ANR.

## Documentation Reference

Android documents `ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(...)` as a potentially slow call:

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/pm/ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(java.util.List%3Candroid.content.pm.ShortcutInfo%3E)

## Possible fix

The shortcut objects can still be prepared as they are today, but the actual publishing call should probably be moved off the UI thread:

```kotlin
val shortcutsToPublish = shortcuts

lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
    shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = shortcutsToPublish
}
```

If you prefer not to use coroutines here, a small background executor would also be fine. The main point is to keep `setDynamicShortcuts(...)` away from the main thread.

Android 13

### Affected device model

Realme 10

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

Using latest nextcloud version 28.0.1 causes a problem with calendar invitations. 

Usually if you create an event on a Android device using simple mobile calendar having attendees invited nextcloud sends an invitation email to the user. 

Since the last update of simple calendar this emails are not sent anymore. This means if you invite members to an event in simply calendar and you sync it to next cloud then this invitation emails are not sent anymore. Even if you update any shared events updates are not sent via email. Only if you delete an event with attendees an email will be sent to this users.

If you use Google calendar on the Android device or if you create any calendar entries with NTS in for example Thunderbird calendar these emails are sent correctly.

So it seems that within simple calendar that must be an issue that causes nextcloud not sending any emails.

Hopefully you can invest some time to check if this can be fixed within simple calendar app as this app is gorgeous.

Thank you

### Expected behavior

Calendar events with invited members should be sent via nextcloud email. The calendar app creates events different to e.g. Google calendar where mentioned events are working fine 

### Actual behavior

As described above

_No response_

_No response_

DAVx5

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. In Davx5, set up an account to synchronise some Calendar (from Nextcloud).
2. In Fossify Calendar, refresh to get the new calendar.
3. In Parameters, choose Manage calendars.
4. Change the title and the colour of the synced calendar.

### Expected behavior

The calendar should appear with the chosen name and colour.

### Actual behavior

The calendar name reflects the chosen title, but resets after a while.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

There is a setting in Davx5, under CalDAV, to state that the colour of the calendar should be set in the calendar app options, and this does work. However, there is no such setting for the title of the calendar.

The question is bugging me because I get calendars from several organisation that I am a member of, and within their respective Nextcloud instance, they are given a title that makes sense in their respective context, but not in mine. For example, OrgA and OrgB will have a Bureau or Comité calendar, and I need them to appear as OrgA and OrgB respectively, not as Bureau.

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Expected behavior

The picture should be displayed in full

The default app for images, or videos respectively, is opened. E.g. Fossify Gallery.
But this is not allowed on the locked phone, so you need to first unlock to view the taken image/video

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

The image/video should directly open and be viewable without unlocking.

Stretch: All images/videos captured in this app session, should be viewable, allowing to swipe through them.

### Additional information

Making Fossify Camera available from lock screen apparently doesn't always work: #52

On my Emulator and on my non-Samsung phone it works like this:

1. Install the application
2. Start camera from lock screen.
3. This will now not open a camera but instead ask to unlock. Because it will show you the app selector, with the stock camera app and Fossify Camera up for selection
4. Select Fossify Camera and "Always"
5. From now on, the Fossify camera will open directly

Pixel 10 & Emulator Intel x86_64

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Before opening the app, rotate the device to landscape.
    - From own experience: for reliable reproduction, make sure the phone sensors have actually registered the rotation change, before advancing to the next step. Else, you will not see the bug
 2. Open the app
 3. Take a picture or capture a video
 4. Open the picture / video

### Expected behavior

The picture/video should be taken in the orientation you saw it during preview (landscape)

### Actual behavior

The picture/video is taken in portrait rotation.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/80c9d31d-9bdb-47e6-a269-23787fdf7db3

The issue only happens when rotating before starting the app. All rotations happening while the app is already open are handled correctly. Video of that failed to upload - probably the device rotations during capture confused the Emulator screen recording.

### Additional information

Maybe related to #37, but as one user stated there:
> Confirmed that turning the option to save metadata on resolves it

this seems to be two different issues. My bug happens with metadata saving on and off.

title: `setDynamicShortcuts()` is called from the Activity main thread
body:
Hi, I'm working on a research prototype static analyzer targeting Kotlin-based Android apps, and running it against this repo. It detected a small threading issue.

### Checklist

- [x] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Clock/releases/latest).
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Clock/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Clock/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:clock) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:clock) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed.
- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Clock/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

Trunk

## Checked file:

```text
app/src/main/kotlin/org/fossify/clock/activities/MainActivity.kt
```

## What I found

In `MainActivity`, `onResume()` calls `checkShortcuts()`. Inside `checkShortcuts()`, the stopwatch shortcut is published like this:

```kotlin
shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = listOf(stopWatchShortcutInfo)
```

This property setter calls `ShortcutManager.setDynamicShortcuts(...)`. Android's API documentation warns that this method may take several seconds to complete and should only be called from a worker thread.

## Verified bug trace

```text
org.fossify.clock.activities.MainActivity.onResume()
  -> checkShortcuts()
  -> MainActivity.kt, around line 133:
       shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = listOf(stopWatchShortcutInfo)
  -> Kotlin property setter calls:
       ShortcutManager.setDynamicShortcuts(List<ShortcutInfo>)
  -> platform shortcut state is updated through ShortcutManager
  -> documented as potentially taking several seconds
  -> call is reached from the Activity main-thread lifecycle path
  -> possible UI stall / delayed startup / ANR risk
```

I did not see a worker-thread boundary around this specific shortcut publishing call. There is an `ensureBackgroundThread { ... }` used elsewhere in the same Activity for alarm rescheduling, but not around `shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts`.

## Why I think this is worth changing

This probably does not fail every time, and it may be fast on many devices. The problem is that the platform API is documented as potentially slow, and the work may depend on system/launcher behavior. If it takes longer than expected, it can block UI drawing and input handling.

## Suggested change

Move just the publishing call to a worker thread:

```kotlin
val shortcuts = listOf(stopWatchShortcutInfo)

lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
    shortcutManager.dynamicShortcuts = shortcuts
}
```

This keeps the UI thread free while preserving the same shortcut behavior.

## Reference

Android documents `ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(...)` as a potentially slow call:

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/pm/ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(java.util.List%3Candroid.content.pm.ShortcutInfo%3E)

Android 15 / Xiaomi HyperOS 2.0.202.0

### Affected device model

Poco F7 Pro

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Set phone to vibrate/silent mode
2. Tap the "Create timer" (+) button
3. Set a timer for 30 seconds and make sure a timer sound is selected
4. Start the timer
5. Send the app to the background (go to home screen) and turn off screen
6. Wait for the timer to fire

### Expected behavior

I expect the selected timer sound to be played even if my phone is in vibrate/silent mode.

### Actual behavior

A "Timer expired" notification is displayed and, if configured for the timer, the phone vibrates but no sound is played.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

I think this is due to the way timers currently work in the app (I haven't looked at the code yet so I'm just guessing from what I can see in the app): Creating a local notification with the specified sound when the timer is up. However, notification sounds will not be played if the phone is in vibrate/silent mode.

see steps in report

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid ### Steps to reproduce the bug 1. Open the following PDF [test.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27279225/test.pdf) 2. Scan the QR-Code on your Android mobile device 3. Open in Contacts 4. Contact Crashes, Address is not imported The QR-Code contains a VCard, generated from LaTeX as follows: ``` ``` Result: If the ADR field is absent, the contact is imported, as soon as there is an ADR field, Contacts crashes. Regardless what other fields are defined and regardless on what is in the ADR field. The visiting card is generated with xeLaTeX usig my template: [businesscard-qrcode](https://mrw.dev/templates/businesscard-qrcode) ```LaTeX \documentclass{businesscard-qrcode} \begin{document} \givennames{Test} \familynames{Test} \type{work} \street{Technoparkstrasse\ 2} \city{Winterthur} \region{ZH} \zip{8406} \country{Switzerland} \drawcard \end{document} ``` ### Expected behavior VCard from QR-Code is imported in Contacts. ### Actual behavior Contacts crashes when there is an ADR line. Sometimes I get a crash report dialog, but most often it just does not open and switches back to the QR scanner. ### Screenshots/Screen recordings _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

title: PSA: Fossify Contacts backup or sync may silently lose some event date fields from your contacts
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/releases/latest).
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:contacts) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:contacts) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed.
- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.6.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

GrapheneOS 2026040800 (Android 16)

### Affected device model

Google Pixel

### How did you install the app?

Google Play Store

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Add a new contact with more than one birthday and/or more than one anniversary and/or one or more "other" event dates
2. Export the contact or use the backup function

OR

2. Synchronise via DAVx5

### Expected behavior

All fields should be stored when exporting the contact to vCard.
All fields should be stored when synchronising/saving to a CardDAV server.

### Actual behavior

When exporting / backing up to a vCard file, Fossify Contacts removes ALL events entered as "other". They're gone. That's it.

When synchronising to a CardDAV server via DAVx5 only **one** birthday and **one** anniversary is stored. All other birthdays and anniversaries in that contact are discarded. They stay stored on the local storage (the Android Address Book), but not in the CardDAV server. (Maybe multiple birthday and multiple anniversary fields are not allowed by the vCard spec?) I've tested with vCard v3.0 (Nextcloud Contacts and Radicale) and v4.0 (Radicale).

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_Not provided_

### Additional information

I think this deserves some attention as data loss is likely if you don't have a backup of BOTH the CardDAV server and Fossify Contacts export/backup.

The issue is probably linked to:
[Support all NextCloud-supported contact attributes.](#85) #85
[Add "Custom" label to Dates in a contact entry](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/issues/154) #154 
[Support Import of Apple & Google's vCard v.3.0 Extension, itemN.X-ABLabel. Fossify Supports these as "Custom"](#187) #187
[export does not export date field "other"](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/issues/145) #145

I will switch to the stock GrapheneOS Contacts app for now. It only allows adding a single birthday and a single anniversary, and multiple "other" and "custom" (name them yourself) event date fields. These seem to get synchronised correctly via DAVx5, but exporting the contacts directly from that app drops a lot of info too as it uses vCard v2.1

Edit: I think it's worth looking at https://github.com/Goodwy/Contacts. Their export system works as expected however they also allow multiple "Birthday" and "Anniversary" fields when adding/editing contacts which the vCard standard doesn't allow.
So: TL;DR: I use the stock GrapheneOS contacts app to add, edit and browse contacts and I use Right Contacts to automatically backup my contacts to correctly formatted vCard v4.0 files but don't use that app for anything else. Contacts are synced via DAVx5 to a Radicale server that supports vCard v4.0 (currently that requires a patch).

- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Contacts/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.6.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 15

### Affected device model

Fairphone Gen 6

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Go in the fossify Phone app
2. Go to the contacts list
3. Select any contact so it will open the Contacts app

### Expected behavior

It should display the contact I selected.

### Actual behavior

It always displays the first contact of my contacts list.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

After some digging in the source code, it seems the contact lookup key is always "0i" when coming out of the Phone app.
When coming in the Contacts app the call to the fossify.commons Helpers always grabs the whole list of contacts from the database, then the processing gets the first one of the query result (which is not the intended contact.

As I get it, the intent URI is `<[...]/lookup/<key>/<id>` but `id` is ignored and the `key` is always `0i`. I would guess the wrong part is in the grabbing of the lookup key when building the intent but the intent does open the Google Contacts app in the expected manner so I'm not really sure.

title: Allowing storage access to USB device and later connecting a different USB device doesn't allow access to that device and doesn't prompt for access either
body:
### Checklist

- [X] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/releases/latest).
- [X] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [X] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/discussions) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/discussions?discussions_q=is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [X] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed.
- [X] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [X] This issue contains only one bug.
- [X] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.0.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 13

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Connect a USB device to your phone via the USB-C port.
2. In the app, navigate to the root folder of the USB device. File manager will prompt you for storage access rights for that directory.
3. Grant the access rights.
4. Disconnect the USB device.
5. Connect a different USB device and navigate to its root folder.
6. File manager will display an empty directory, despite the USB device containing files. This is not an issue with the USB device. Android's default file manager manages to correctly display the files. Also, I was able to reproduce this with the order of USB devices reversed. The one for which the permission was granted continues to work, while the other one is always displayed as empty.

### Expected behavior

Either display the contents of the USB device correctly, or recognize that permissions are missing and request them.

### Actual behavior

The USB device for which the access rights were granted will have its contents displayed, while other devices appear as empty and do not prompt for access permissions.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

- I think the storage access framework recognizes the USB devices as distinct storage locations despite being mounted and accessed through the same path.
- Fossify Gallery 1.1.1 is also affected by this bug.
- Could be related to #9.

### Affected app version

1.1.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 15

### Affected device model

Nothing phone (1)

### How did you install the app?

Built from source

### Steps to reproduce the bug


1. Click on a ZIP file in the _Files_ tab
2. Click the **Decompress** icon in the action menu
3. Press **OK** on the "Select a folder" dialog

### Expected behavior

Folders retain their original timestamps.

### Actual behavior

Folders have newly created timestamps instead of the original ones.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

Pay attention to the following:

- If a file is located in multiple nested folders, timestamps should be updated recursively. The current logic skips nested folders.
- The logic in `DecompressActivity` completely skips empty folders.

Related to:
- https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/pull/178#issuecomment-2896461965
- https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/pull/178#issuecomment-2906914424

I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.** - [X] This issue contains only one bug. - [X] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/File-Manager/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md). ### Affected app version 1.0.1 ### Affected Android/Custom ROM version Android Oreo (8.0) ### Affected device model Xperia XZ ### How did you install the app? F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid ### Steps to reproduce the bug Install the application for the first time on a device and run it. ### Expected behavior It should just request storage permission as it's needed for the application to operate. Nothing triggering this exception should be done if it doesn't have the requested permission. That aside, does it even make sense for a file manager to allow the user to interact with it before providing the needed permission? It doesn't seems like a file manager can have any use if it can't access storage, does it? ### Actual behavior Application triggers an exception in a popup related to the storage permission, even on the first time the application is launched and before the user could do anything to give the application the requested permission. See screenshot below. Note that as I wanted to reproduce the issue for the screenshot, rather than uninstalling the application to reinstall it, I just wiped data and removed storage permission but it didn't act as the first launch curiously: it didn't request the storage permission again. ### Screenshots/Screen recordings

body: ### Checklist - [x] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Gallery/releases/latest). - [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Gallery/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Gallery/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information. - [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:gallery) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:gallery) - to which I could contribute my information. - [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed. - [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.** - [x] This issue contains only one bug. - [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Gallery/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md). ### Affected app version 1.13.1 ### Affected Android/Custom ROM version Android 16 / OneUI8 ### Affected device model Samsung Galaxy S23 / A53 ### How did you install the app? F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid ### Steps to reproduce the bug 1. On WeChat, send a photo using the "Full Image" sharing option 2. On the receiving end, save the photo to gallery (filename format: mmexport[13-digit timestamp].jpg, e.g. mmexport1776520848400.jpg) 3. Open Fossify Gallery 4. Browse the WeChat folder or the main timeline ### Expected behavior When no EXIF DateTimeOriginal is present, the gallery should fall back to parsing the 13-digit Unix timestamp (milliseconds) from the filename and use it for display and sorting. ### Actual behavior When sent as "Full Image", WeChat preserves partial EXIF data but strips DateTimeOriginal. Fossify Gallery fails to fall back to the filename timestamp (mmexport[13-digit ms timestamp].jpg) and shows the photo without date, incorrectly sorted. When sent compressed (non Full Image), EXIF is absent entirely — Fossify appears to handle this case correctly. Additionally, manually removing the partial EXIF via Fossify does not trigger a re-sort based on the filename timestamp. ### Screenshots/Screen recordings I'm giving you an image sample saved as Full Image from WeChat, and a screenshot of the properties of this image (the EXIF details are highlighted) ### Additional information - The filename timestamp is in milliseconds (13 digits), not seconds (10 digits) — this may be the parsing edge case Fossify is missing. - Tested with other gallery apps (Ente Photos + [les autres que t'as testés]) : all correctly parse the date from the mmexport filename and sort the photo accordingly, confirming the issue is specific to Fossify Gallery.

### Affected app version

1.10.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 11

### Affected device model

moto g stylus (2021)

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1.With any app open
2. go to split screen by
2a. swiping up from the bottom and 2b. holding, then
2c. click the appicon at the top of the image of the app, and
2d. Selecting "split screen" 
3. In the bottom half of the screen, go to the launcher by swiping up. This will minimize the existing app at the top of the screen so that it only takes up, effectively, one row of icons.
4. Tap on any app.



### Expected behavior

Under all circumstances, tapping an app on the launcher screen should launch that app, not a different one based on app state or arrangement of icons on the launcher. 

### Actual behavior

The launcher will behave as if you tapped the app below the one you actually tapped, and launch that app instead. 

As a work around, you can tap the icon above the app you actually want to launch, but this is impossible to do with apps on the the top row.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

This bug exists in all versions of the launcher I've tried (so at least for the last six months)

It would be useful if Fossify Launcher would show the microG app.

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Make sure that the color scheme is set to 'System' in the launcher settings.
2. Go to the homescreen.
3. Check the color of the status bar (in my case 'white').
4. Start an App from the homescreen.
5. Exit the app using the back arrow.

Samsung SM-N986U1

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Install app.
2. Open app.
3. Set app as default or it just exits.
4. Create new message to contact.
5. Click photo button.
6. Select a photo (i.e. taken by camera, or a screenshot).
7. Send message.
8. MMS message fails to send.

### Expected behavior

I expect the messages app to send the MMS image.

### Actual behavior

A bogus error message because the app is programmed incorrectly.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

There was another issue where the developer tries to gaslight the user with absolute lies about needing mobile data active to send MMS. Completely wrong.  It is sufficient to have WiFi only.  SMS and MMS just connects to a server on the internet.  For accounting purposes, the mobile provider may count these messages against the mobile data plan, but not necessarily, it depends on the provider and plan.  Some broken and braindamaged providers may create false problems to extort money or instill feau, uncertainty, doubt.  But I've used this phone and provider 6 years and never once had to turn on mobile data for MMS messages, even without WiFi.  If I use the default Samsung Messages app, I can send several photos at once, zero issues with failed messages in 6 years.  Only with Fossify Messages is there a problem, therefore Fossify Messages is programmed incorrectly with respect to SMS/MMS.  Q.E.D.

title: Widget note is not updated if note file.txt is modified by SyncThing - app itself displays all changes OK.
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I can reproduce the bug with the latest version given [here](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Notes/releases/latest).
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Notes/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Notes/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:notes) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:notes) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed.
- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the bug report will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one bug.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Notes/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.6.0 org.fossify.notes

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

LineageOS 20-20240705

### Affected device model

Samsung SM-G965F

### How did you install the app?

GitHub releases

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Install notes-12-foss-release.apk
2. Manually create Note.txt file somewhere in your directory tree, set Note.txt as widget on the screen.
3. Open Note.txt with fossify notes.
4. Add some text via fossify notes user interface to the note (edit file Note.txt).
5. Close fossify notes.
6. Open file Note.txt with editor, change content and save it. (in real life SyncThing modifies the file during synchronisation process).
7. Open Note.txt with fossify notes again. All changes are reflected properly. Close fossify notes.
8. Check note content at the widget - changes are not reflected.
9. Open Note.txt with fossify notes again. Press enter or add some text. Close fossify notes.
10. Check note content at the widget again - changes are reflected.

SUMMARY: Widget note is not updated automatically when note txt file is modified by SyncThing. It gets updated only if the note is edited by main fossify notes application. This prevents widget usability if notes are synchronized between devices.

### Expected behavior

If note.txt file is modified by SyncThing, note widget content shall be updated automatically.

### Actual behavior

If note.txt file is modified by SyncThing, widget note content stays unchanged until fossify notes application opens and modifies the file.

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

- [X] This issue contains only one bug.
- [X] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Notes/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Affected app version

1.1.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

Android 10

### Affected device model

Samsung Galaxy Note 9

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

1. Find a big file without newline to open...

The file I found that triggered the crash is the .json file produced by the "Export Notes" function of SimpleMobileTools Notes. (Yes, I tried _opening_ it as a note rather than _importing_ it into Fossify Notes.) The file was about 430000 bytes, without a single newline. Unfortunately for privacy reasons I cannot provide that file.

If you have [Termux](https://termux.dev/en/), you can generate such a file with a _very_ long line, enough to reproduce a crash or hang:

```sh
for ((i=1; i<=4000; i++)); do printf '%100d' $((i * 100)); done | sed 's/ /-/g' >very-long-line.txt
for ((i=1; i<=4000; i++)); do printf '%99d\n' $((i * 100 - 1)); done | sed 's/ /-/g' >line-wrapped.txt
```

2. In Fossify Notes, bring up the menu (the ellipsis icon in the upper right corner of the screen) → "Open file" function.
3. Select and open the "very-long-line.txt" just generated within Termux.
4. Select "Update the file itself at updating the note". (If you don't do this, you will be very difficult to escape the crash once reproduced.)
5. "New note type: Text Note", then tap "OK".
6. You should experience things slowing down by now.

### Expected behavior

No crash at least. I can tolerate Fossify Notes not showing the whole line or not allowing to edit the file, but there should be no crash.

### Actual behavior

Fossify Notes slows down significantly. After trying to bring up "Settings" or "About" from the menu, the app crashes.

Note that if you opened the "line-wrapped.txt" file instead of "very-long-line.txt", there would be no significant slowdown.

(Since Fossify Notes keeps the opened file when the app restarts, you might experience repeated hangs or crashes. To escape from that, delete the "very-long-line.txt" from within Termux.)

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

### Affected app version

1.2.0

### Affected Android/Custom ROM version

OxygenOS (Android) 14

### Affected device model

Oneplus Pad Go

### How did you install the app?

F-Droid / IzzyOnDroid

### Steps to reproduce the bug

sometimes paint goes to "draw to wrong place" bug mode. This happens typically after first drawing something with pencil and then zooming out with 2 fingers. Also I now noticed that the bug appears sometimes when I turn the device from portrait to landscape or back (auto rotate android UI is enabled).

When bug is present if I touch pixel 300,300 and pull it to pixel 600,600, the actual line on the screen could appear to pixels 600,100 - 900,400.

The length of error vector varies, sometimes it could be 25% of the length of display and sometimes only 2-5%

Getting rid of the bug I have to go to settings - apps - Fossify Paint - force stop.

Device is Oneplus Pad Go (Mediatek G99 soc, 8GB RAM, 2480 x 1720 resolution (1.4 aspect ratio), 11 inch screen).

I did just 5min test with my phone (galaxy S21fe, 2300ish x 1080 res) and in that time I was unable to reproduce it.

Worth noting is that this tablet has unusual aspect ratio and resolution for androids (typical android tablets are 1.6 aspect ratio and phones are over 2). Nevertheless IMO this is best aspect ratio and e.g. iPad aspect ratios are at same ballpark (1.33 - 1.5).

With tablet it is much more common to rotate the device than with phones (IMO) so program should take this into account.

The bug does not appear every time, I am not sure what factors cause it. But if you take a tablet, start drawing, doing lot of 2 finger pinch zooms in and out and drawing in different zooming factors, sometimes panning (moving 2 fingers to any directions), and then rotating the device so that auto-rotate happens you have high change of running into this bug.

I suspect that the bug is on the algorithm which translates touch coordinate to drawn pixel coordinate.

### Expected behavior

pen location remains in touch location

### Actual behavior

pen location is different than touch location

<!-- Failed to upload "fossify-paint-touch-pen-location-bug1-H5-qb3000k.mp4" -->

### Screenshots/Screen recordings

Here is link to screen recording of the bug (skip to 00:28 if you want to see it directly). I tried to attach it but was unable to do it so below is a nextcloud to the recording (h265)

[https://nx76363.your-storageshare.de/s/AY8GA5gkaXkqzc4](https://nx76363.your-storageshare.de/s/AY8GA5gkaXkqzc4)

### Additional information

.

title: Option request: Disable one-finger gestures, keep two-finger gestures enabled.
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing issues** - [open](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Paint/issues) or [closed](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Paint/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I made sure that there are **no existing discussions** - [open](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:open+label:paint) or [closed](https://github.com/orgs/FossifyOrg/discussions?discussions_q=is:closed+label:paint) - to which I could contribute my information.
- [x] I have read the FAQs inside the app (Menu -> About -> FAQs) and my problem isn't listed.
- [x] **I have taken the time to fill in all the required details. I understand that the request will be dismissed otherwise.**
- [x] This issue contains only one feature request.
- [x] I have read and understood the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/FossifyOrg/Paint/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

### Feature description

Currently the user can enable/disable movement gestures. I'd like an extra level of granularity: Disable gestures like double tap to zoom, while keeping gestures like two finger zoom enabled.

### Why do you want this feature?

When painting, one often strikes the canvas multiple times in rapid succession. With gestures enabled, this frequently causes unintended zooming in and out. The only way to prevent this is to completely disable gestures. But gestures like two-finger zoom / move are very useful, and they wouldn't cause the same issue if enabled.

### Additional information

_No response_

title: [BUG] critical upstream
body:
Critical — widget auth bypass :
The root cause is in three places working together. In widget/ui/Note.kt, when a user taps the widget it launches MainActivity with FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK, which wipes the back stack and starts fresh. The noteId extra is passed along, and in NavHost.kt, AppNavHost sees noteId != -1 and routes directly to the Edit screen — skipping the lock screen entirely. The onResume lock check in MainActivity only fires when navigating within the app, not on a fresh cold start from a widget tap. On Android 16 / Pixel Fold this is particularly reproducible because the foldable's home screen configuration triggers a clean task launch path more aggressively.
Separately, getDefaultRoute() in NavHost.kt only checks passcode != null — if you're using fingerprint or pattern only, it falls through to NavRoutes.Home.route. And there's a typo in SettingsModel.kt line 22: defaultRoute == NavRoutes.Home.route uses == (comparison) instead of = (assignment), so when there's no lock set, defaultRoute stays null rather than being set to Home.

High — encryption weaknesses
The vault encryption in EncryptionHelper.kt uses a single SHA-256 hash of the password as the AES key with no salt and no iterations. This is trivially brute-forceable. Modern Android security guidelines require PBKDF2 with HMAC-SHA256 (minimum 10,000 iterations) or Argon2 via the Jetpack Security library. Additionally, the passcode and pattern are stored as plain strings in DataStore preferences — they should be hashed with bcrypt or stored as a salted SHA-256 at minimum, or ideally the key material should live in the Android Keystore.

Medium — missing Android security features
data_extraction_rules.xml and backup_rules.xml are both empty templates, meaning notes and passwords sync to Google Drive/D2D transfer unprotected. The Room database has no at-rest encryption (no SQLCipher). The widget renders note content on the home screen with no auth gate — encrypted vault notes would show their decrypted content if the widget was set before locking. NotesWidgetActivity is exported with no permission protecting it from other apps on-device.

Low — hardening
The release build config uses signingConfig = signingConfigs.getByName("debug") — a release APK should have its own signing key. The debug build has isDebuggable = true which is expected, but worth flagging. No network_security_config.xml is defined.

title: Lawnchair QuickSwitch issues
body:
The following bug is an aggregated bug report of what's internally known:

### All[^1][^2]
- [ ] Lawnchair Recents preference are not tested, and have been heavily affected by Android 16 and 16 QPR1 merge
- [ ] Contextual Search (commonly known as Circle-to-Search) is not responding 
- [ ] Other Android version that are not listed here are status unknown as it's not tested

### Android 16.0[^1]
- [ ] Recent task switcher only work when you are in home screen (and may hang for a little bit when you try to use it while other app is running)
- [ ] Recent task switcher show invisible app process
- [ ] Swipe up to exit the app does not work

[^1]: Tested with Nothing (3a)-series on Android 16.0 [(Nothing OS 4.0), March 2026 security patch](https://github.com/spike0en/nothing_archive/releases/tag/Asteroids_B4.0-251118-1551) with [Magisk v30.7](https://github.com/topjohnwu/Magisk/releases/tag/v30.7)

### Android 15 QPR2[^2]
- [ ] Recent task switcher is non-existent
- [ ] Predictive back is not responding (likely because some of the refactored predictive back code use Baklava API)

[^2]: Tested with Nothing (3a)-series on Android 15 QPR2 [(Evolution-X 10.5 unofficial GSI), April 2025 security patch](https://github.com/mytja/treble_evo/releases/tag/20250421) with [Magisk v30.7](https://github.com/topjohnwu/Magisk/releases/tag/v30.7)
