title: Problems with the new “Manage styles” function
body:
## Vespucci Version
v22.0.0.1

## Download source
Google Play Store

## Device (Manufacturer and Model)
Samsung A33

## Android Version 
16

## Behaviour/Symptoms
First of all, I have to say: HURRAY!!! You've finally improved this (see #2631)! And it looks great... I think it's really fantastic. And it seems to be working, too. At least after some pretty serious problems I encountered.

Preliminary note: as you might remember from other issues (e.g. #2614), I use very extensive custom styling, which is a significant extension of "Colour round nodes." My main styling currently has 137,400 lines, the XML is 11.6 MB, and zipped with icons (since #2614 I use PNG only instead of SVG) it's 8.6 MB – unzipped it's about 25 MB. And I don't know if that could be the underlying cause.

What happened?

After the update from v21.2.4 to v22.0.01, my custom data styling was, of course, gone (in Vespucci, not on the device of course). When I added my first styling (I use two different ones, roughly the same size), I got two error messages in quick succession:

1. “Invalid style file content (…) null”
2. “Invalid style file content (…) At line 68234, column 3: unclosed token”

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c2873603-79f0-43f5-8090-4f24fc4dc589)

I ignored it for the time being because it was already quite late, and I wanted to take a closer look at it the next day.

Next day (2026/03/28): At first, I couldn't find any errors in the XML. The line 68234 was:
`<feature type="way" tags="addr:housenumber" labelKey="addr:housenumber" />`

When I then tried to open Vespucci the first time, several crash report messages appeared in succession (after pressing "OK"), but I couldn't submit them (after pressing "OK" several times, finally "Could not send report" appeared).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a4500aa-e041-44b1-92a4-23b082d6f01e)

I couldn't get rid of the messages. I had to close Vespucci in the app switcher and was then able to open it without any error messages. My added custom styling was in the list. After attempting to update the style, I received another error message, this time: "Invalid style file content (...) At line 136822, column 3: unclosed token." However, there was no error in the XML on this line either.

I don't remember the exact sequence, but I closed Vespucci, restarted it, activated my styling, and exited the settings. Then a strange styling was displayed (should be the built-in minimal styling I think): all lines were shown as solid black lines, and only icons for nodes (= icons of presets) were displayed correctly. See screenshot... I'd been aware of this for some time – up until Vespucci v21.2.4, I had to open the preferences once (without doing anything there) and then exit again for my styling to display correctly (this was only necessary after updating the styling!). This time, that didn't work anymore. And I don't remember exactly when it finally worked correctly. I think I performed another "Update style" and exited the settings.

It looks like that:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b0ffccc9-ab76-4ebe-96d2-75e55bf83c6d)

In any case, everything suddenly worked correctly (and there were no more error messages):

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d2def23-bf3c-46cb-b00c-e8766afe9c83)

Adding my second style followed a similar process, but without "Invalid style file content" error messages and without crashes. Since then, I've only had one system message: "Vespucci is not responding - close app / wait". But since closing and restarting the app, everything has been working without problems (for today …).

And I can now easily switch back and forth between the two styles; it works just like it did before in v21.2.4.

This leaves me somewhat puzzled... I'm glad it finally works, but something doesn't seem quite right. Could it be due to the large style (memory and/or timeout problems or similar) and you're unable to reproduce it? (I could send it to you for testing in a private message, as I suggested in another issue.) Here a screenshot of the debug infos:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/39649a99-3c43-49a8-b888-d68d50d3d764)

What do you suggest?


And if I may ask another question:

I assume nothing has changed regarding the style memory management, right? That is, all styles in the list are always loaded into memory for quick switching. (Which can lead to memory shortages with several very large styles, like mine.) It would be great, of course, if the memory used by inactive styles were released when switching styles, and only the newly activated style was loaded into memory.

What I was also hoping for was the ability to activate multiple styles, similar to presets (as is possible in JOSM). That would open up entirely new possibilities, but apparently that's not possible (yet). Just a comment (or a suggestion) …

title: PropertyEditor in split window mode needs to do something with action modes
body:
If a PropertyEditor fragment in split window mode with an active action mode is overlaid with a new instance of the fragment, the action mode header remains visible (because it is an activity level thing).

We should likely stop the action mode when we are being hidden and have changes and restore, if we are being completely replaced there is no harm in just stopping.

title: Selection State Desynchronization during Redo Operations
body:
## Vespucci Version
Vespucci 22.0-BETA-1 (22.0.0.1)

## Download source
Self-built from GitHub source (osmeditor4android)

## Device (Manufacturer and Model)
Oneplus Nord CE 3 Lite

## Android Version 
Android 15

## Behaviour/Symptoms
During a "Redo" operation, the application correctly restores the geometrical and tag state of an OSM element, but it fails to synchronize the UI selection state. 

If multiple elements were edited in sequence, performing multiple "Redo" actions leaves the selection highlight (yellow rings and "Node selected" text) on a previously redone element rather than focusing on the element currently being redone. In many cases, the selection is lost entirely, leaving the UI in a state that does not match the logical focus of the redo stack.

## Expected Behaviour
The selection state should be treated as part of the checkpoint metadata during a Redo operation. When an action is redone, the application should automatically restore the selection to the element(s) that were the focal point of that specific checkpoint, matching the behavior of the "Undo" operation.

## How to recreate
1. Select **Hospital A** and move it to a new location.
2. Select **Hospital B** and move it to a new location.
3. Tap **Undo** twice. Notice the selection correctly follows the focus back to Hospital A.
4. Tap **Redo** once (Restores Hospital A move). Focus is on A.
5. Tap **Redo** again (Restores Hospital B move).
   * **Actual Result:** Hospital B moves, but the selection highlight stays on Hospital A (or disappears).
   * **Expected Result:** The selection should automatically jump to Hospital B.

## Crash dump submitted (no or yes + date)
No (This is a logic/state desynchronization issue, not a crash).

## Any other potentially relevant information
Technical Analysis: 
The root cause is a primitive return type in `UndoStorage.java`). The `redo()` method returns only the `String` name of the checkpoint, whereas `undo()` returns the full `Checkpoint` object. This prevents `Logic.java` from accessing the `Selection.Ids` stored in the checkpoint during a Redo. 

The fix will require:
1. Updating `UndoStorage.redo()` to return the `Checkpoint` object.
2. Updating `Logic.java` to call the selection-refreshing helper (`postUndo`) during Redo operations.

title: Data styling – casingStyle="" can no longer be used to remove a casing set in a parent block
body:
## Vespucci Version
22.0.0.1

## Download source
Google Play Store

## Device (Manufacturer and Model)
Samsung A33

## Android Version 
16

## Behaviour/Symptoms
I've noticed in my custom data styling, that `casingStyle=""` can no longer be used to remove a casing set in a parent block since Vespucci v22.0.0.1. I have several styling blocks with a main casing style that's modified in sub-blocks or, in some cases, removed entirely. To remove the casing style, I've been using `casingStyle=""`, but this no longer works (the casing of the parent block continues to be displayed).

As a workaround, I defined a casing with a line width of 0 and opacity of 0% (to be sure ...) and assigned it – this works:

`<feature type="no_casing" widthFactor="0" color="00000000" style="STROKE" cap="BUTT" join="MITER" />`

But was this change intentional (and if so, why?) or is it some kind of regression? Using `casingStyle=""` would save me some code (which wouldn't be unreasonable, see, for example, #3176), and it also seems very useful to be able to use it this way.

## Expected Behaviour
It's not a serious problem, but it would be nice if `casingStyle=""` worked like in v21.2.4.

title: Having trouble with Sign in with Google on the OSM login page
body:
## Vespucci Version
<!-- required, see debug information screen (select debug in main menu) -->
21.2.3.0

## Download source
<!-- from where did you obtain the app? google play store, amazon, f-droid, github, ... -->
Play store
## Device (Manufacturer and Model)
<!-- required -->
Oneplus 13s
## Android Version 
<!-- required, please indicate if you are using a custom version -->
16
## Behaviour/Symptoms
<!-- required -->
The app opens a webview with the OSM login page. That page has "Sign in with Google" (and Apple, Facebook, etc.). I click that, and it opens Google's login in the same WebView. It doesn't seem to work for me and shows an error toast

## Expected Behaviour
<!-- required -->
After I sign in with Google and authorize, the app should log me in and let me upload the note.

## How to recreate
<!-- required -->
1) Upload a note (or  something that requires login)
2) App prompts for login
3) WebView opens with OSM login page
4) Click Sign in with Google
Google login opens in the same webview which fails afterwards


## Crash dump submitted (no or yes + date)
<!-- required -->
no


## Any other potentially relevant information
<!-- optional -->
**context**:

From what I can tell, Authorize loads the OSM login page in a WebView. If you use "Sign in with Google," that runs in the same WebView and gets blocked by Google. If you use OSM username/password, OSM redirects to vespucci:/oauth2/ with a code. OAuthWebViewClient.handleLoading catches that and fires an intent so VespucciURLActivity can exchange the code for a token. 

**possible solutions:-**

I'm not sure if this is the right approach, but it might help to open the login in an external browser instead of inside the app. That way "Sign in with Google" could work, and when you're done the app might still be able to pick up the callback. Chrome Custom Tabs seems to be one way to do that on Android. It keeps you in the app but uses a real browser under the hood.

<!-- ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3ee1a866-6061-4c55-8e81-bab00cd047e7)-->

**Some links:**
[Remediation for OAuth via WebView](https://support.google.com/faqs/answer/12284343)
[Chrome Auth Tab](https://developer.chrome.com/blog/android-auth-tab) (Chrome 132+)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cdc0fd38-f876-4db1-9d77-e043a12c40cb

title: Transaction list order is inconsistent for transactions created on the same day
body:
**Describe the bug**
Starting from a fresh database, when two transactions are created on the same day, the transaction list can display them in an unexpected order: a later-created transaction may appear before an earlier-created one. This happens even when both transactions have the same date.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start with a new/empty database.
2. Create an income transaction of +500 for today.
3. Create an expense transaction of -500 for the same date (today).
4. Open the account's transactions list
5. Observe that the -500 transaction may appear before the +500 transaction, even though it was created after.

**Device Information:**
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy
 - OS: Android 16
 - Storage: Phone storage
 - Synchronization: 10 min interval
 - Encrypted: no
 - Security: no
 - App Version: 5.4.13 (1103)

**Additional context**
It would be nice if the app could also support the time-of-day and not only date. Of course, that alone wouldn't solve the sort ambiguity -- because two transactions could still occur on the same time --, but that would certainly help and improve the app's functionality. A stable sort tie-breaker is still needed, and that could be the transaction ID.

body:
**Describe the bug**
When playing a video on Youtube, you can start Picture-in-Picture (PiP) mode by returning to home with the home button on the navigation bar or swiping up on from the navigation bar if gestures are on. With KISS as the default launcher, if Youtube is playing a video, and I return home, the video continues to play; however, PiP never appears.

This behavior is not present in other launchers I've used, which include the Pixel Launcher, OnePlus Launcher, and Nova Launcher.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Ensure KISS Launcher is set to default
2. Ensure PiP mode is enabled in Youtube settings
    a. In the Youtube app, click on your profile on the bottom right
    b. Click on the gear in the top right to open settings
    c. In Video and audio preferences, click on Playback
    d. Toggle Picture-in-Picture on
4. Begin playing a Video
5. Return home by pressing the home button or swiping up (if gestures is on) on the navigation bar
6. Observe, audio continues to play; however, no Picture-in-Picture floating window appears
**Expected behavior**
When returning home while playing a video, a small floating window should appear, which displays the video that was playing, and continues playing that video, displayed above all other apps.

**Screenshots**
I can provide screenshots with more detail if needed, just ask what we need to clarify.

**Additional information**
 - App version: 3.21.3
 - App installed via: (f-droid, Play Store, debug APK): Play Store

**Additional context**
Tested on:
OnePlus 9 Pro
OxygenOS 14.0 LE2127_14.0.0.240 (Android 14)
Security Patch April 5, 2024

Google Pixel 8a
Android 15 Build number BP1A.250305.019
Security Patch March 5, 2025

title: KISS periodicially freezes and becomes unresponsive
body:
**Describe the bug**
Periodically KISS will freeze and become mostly unresponsive. The app list can no longer be scrolled, tapping apps to launch them will only work intermittently, and the app list does not animate on tap like normal.

The search bar still opens the keyboard but is otherwise unresponsive. The KISS button (green circle) is unresponsive.

The KISS options menu (three-dots button) still works and restarting KISS will fix the problem (until the next occurrence!).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Unknown, sorry :( it usually happens when I am unlocking the device or using the app switcher quickly (for example)

**Expected behavior**
All elements of KISS should function as normal!

**Screenshots**
Looks very similar to the screenshots in https://github.com/Neamar/KISS/issues/2308 - let me know if a video would be helpful.

**Additional information**
 - App version: 3.23.0
 - App installed via: F-Droid
 - Device: Samsung Galaxy XCover6 Pro (SM-G736U1)
 - Android version: 15 (OneUI 7.0)

**Additional context**
 - This started happening a while ago (several months) and I've just been dealing with it lol. Probably since v3.22.x but I'm not sure! Later I will try downgrading to see if I can determine a known-good version.

title: Regression In 3.25.2 : Favorite Bar Uses Default Icons Instead of Assigned Icon
body: **Describe the bug** Pinned favorites sometimes revert to their default icon rather than a custom assigned one. They start as their assigned icons, but at some point (unknown trigger), will revert to their default icon. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Assign a custom icon to an item in the pinned favorites 2. Restart KISS 3. Observe custom icons properly loaded on pinned favorites 4. Wait (?) 5. Observe default APK icons on the pinned favorites. 6. Restart KISS 7. Observe correct icons again **Expected behavior** The custom assigned icon should continue to be shown. **Screenshots** Please add screenshots to help explain your problem. You can upload images directly to Github, please do not use an external website to host your images. Correct Icons after a restart: Icons reverted to their defaults: **Additional information** - App version: 3.25.2 - App installed via: f-droid **Additional context** I am using a custom icon pack and most of the icons seem to work, but for my pinned favorites, I have some custom icons I want separate from the pack (ie, use the firefox icon for waterfox). I have no idea what actually triggers the icons to revert, I've just noticed twice today that my icons have been wrong, and resolved it by restarting KISS (which confirmed that the icons were still properly assigned, just not being used).

title: BUG with Icon Pack1
body: Hi, when I use an ICON PACK with Kiss Launcher and change an icon to a custom icon and then clear the KISS LAUNCHER cache, the custom icon returns to the default icon pack. For me it's a BUG and needs to be fixed. I hope it will be fixed soon with an update or that someone will show me the solution for this BUG. I hope to receive a response soon. Thank you

title: Recent apps gesture opens wrong view when an app is open
body: Describe the bug: On my Samsung Galaxy S9 Plus running Android 10, the recent apps gesture only works normally on the home screen. When I have any app open, or even when I am in system settings, the gesture does not show the normal recent apps overview. Instead, KISS opens a strange different view with icons, and I cannot switch between apps. The issue appeared suddenly. It was not happening before. Restarting the phone fixed it for now. To Reproduce: Go to the home screen. Open any app or system settings. Use the recent apps gesture by swiping up from the bottom center. See the wrong KISS view instead of the normal app switcher. Expected behavior: The normal recent apps screen should open so I can switch between open apps. Screenshots: Attached screenshot shows the problem. Additional information App version: 3.2.52 App installed via: F-Droid Additional context: This problem only happened when an app or system settings were open. On the home screen, the gesture worked normally.

title: The list of apps is inaccessible if there are any apps in the history
body:
**Describe the bug**
The list of apps is inaccessible if there are any apps on the history. The swipe to the left gesture is only available if history is empty.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install and run KISS Launcher.
2. Swipe to left for applications list. 
3. Run any application to add it to the history. 
4. Try swipe left again on the main screen.

**Expected behavior**
Expected to be able to use the applications list when the history is not empty.

**Additional information**
 - App version: 3.21.3
 - App installed via: f-droid

**Additional context**
The bug was in the version 3.21.1 too. My Android version is 14 (Motorola Edge 40 Neo).

title: Backup restore doesn't restore any widgets (A10 -> A13)
body:
## Description
When restoring from a backup (made on A10) the widgets are not restored at all on A13 (and A12).


## Expected Behavior
Widgets should be restored as well.


## Actual Behavior
No widgets present at all after a restore. They can still be added manually as usual.

I would expect _some_ of the widgets to fail on crDroid because some of the packages have different names but at least the 3rd-party widgets from identical packages that have already been installed should have been restored.


## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a backup (on A10)
2. Restore the backup on A12 or A13


## Device and OS
* Device: starlte -> lemonadep
* Android version: 10 (starlte) -> 12 and 13 (lemonadep)
* Launcher version: v0.9.3 (build 934) on both
* Rom: /e/ OS 1.4 (starlte) -> /e/ OS 1.5 (A12 and A13 versions), crDroid v9.0 (A13)


## Logcat
https://gist.github.com/jficz/c214189c5a512eb87006f8ca230f1887

title: Empty homepage keeps getting deleted
body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
<!--- Check if your issue or something similar has been reported before (if yes upvote/comment there) -->

## Description
<!--- Provide a more detailed introduction to the issue itself, and why you consider it to be a bug -->
Even though the "Allow empty pages" option is enabled in settings, the empty page I use as a homepage gets deleted after a while. 

## Expected Behavior
<!--- Tell us what should happen -->
The empty page should remain the default homepage.


## Actual Behavior
<!--- Tell us what happens instead -->
I am trying to use an empty page as the default homepage and a page with a single widget as the second page. After a while, the empty page disappears and the single-widget-page becomes the default and only homepage. The deletion seems to be happening at random (I haven't noticed anything specific that triggers it).

I also have the "Hide the status bar" option enabled.



## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a link to a live example (screenshot/recording etc..), or a set of steps to reproduce the issue -->

1.Enable "Allow empty pages" option.
2.Delete everything from the homepage and create a second page (with a single widget).
3.Wait for a while (do random actions with your phone).
4.Observe the empty page getting deleted.


## Device and OS

title: Cannot change tab color
body:
The app won't change a tab's color when using the tab long-press menu or via the launcher's settings

## Expected Behavior
When changing a tab's color, the new color should be immediately applied

## Actual Behavior
Long-pressing a tab and pressing "tab color" returns me to the home screen

When trying to change a tab's color via launcher settings (drawer > categorize > select a tab > tab color), the new color will not be applied, and the launcher will revert to the old color

## Steps to Reproduce
Long-press tab in drawer, press "Tab color"
Alternatively, change color in launcher settings

## Device and OS
* Device: Pixel 6 Pro
* Android version: 13
* Launcher version: 0.9.3
* Rom: Calyx OS 4.6.0

title: "Turn off work apps" displayed on Personal tab
body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
<!--- Check if your issue or something similar has been reported before (if yes upvote/comment there) -->

## Description
I do not think (hope) that this is a feature, but every now and then, and always when the Neo Launcher is restarted, it displays the option to disable work apps when opening the drawer. I think it is bug because if you swipe to Work tab and the back to Personal it is hidden (for a while). Also, other launchers I have tried only display the button on the Work tab as well.

Also, the button background is always greenish, it does not follow the accent color.

## Expected Behavior
The button to turn off work apps should appear only when the Work tab with related apps is displayed. Ideally, the button color background should follow the accent color as well IMO.


## Actual Behavior
The button to turn off work apps is always displayed when the launcher is restarted. It also has always a green background, not respecting the accent color chosen in settings.


## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a link to a live example (screenshot/recording etc..), or a set of steps to reproduce the issue -->

1. Install Neo-Launcher
2. Activate Work profile (use Island, for instance)
3. Restart Neo-Launcher
4. Open apps drawer

title: Updating an app causes the app icon to reset to the system's default one
body:
## Description
When I update any app, the app logo/icon in the launcher changes to the [default Android one](https://www.startpage.com/av/proxy-image?piurl=https%3A%2F%2Fencrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com%2Fimages%3Fq%3Dtbn%3AANd9GcQfiKDIkESK9Wq7sC-SikNdfXt78HPOut7Ha1k0E3uTzD_8wLxt%26s&sp=1662142870Tf14fed2d207a54ac09ad37f51eb3fb6c2732c30a38cbaa3a2aa827104ef7d6ac).
This issue does not occur in the standard launcher (here: GrapheneOS launcher3).

## Expected Behavior
The app icon should update to the one packagacked in the new version (in most cases just stay the same).

## Actual Behavior
The app icon distinguishes and gets replaced by the [app logo that shows when an app has no icon set](https://www.startpage.com/av/proxy-image?piurl=https%3A%2F%2Fencrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com%2Fimages%3Fq%3Dtbn%3AANd9GcQfiKDIkESK9Wq7sC-SikNdfXt78HPOut7Ha1k0E3uTzD_8wLxt%26s&sp=1662142870Tf14fed2d207a54ac09ad37f51eb3fb6c2732c30a38cbaa3a2aa827104ef7d6ac).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Download & install Neo-Launcher 0.9.2

2. Set Neo-Launcher as the default start app

3. Update an installed app
     3a) Download & install an old version of e.g. [Molly](https://github.com/mollyim/mollyim-android/releases/download/v5.44.3-1/Molly-v5.44.3-1-FOSS.apk)
     3b) Update the installed app to a newer version (in case of Molly for example [version 5.44.5-1](https://github.com/mollyim/mollyim-android/releases/download/v5.44.5-1/Molly-v5.44.5-1-FOSS.apk
))

## Device and OS
* Device: Google Pixel 6a
* Android version: 13
* Launcher version: 0.9.2
* Rom: GrapheneOS 20220830

title: ALL PLAYBACK ERRORS Megathread / Source error 2004 / Error 403 / Infinite loading
body:
# Megathread for playback errors (that do not crash the app)

> [!WARNING]
> Do not create issues for playback errors, they will be closed without explanation. Instead, reply to this issue with logs. **ONLY** if the app crashes, then you may create a new issue



## Known errors
- Any video that requires you to be logged in does not work
    - Logged in playback is broken entirely. Pull requests to fix the issue are welcome. 
- Source error 2004: Sign in to confirm you're not a bot
    - This is due to YT API issues throttling. Try again later or log in to YouTube Music through the app
- Songs stuck at 0:00, or not playing 

## Won't fix errors
- Network related issues (java.net.SocketTimeoutException: failed to connect)
- Source error (2004): Response code: 403
<!--

- ktor error 401: "Request is missing required authentication credential. Expected OAuth 2 access token, login cookie or other valid authentication credential"
    - Your login needs to be refreshed. Please log out (from both the web portal, and the log out button in the app) and then  logging back in

- Playback Error 403
- Source error 2004
- Playback stops after 30 seconds
- Sign in to confirm you're not a bot
- This video is private
- This video is inappropriate for some users
-->

<!-- ## More information -->


## Taking logs
Logcat is preferred for reporting issues, however for basic logging, copy the error text that shows up in the player screen.

### OuterTune version

0.9.2.1 or higher

### Device & Android version

iPhone 16s Pro Plus Max Air SE 5G

title: [BUG]: Experiment pull issues when Experimental UI is selected
body:
### Description

We are experiencing difficulties pulling certain trials/experiments from DeltaBreed via BrAPI into Field Book when the 'New BrAPI import UI' is selected (if we uncheck the box, the imports work as expected).

In a few cases, we were able to isolate the possible reasons for the issues we encountered and create work-arounds for successful import into Field Book (see below).  However, in one instance, we failed to identify a cause. 

1. Issues with exp unit ID in DeltaBreed. When this column contains longer identifiers with alpha, numeric and special characters, we are unable to pull the experiment into Field Book. The 'fetching observation units" message and a spinning wheel are shown for long periods of time and the field will not get pulled in.
2. Issues with treatment factors. We are unable to pull in experiments with treatment factors. When that specific field is selected, the screen resets as though it was not selected. Please refer to [https://github.com/PhenoApps/Field-Book/issues/1218] for more information on this issue.

Please feel free to reach out if you would like file examples or to discuss the issue further.

_No response_

title: [BUG]: Error about non-unique IDS when loading fields via BrAPI to a device that already has fields loaded by CSV
body:
### Description

Greetings,

We have given trainings to several users about how to use BrAPI to pull data from the EBS to their devices. They already have fields (occurrences) that have been loaded by csv to their devices.
When we try to import via BrAPI we get an error saying that there is a conflict in the unique ID. But, we have created the experiments just for the training and we know that they have new plot_id values which should be not be on their device.

We are not sure if somehow the trials that they uploaded without plot_id values were accidentally assigned overlapping numbers with the IDs that we are loading from the EBS, but that seems unlikely as we have seen the same problem for several users.

Can you please help us to identify how to overcome this error so that users can import new fields via BrAPI onto devices that already have fields with data?

I apologize in advance if we are committing some kind of error in our import process.

We look forward to your feedback.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

6.2.2

title: Requesting permissions is not done everywhere
body:
While making PR #956 i noticed not all fragments requested permission when the permission was not already given. After looking a bit into it, the analysis showed me that the following classes are NOT asking for storage/camera permissions when they need:
- HistoryFragment
- InvertPdfFragment
- MergeFilesFragment
- PdfToImageFragment
- QrBarcodeScanFragment (camera gets asked, but not storage for saving)
- RemoveDuplicatePagesFragment
- RemovePagesFragment
- SplitFilesFragment

### Steps to reproduce

1. Remove storage/camera permissions for the app
  a. hold the icon long
  b. click the small i in the top right corner
  c. click on "Permissions" (or how its named)
  d. turn off storage/camera
2. Open the app
3. click on any of the listed fragments
4. try to do something that requires the permission, for example try to open a PDF in the SplitFilesFragment

### Expected behaviour
A permission request should show up asking for storage permission.

### Actual behaviour
The snackbar shows up with the R.string.encrypted_pdf message.

### Would you like to work on the issue?
I would work on the issue if no one else wants to do it and i improved the code climate of ImagesToPdfFragment to something better than F.

PS: the behaviour of #881 can not only be caused by the Parent folder not existing but also by this

title: [Google Calendar sync] Task without a due time ends the day before its beginning
body:
If a task without a due time (but only with a due date) is created and synced as an entry to Google Calendar, its ending is set on the day before its beginning. This also happens if the option "calendar entry starts at due time" is deactivated.

If the calendar entry is altered manually, this has to be corrected manually before saving the entry. It would be helpful if this could be corrected or if a workaround could be shown. Thanks.

title: All day task not showing in calendar
body:

You can open the event from Tasks but when you click edit you see that the end time is invalid as it is less than the start time.

![Screenshot_20210617-011413.png](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13605448/122311246-abdd6280-cf09-11eb-82cf-8f7e32ab21af.png)

Create a recurring Google Task in Tasks.org then mark it completed from Google Calendar

The task gets completed in Tasks.org and does not repeat. Two completed tasks appear in Google Calendar

title: Repeating task miscalculates new due date
body:
In version 11.10.2 my repeating tasks are miscalculating the new due date.

Task Configuration:

    No start date
    Due date Today
    Repeats Daily
    Repeats from due date

When I complete the task it reschedules it for today. (that's right, it keeps coming back and I can't get rid of it)

My current timezone is UTC+13. We just started DST this morning, yesterday we were in UTC+12, and the bug may have started yesterday or before, when tasks were to be rescheduled over the DST boundary.

This is the same behaviour I reported back in February with issue https://github.com/tasks/tasks/issues/1374

Thanks for your effort on this software.

In Notification Center I would see 3 tasks: "A", "B", "C and D"

10 (with Huawei's EMUI 10.0)

title: Backup fails with Nextcloud content provider folder
body:
Hi there,
To be honest, I'm not sure if this is an issue with Tasks or with the [Nextcloud Android client](https://github.com/nextcloud/android/).


## Description:
The nextclouod client provides a content provider, so remote Nextcloud folders can be accessed directly by Android apps. When I choose my devices local Download folder, choosing "Backup now" in tasks works fine, notifying me of the updated backup with a correct date and time. When I choose a nextcloud folder and grant Tasks access to it though, Tasks appears to be backing up (I see the progress bar), but the "Last backed up" date doesn't change and the backup never appears in my Nextcloud.

## Specs:
Server: Nextcloud 20.0.1 with MySQL and PHP 7.4 using Yunohost
Nextcloud client: 3.14.1 from FDroid
Phone: Samsung A51 (SM-A515W), Android 10, Security patch Nov 1, 2020
Tasks: 11.1.1 from FDroid


## Steps to reproduce:
1. Make sure Nextcloud client is installed
2. In Tasks, go to settings > backups > backup folder
3. Tap the hamburger menu and choose the "Nextcloud" account from the content providers on the left.
4. Choose the desired folder to save the backup in, click "Allow access to 'TASKS'" and then allow.
5. Click "Backup now".

## Expected result:
The current date and time appears next to where it says "Last backup:" and a backup file for tasks appears in the selected Nextcloud folder.

## Actual:
Tasks shows a progress bar. When it completes, it closes, going back to the Backups settings folder, showing "Last backup: never". No Tasks backup file appears in the selected Nextcloud folder.

### Affected version

0.28.6 and all release versions

### Description

On the history page, when the list of entries is long, and the user scrolls up to see more entries, the control buttons at the top (Sort button + action buttons: Background | Play All | Popup) scroll together with the list and disappear from view.

This can make it inconvenient for users to quickly sort or perform actions, as they have to scroll back to the top to access the buttons.

### Suggestion:
Keep these buttons sticky at the top so they remain visible while scrolling through long history lists.

### Why this improves User Experience:

1. Reduces the number of times users have to scroll back and forth
2. Makes sorting and actions more efficient and immediate

Then the 0.28.6 came out, I tried to install that one, and the exact same thing happened. I read the FAQ (the first one here: https://newpipe.net/FAQ/install/), where it said this could happen when trying to use a build from GitHub to update a built from F-Droid (I had also noticed the latest version on F-Droid was 0.28.4 the one I had before this issue started), so I followed the steps indicated by the FAQ and tried to uninstall NewPipe and reinstall it. And the exact same thing happened: it just doesn't get installed. I tried with multiple versions: not only 0.28.5 and 0.28.6, but also 0.28.4 (the last one that had been installed without problems) and even a few of the versions numbered 0.28.x. But I got the exact same error every single time. ### Screenshots/Screen recordings ### Logs _No response_ ### Affected Android/Custom ROM version Android 10 ### Affected device model Motorola moto g(7) power ### Additional information Maybe this is a problem with my phone, but I have no way of identifying what could be going on. title: Text of axis labels is hard to read
body:
**Describe the bug**
When I open the channel graph or time graph the axis labels are not clearly readable when using system theme and system theme is dark

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Make sure device theme is set to dark mode
2. Go to settings and set theme to system
3. Go to channel graph -> axis label text color is black
4. Go to settings and change theme to dark
5. Go to channel graph -> axis label text color is white

**Expected behavior**
White labels when in system theme

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Note**
I think problem comes from [GraphViewBuilder.kt](https://github.com/VREMSoftwareDevelopment/WiFiAnalyzer/blob/f187ec753fbd6d0246242ba8be0271e17c200381/app/src/main/kotlin/com/vrem/wifianalyzer/wifi/graphutils/GraphViewBuilder.kt#L47) and [GraphViewWrapper.kt](https://github.com/VREMSoftwareDevelopment/WiFiAnalyzer/blob/f187ec753fbd6d0246242ba8be0271e17c200381/app/src/main/kotlin/com/vrem/wifianalyzer/wifi/graphutils/GraphViewWrapper.kt#L116). Both check `if (ThemeStyle.DARK == themeStyle) Color.WHITE else Color.BLACK`. This probably makes system go to `else`.

Android 14 on Samsung A52s
WiFi Analyzer v3.1.4

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

**Provide Device/Android OS information**

In opening the app the access points are displayed just fine.

title: Missing WPA3 filter and security icon.
body:
**Describe the bug**
WPA and WPA2 are shown appropriately and with RSN-PSK-CCMP appended after the WPA2 listing, but WPA3 is missing itself and only shows RSN-AEA-CCMP. Wireless settings in Android settings for the network properly show the security standard as "WPA3-Personal", so it's working correctly there and I'm sending this bug report while connected to it. I have all other wifi security standards disabled. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. View a network with WPA3-Personal enabled with CCMP-128.
2. Notice that WPA3 is not shown even when connected. 

**Expected behavior**

title: [BUG] Can't use a p12 client certificate without a password
body:
### Steps To Reproduce
1. Install an HTTP shortcuts script like this:

```
{
  "categories": [
    {
      "id": "421fe30e-6ecd-4ba8-a090-8c4074dda643",
      "name": "Shortcuts",
      "shortcuts": [
        {
          "clientCert": "alias:26d41bc07e4cc3ee08258e8311bf3961c199ceb6",
          "iconName": "flat_color_brightness_3",
          "id": "680b09e9-4301-4117-bb7a-31f9bc1bedd2",
          "method": "PUT",
          "name": "nbsp open",
          "requireConfirmation": true,
          "responseHandling": {
            "successMessage": "Success",
            "successOutput": "message",
            "uiType": "toast"
          },
          "url": "https://padlock.nobreakspace.org/api/lock/261175/open"
        },
        {
          "clientCert": "alias:26d41bc07e4cc3ee08258e8311bf3961c199ceb6",
          "iconName": "flat_color_lightbulb",
          "id": "4004b454-1130-42fe-b819-c53bee4ebc8c",
          "method": "PUT",
          "name": "nbsp close",
          "requireConfirmation": true,
          "responseHandling": {
            "successMessage": "Success",
            "successOutput": "message",
            "uiType": "toast"
          },
          "url": "https://padlock.nobreakspace.org/api/lock/261175/close"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "version": 53
}
```
2. Go to Authentication -> Client Certificate Authentication
3. Select a passwordless .p12 certificate file
4. HTTP-Shortcuts will ask for and require a password, an empty password cannot be set -> any execution will fail

If the client certificate has a password set and the same is password is set in the HTTP-Shortcuts password dialog then the script works as expected. Only a client certificate without a password does not seem to work.

### Expected behavior

HTTP-Shortcuts should either allow setting an empty password. Or (if possible?) detect that the provided .p12 file has no password and skip the password dialog then.

### Actual behavior
An empty password cannot be set, using a .p12 certificate without a password will fail.

### Screenshots
N/A

### Context
Please complete the following information:
- Android OS Version: 15
- LineageOS Version: 22.2, 9th Sept. 2025
- App Version: 3.33.0
- Fairphone 5

title: Static global variable widget and Popup do not display "&" character correctly
body: Hello I'd like to thank for the great app first! I'm not sure if this is an issue of the app, just a character encoding problem on my local side or if my source is delivering something weird. Running [moodeaudio ](https://moodeaudio.org/)on a RPi3 as a headless music player. HTTP shortcuts is used to control (play/pause/next etc) the unit and to display some information about the song being played now. There is a GET command executed, a JSON returned from the Pi and variables are set, nothing too fancy. Artist, album, radio station etc can now be shown either as toast (preferred method), popup or whatever. Now I experience some issues with displaying the "&" character, but only in certain circumstances. I don't know, but it might be some escape character issue or something similar. I don't know if any other characters are affected. The following track is played: Artist: Dirkschneider & The Old Gang Track: Every Heart Is Burning Toast shows correctly `Dirkschneider & The Old Gang` Global variable widget shows `Dirkschneider & The Old Gang` Popup shows `Dirkschneider & The Old Gang` See screenshots for more details. Response character encoding is set to automatic, changing to UTF-8 does not help Am I missing something or are there some settings I need to adjust? Happy to provide any additional informations. System is as follows: - Android OS Version: 15, security updates 01. Sept - App Version: 3.33.0, installed from F-Droid - Device language is german, localisation is Swiss. changing to english does not help - Device ID if possible: Device is a Nothing Phone (2a) Thanks Michael

title: [BUG] Global variable value with URL uses an old value when calling the shortcut from Tasker
body: ### Steps To Reproduce 1. Click overflow menu > *Global variables* 2. Create a *Static variable* called `HA_ADDR` 3. Assign an URL as value 4. Do *not* check the *URL encode* checbox 5. Create a new shortcut with *Basic Request Settings* set to *POST* to something that uses the global variable, e.g. `{HA_ADDR}/api/test` ### Expected behavior When changing the `HA_ADDR` value the shortcuts based on that global variable should change the URL, but this does not seem the case everytime. ### Actual behavior I'm not sure, but it seems that the shortcut picks up an old value (in my case) that the global variable had before. ### Screenshots In the screenshot below the HTTP Shortcut failed (on the left) due to a non-existent domain. If I edit the shortcut, you can see the request is referring to an URL with a global variable which, on the third image, doesn't have the value the shortcut used! Also, in the event history, the URL is still using a wrong domain (variable value). I tried doing the same with the *URL Encode* checkbox checked, but that gives an invalid URL (and still uses a wrong value as before). The wrong value is the domain that I used before, which sometimes is still working while other times it is not and this is why I didn't notice this issue previously... because the old domain was still working 😃 ### Context Please complete the following information: - Android OS Version: `13` - App Version: `3.32.0` - Device ID: `9AXYHW135V`

title: Birthday on February 29,2017 is not displayed
body:
#### Short Description

_App currently in Play Store crashes on Marshmallow, custom rom_

##### Steps to reproduce
 1. Open app from home screen's icon
FYI: There's a contact that has his birthday on 29th of february.

##### Resulted in
App crashes.

> _More details in case that could matter: My XPeria Z was acting fishy and especially gave a lot of bluetooth problems. That much that my bluetooth headset stopped working. Before buying another phone, I decided to unlock the bootloader and flash another rom. I chose StryFlex - an AOSP Marshmallow based ROM, based on the original Sony rom for the XPeria ZX. The phone is working smoothly now and I have no more issues._
>
> _Except for Memento_ 😉

I checked out the most recent code, build and installed that one to see whether I could solve the problem myself. But that build works smoothly, **except for the fact that the contact isn't displayed**

FYI: I am using dummy `crashlyticsKey`, `androidVendingKey` and `mixpanelKey` here and I even commented out `apply plugin: 'com.google.gms.google-services'` in mobile/build.gradle to work around the missing `google-services.json`. So maybe the error is somewhere inthere?

So I sent the crash report anyway. Maybe you can find the cause... Since it's obfuscated, I can't read the stacktrace :-)

title: User image is not uploading when you clicked image from device camera and clicked image quality is also very poor.
body:
#### Expected behavior Memento Calendar is a modern namedays app for Android. In this app you can see upcoming events like birthdays etc. of your contacts or you can also add a new event for your contact from the app in 'Add a new event section'. While adding a new event for your contacts you can also set profile pictures to your contacts from your gallery or from your camera. When you select camera to add a profile picture, your device camera will open and you need to click a new image. After clicking image the image will upload and show in contact image area (at place of blue area). ![WhatsApp Image 2018-02-12 at 17.06.42.jpeg](https://res.cloudinary.com/hpiynhbhq/image/upload/v1518436973/rs7dcgqw2mc46tsnlynh.jpg) #### Actual behavior Today i installed Memento Namedays android app and after installing i came to main screen of where i can see list of my added events or i can add a new event on clicking the "+" button. I clicked on the "+" button to add a new event. After clicking on "+", a new page opened where i can create a new event with user image, contact name and event dates. I clicked on user image(the blue area with white user icon) to add contact image, select camera, click a new image and select ok. I came back to add event screen but the image i clicked from my camera is not uploaded at place of user image placeholder. ![WhatsApp Image 2018-02-12 at 17.06.42.jpeg](https://res.cloudinary.com/hpiynhbhq/image/upload/v1518435826/ctfpuh168d3gwu7meptt.jpg) #### How to reproduce To reproduce the bug follow the following steps - - Open app and click on "+" button to add a new event. - Now click on camera icon to upload a picture and select camera to click a picture. - Click image from device camera and click ok. Now you see that your clicked image is not set as contact profile image. 

* Device:  Samsung J7 Prime
* Operating system: Android 7.0 Nougatt
* App Version: v11.2
#### Recording Of The Bug https://youtu.be/VNzcpiORv1E
Posted on Utopian.io - Rewarding Open Source Contributors

The date, month, year should be invalidated when the `Include Year?` checkbox is checked, as we might end up with false dates (i.e unchecking the year should always include February 29, but February 29, 2018 doesn't make sense).

title: Default backup password can be less than len(6)
body:
#### General information

* **App version:** 0.9.0.1-play
* **Android Version:** 9

#### Expected result
**What is expected?** 
password length requirement is enforced everywhere

**What does happen instead?** 
minimum length is not enforced in Settings -> Backup Password

#### Steps to reproduce
 
 * Go to settings -> Backup Password and set a short password (len<6)
 
 This is the continuation of my comment on #168. I assume this is how I got myself into [that situation](https://github.com/andOTP/andOTP/issues/168#issuecomment-997010649). I'm still thinking that the cheap hack of removing the length limit on the *decryption* secret entry is a good way to prevent more user frustration. We don't know how many backups with short secrets are out there.

title: Backup/Restore function fails if screen turns off/ goes home
body:
#### General information

* **App version:** master, 4ea89105eb79e648437e9d75104bffdb90139a96
* **App source:** GitHub, master
* **Android Version:** Android 10 (Samsung S10)
* **Custom ROM:** N/A

#### Steps to reproduce
1. Make sure one or both of the _Re-lock on screen off_ or _Re-lock when going into the background_ settings are checked.
1. Open _Backup_ from menu.
2. Choose to Backup/Restore (either plain-text or encrypted), opening file chooser Activity.
3. Turn screen off/ wait for screen to turn off/ go home (depending on setting from 1).
4. Turn screen back on and choose file/save backup location.

#### Expected result
**What is expected?** 
I'd expect the backup/restore actions to continue once the unlocking process is complete.

**What does happen instead?**
The app unlocks and doesn't continue the backup/restore process. For backup, a password isn't prompted for (and therefore an empty file is left in the location you set to save), and for restore it doesn't restore.

#### Logcat
N/A

title: KeyStore error on MIUI 12.5.2.0
body:
#### General information

* **App version:** 0.9.0.1-play
* **App source:** Google Play
* **Android Version:** 11 // MIUI 12.5.2.0.RGGEUXM (Stable Beta)
* **Custom ROM:** no
* **Device:** Xiaomi Redmi Note 8 Pro

#### Expected result
I open the app and unlock it with my fingerprint or with a pin / passwort
Then I can use the OTP keys

**What does happen instead?**
After an update to the MIUI Stable Beta 12.5.2.0, it shows me a message, that there is a KeyStore error and it can't load the encryption key from the KeyStore.
It also tells me, that I can go to the settings and change the database encryption.
When I try this, the app goes back to the start message.
I also tried to make a backup, but when i try it, the screen flashes white and the app crashes completely.

Similiar to #847, but I didn't transfered andOTP to a new device,
I also couldn't make a backup, so I haven't tried to wipe the data...

#### Logcat

https://gist.github.com/MagicLike/ecf9cc51bd2e59d92340f89f8db8eafe

#### Steps to reproduce
 
 * Update to the MIUI version 12.5.2.0
 * Open andOTP
 * Unlock it with the fingerprint
 * Try to change the encryption
 * Change the authenticate method, because the app says it's needed to change the encryption
 * Try again to change the encryption, this time it reminds to make an backup
 * Try to make an backup (app crashes)
 * Try to change the encryption without a backup
 * App goes back to the start message

title: Allow saving automatic backups to Google Drive
body:
* **App version:** 0.9.0.1-play
* **Android Version:** 10 on Fairphone 3
* **Custom ROM:** no

When selecting the *Default Backup Location* in the settings, I am only able to access the local storage, and not select Google Drive as a backup location. I can select Drive when making a manual backup, but not for automatic backups (e.g. triggered by creating new entries).

From my understanding, this must be a bug, because saving backups to local storage is pointless, is it not? Backups are supposed to protect against, e.g., device loss, but local storage is lost with the device.

title: BUG: Video player fails to detect other videos in folders containing .nomedia
body:
**Describe the bug**
When a folder contains multiple videos, selecting one normally shows “previous” and “next” buttons beside the play/pause controls.

However, if the same folder includes a .nomedia file, the player no longer detects the other videos, so the previous/next buttons are not shown or function.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
- Create a folder and add multiple video files.
- Add a .nomedia file (or folder) inside it.
- Open any video from the My Files app using the video player.

The player can't detect other videos in the folder and display the previous/next buttons when .nomedia file is present.

**Screenshots**
![Screenshot_20260417_073037_Next Player(1).jpg](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4c8da87e-812d-4f7a-8c5a-037ed430301a)

**Device info:**
 - Device: Galaxy S22 Plus (Snapdargon 8 gen 1)
 - Android version: 16
 - App version: 0.16.3

**Additional context**
.A .nomedia file is used to prevent media files in a folder from being indexed or shown in gallery and other apps. This is useful, for example, when transferring large folders with many images or videos that you don’t want appearing in gallery apps.

Despite this, some players (e.g., MX Player) can still recognize and navigate between videos in such folders, even when a .nomedia file is present.

I’m requesting an option that allows the video player to detect and navigate between other video files in the same folder, even if a .nomedia file is present.

title: Shortcuts/share to ankidroid should not crash the app when the file permissions are missing
body:
How to reproduce:
* install ankidroid from a source that is not the playstore. If you build ankidroid yourself, you can use the full variant.
* open ankidroid, give permission to access file
* open the settings, go to apps > Special  app access> all files access> ankidroid> toggle off "allow access to manage all files" (Or, you can wait a few months, android remove permissions to apps that are not used)
* Then, either:
  * long tap ankidroid, and open the shortcut for study, add or card browser
  * select some text, and share it with ankidroid, with note editor or instant note editor ( ping @criticalAY )
  * select an image, and share it to ankidroid > Add Image

Actual result:
* You'll see a snackbar "Missinq required storage permission. Please grant storage permission then r..."

Expected result:
* same as when you open ankidroid directly, open a downloaded deck, or when you share an image to AnkiDroid > Image Occlusion,  that is, opens a page that state 
> Ankidroid needs some permission to work. 


This is a very low priority bug, because it can only occurs to people who:
* don't use the play store version,
* have spent months without using ankidroid, have manually removed a permission, or try to share to ankidroid before opening ankidroid a first time normally
* have created shortcuts or decides to share images or text to ankidroid.

This probably happened to some of our users, but I expect this to be quite rare

title: Potential ANR / main-thread blocking risk: Geocoder.getFromLocation() in UploadDeviceInfoFragment.onViewCreated()
body:
Hi GPSTest Team,

I’m a PhD student researching Android performance issues. My research group recently ran a static analysis scan for thread-affinity and main-thread blocking bugs, and our prototype flagged a potential issue in GPSTest.

## Checked target

+ App: GPSTest (`com.android.gpstest.osmdroid`)
+ Repository: `https://github.com/barbeau/gpstest`
+ Source path checked: `GPSTest/src/main/java/com/android/gpstest/ui/share/UploadDeviceInfoFragment.kt`
+ APK-level caller: `com.android.gpstest.ui.share.UploadDeviceInfoFragment.onViewCreated(...)`
+ Detected API: `android.location.Geocoder#getFromLocation(double, double, int)`
+ Observed context: `main/UI` thread
+ Expected context: `worker/background` thread

## What I found

The analyzer reported that `UploadDeviceInfoFragment.onViewCreated()` calls Android’s synchronous geocoder API from a UI lifecycle callback:

```kotlin
val geocoder = Geocoder(requireContext())
var addresses: List<Address>?
try {
    addresses = geocoder.getFromLocation(location.latitude, location.longitude, 1)
} catch (e: IOException) {
    addresses = null
}
```

The Android documentation for the synchronous `Geocoder#getFromLocation(...)` API warns that it may hit the network and block for excessive time; Android strongly encourages the asynchronous API or a background thread.

## Verified bug trace

```text
UploadDeviceInfoFragment.onViewCreated(view, savedInstanceState)
  -> Fragment lifecycle callback executes on the main/UI thread
  -> obtains device latitude/longitude from last location
  -> creates android.location.Geocoder
  -> calls Geocoder.getFromLocation(latitude, longitude, 1)
  -> Android geocoder backend may perform network or service-backed work
  -> analyzer observed the call on the main/UI thread
  -> possible upload-device-info screen delay / UI freeze / ANR risk
```

## Why this matters

Reverse geocoding may depend on a backend service and can block. On devices with slow network, unavailable geocoder backends, or transient service delays, this path can keep the fragment’s UI lifecycle callback busy. That can produce visible jank or, in worse cases, an ANR.

## Possible fix

Move the synchronous reverse-geocoding call to a worker context and post only the result back to the main thread:

```kotlin
viewLifecycleOwner.lifecycleScope.launch {
    val country = withContext(Dispatchers.IO) {
        try {
            if (!Geocoder.isPresent()) return@withContext null
            val geocoder = Geocoder(requireContext())
            geocoder.getFromLocation(location.latitude, location.longitude, 1)
                ?.firstOrNull()
                ?.countryCode
        } catch (e: IOException) {
            null
        }
    }

    userCountry = country
    // update UI state here if needed
}
```

For API 33+, another option is to use the asynchronous geocoder overload with `Geocoder.GeocodeListener`.

## Reference

+ Android API: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/location/Geocoder#getFromLocation(double,%20double,%20int)
  + Relevant Android documentation wording: “This API may hit the network, and may block for excessive amounts of time. It's strongly encouraged to use the asynchronous version of this API. If that is not possible, this should be run on a background thread to avoid blocking other operations.”
+ Latest source inspected: https://github.com/barbeau/gpstest/blob/master/GPSTest/src/main/java/com/android/gpstest/ui/share/UploadDeviceInfoFragment.kt

title: CI can hang on crashpad handler
body:
**Describe the bug**
During the instrumented tests, the pipeline can get stuck after the tests are run and the emulator exited, because the crashpad_handler process hangs indefinitely.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Trigger the CI pipeline

**Expected behavior**
Pipeline terminates when all tests are run

**App, Device and Android version:** 
Happens mostly for a target SDK v29, but not consistently

**Suggested fix**
Kill the hanging process on Gradle wrapper termination:

`./gradlew ${{ matrix.tasks }} --stacktrace && killall -INT crashpad_handler || true`

title: Glonass L1 L2 L3 CDMA frequency
body:
According to glonass official ICDs. I found the following CDMA frequency. 
L1:1600.995 MHz
L2:1248.06 MHz
L3:1202.025 MHz
Cross reference with an research article on new GLONASS K2 sat https://www.gpsworld.com/glonass-cdma-signals-now-on-l1-l2/
We can add that to gpstest codebase and just in case some GNSS chipset support it in the foreseeable future. 
The current L1 CDMA frequency is wrong in the code 

BTW we can rename those signals as glonass is well-known as FDMA modulation. CDMA is something absolute new to it. 
Maybe L1-C L2-C is good to tell it? 
But for beginners it could be confused with GPS L1C, which is already on some smartphone today. People may wondering "Glonass use similar tech like GPS III ?"

The official name is L1OC, L2OC, L3OC. They are longer thus very unlikely to be misread by users. 

L1 CDMA is the most accurate name but seem unnecessary.

title: [meta] Stale bot considered harmful
body:
tl;dr: The stale bot is fundamentally a wrong thing.  It should be de-configured.

<rant>The stale bot is dysfunctional.  Just because an issue has not had activity does not meet it is invalid or unworthy of the space it takes up in the tracker.  I say this as someone who, as maintainer of an unrelated projects, aggressively closes non-useful issues.   if someone who cares doesn't react fast enough, a bug can be incorrectly closed.   On yet another unrelated project, I have seen issues closed, and even with commenting, not re-opened.

It's fine if the maintainer decides that an issue is so speculative that it is not worth the cognitive load.   I have on occasion scanned the entire issue list, asked for updates, and closed some -- especially those on which I have previously asked questions with no response.   But I see zero value in timers.
<rant>

title: Quick Settings tile has ~5s launch delay - app drawer always instant
body:
### Version

3.4.2

### Source

Google Play

### Vault encryption

No

### Device

Pixel 9 Pro

### Android version

Android 16 (patch 5 March 2026)

### ROM

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

- Close all apps
- Reboot the device -> Quick Settings tile launches Aegis instantly (sometimes)
- Use the phone normally for a few minutes / serveral hours
- Tap the Aegis Quick Settings tile -> ~5 second delay before app opens ❌
- Tap the Aegis icon in the app drawer -> opens instantly ✅

Additional info:
- No app lock or password is configured in Aegis
- Aegis has unrestricted battery optimization and unlimited background data
- My vault contains 54 entries
- Whether Aegis is open in the background or fully closed makes no difference -> the ~5s delay is identical in both cases
- Clearing the app cache or force-stopping Aegis does not fix the delay
- A full device reboot **sometimes** does fix it, but only temporarily


### What do you expect to happen?

The Quick Settings tile should launch Aegis with no noticeable delay, consistent with the app drawer shortcut.

### What happens instead?

~5 second delay before app opens

### Log

_No response_

title: copy token to clipboard doesn't work like before
body:
### Version

3.3.1

### Source

F-Droid

### Vault encryption

Yes (with biometric unlock)

### Device

Pixel 6a

### Android version

Android 15

### ROM

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

In Setting --> Behavior --> Copy token to the clipboard --> **single tap**

The tokens are reveal when tap on it (tap to reveal in security setting).

Tap on a token will reveal the code. The code is not copied to clipboard.

Tap again the token will be hide and the code is copied to the clipboard.

### What do you expect to happen?

When tap the first time on a token, the code should copied to clipboard (when the code is revealed).

This is what happened in version before 3.3.1

### What happens instead?

Token is copied at the second tap, when the token is hidden again.

I also tried Setting --> Behavior --> Copy token to the clipboard --> **double tap**.

Here you have to tap twice quickly like except.


At last I disabled **tap to reveal**. Now the code is copied at the first tap.

It seems that something is wrong when **tap to reveal is enabled** and you want to copy the code at the first tap

### Log

_No response_

title: Scan QR code not working on v2.0.1
body:
<!-- Please read the [bug reports section of the contribution
guidelines](/CONTRIBUTING.md#bug-reports) before submitting an issue. -->

###### Info

* __Version__: 2.0.1
* __Source__: Google Play
* __Vault encrypted__: Yes (with biometric unlock)
* __Device__: Motorola G100
* __Android version and ROM__: Andoid 11, security patch 1 July 2021

###### Steps to reproduce

1) Add a new entry via QR code from camera.. Camera opens but doesn't detect any QR code.

2) Open camera app and take photo of QR code. (Phone's camera app already shows the decoded text, so quality is ok.)  Close camera app, open Aegis, and add new entry via QR code from image. Select the photo just taken. Result is Error, cannot decode QR code, details are `com.google.zxing.NotFoundException`.

###### What do you expect to happen?

Can add entry via QR code both from camera or photo/image.

###### What happens instead?

1) Camera doesn't detect any QR code.

2) Scan from image results in error `com.google.zxing.NotFoundException`.

title: Pending sync not shown in some cases
body: When a sync has not been initiated by the sync framework yet, there's no pending worker, and we currently don't show that a sync is pending, which is confusing.

title: Temperature in notification displays in Fahrenheit instead of Celsius when updating in background
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Set temperature to Celsius 
2. Reboot phone 
3. Wait for notification to show

### Expected behavior

Temperature should be in Celsius

### Actual behavior

Temperature is in Fahrenheit 

### Weather source used

Open Meteo

### Breezy Weather version

6.2.0_freenet 

### Android version

Android Go 11

### Device

Cat S22 Flip

Temperature will update to Celsius when the app is launched but will go back to being in Fahrenheit the next time a background update occurs

title: [China] Nowcasting: Invalid or incomplete data received from server
body: ### Steps to reproduce Using the China data source Adding a location where it is currently raining ### Expected behavior Successfully refresh and display the Precipitation nowcasting chart ### Actual behavior Error prompted: `(Precipitation nowcasting): Invalid or incomplete data received from server.` The Precipitation nowcasting chart is not displayed. ### Weather source used China ### Breezy Weather version 6.20 ### Android version Android 14 ### Device Unihertz Jelly Max ### Other details I saw that https://github.com/breezy-weather/breezy-weather/issues/2630 claims this is a data source issue that existed in several previous versions, and it's only now being reported as an error. I performed a packet capture analysis of Breezy Weather's network requests. First, I noticed that compared to version 6.0.11, the requested domain changed from `weatherapi.market.xiaomi.com` to `weatherapi.intl.xiaomi.com`. The response content was `{"status":-1}`, but due to the lack of documentation, I'm not sure what that means. I used `curl` to request the captured URL and found that when making multiple consecutive requests, the first few returned `{"status":-1}`, while later requests returned valid data (see the figure below). Moreover, for the next several minutes, any additional requests returned valid data. This is strange – it doesn't seem like rate limiting due to too‑frequent requests, but rather as if the server is preparing the data. At the same time, I tried replacing `intl` with `market` in the request URL and found that the request then returned valid data. I checked the git commit history and found that this change came from commit https://github.com/breezy-weather/breezy-weather/commit/5a7711bb3d862d3f3afee226dffc27c5b9905117, whose message is "update changelog". It does not provide a detailed explanation for the URL change. The changelog mentions fixing a refresh failure issue for some users, but considering previous commits, that description likely refers to an earlier fix for the double encoding issue with the China source. The China source lacks effective documentation; I don't know where `weatherapi.intl.xiaomi.com` comes from. All the information I can find uses `weatherapi.market.xiaomi.com`. Additionally, during testing I found that even with identical parameters, the data returned by the two domains differs slightly. For example, `weatherapi.intl.xiaomi.com` returns a 5‑day precipitation probability and states that the precipitation probability data comes from AccuWeather, whereas `weatherapi.market.xiaomi.com` returns a 15‑day precipitation probability, states that the precipitation probability data comes from Caiyun, and also includes typhoon and minute‑by‑minute precipitation forecasts. Using online domain resolution tools to look up information on these two domains, I found that the IP address of `weatherapi.intl.xiaomi.com` is located in Singapore, while the IP address of `weatherapi.market.xiaomi.com` is located in Beijing, China. These observations lead me to a hypothesis: these two domains might primarily serve users outside China and users inside China, respectively. **Suggestion** Revert back to `weatherapi.market.xiaomi.com`, considering that the majority of China source users are located within China. Alternatively, split the China source into two options, one using each domain, with clear notes indicating that users inside China and users outside China should choose the appropriate one. ### Acknowledgements - [x] I have searched the existing issues and this is a new ticket, **NOT** a duplicate or related to another open OR closed issue. - [x] I checked that my issue is not covered in the [Frequently Asked Questions](https://github.com/breezy-weather/breezy-weather/blob/main/HELP.md) document. - [x] I have updated the app to **[version 6.2.0 or later](https://github.com/breezy-weather/breezy-weather/releases)** and I can reproduce the issue in this version. - [x] I wrote my request in English, and any screenshot I attached are also in English (language can be changed from Settings > Appearance), unless my issue only happens with a specific language. I can write in my native language under the English text, if necessary. - [x] I understand that this app is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY, so I may not get a positive reply to my request, if any at all.

title: Temperature animation duration is excessive for Kelvins
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Clear app if it is running in the background
2. Open the app
3. Measure the time it takes for the temperature to fully show
4. Change temperature unit to Kelvins
5. Clear app
6. Open the app
7. Measure the time it takes for the temperature to fully show

### Expected behavior

The user should not be punished for using Kelvins instead of a more sensible unit for weather by waiting longer to get the same information. Either make the temperature animation duration fixed or deduct a fixed ammount of time from it when one uses Kelvins.

### Actual behavior

The animation takes longer when the quatity of temperature is higher.
300 K would take longer than 13 °C.
This is really only an issue if one uses Kelvins (and maybe Fahrenheit).

### Weather source used

Open-Meteo - irrelevant

### Breezy Weather version

6.2.0_freenet

### Android version

Android 15

### Device

Moto G 5G

### Other details

Related issue: #2732

title: Today appears twice in Daily forecast if replacing Open-Meteo with KNMI as forecast source
body: ### Steps to reproduce - Select Koninklijke Nederlands Meteorologisch Instituut as the "Forecast" source for a location in the Netherlands - Observe that today appears twice in de the daily forecast block. title: Confirming daily intake via notification after midnight confirms the intake for the next day
body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Schedule a daily intake shortly before midnight.
2. Wait for midnight to pass.
3. Click "Taken" on the notification.

Result:
- The notification will reappear (with alert) after 5 Minutes.
- In the app, the intake hasn't been confirmed yet.
- Instead, the next occurence of the intake will already be confirmed ~24h ahead of time.
- The erroneously confirmed intake behaves as expected, i.e. shows no notification on the next day.

Untested:
- What happens with non-daily schedules?

title: Set first reminder to first repeat day when repeat on some days option is used.
body:
Creating a schedule right now sets the first reminder (Start Date) on the same day it is created as default even if "repeat on some days" are used.

I think the first reminder should be set to the first new day that is a valid repeat day on the "repeat on some days" option.
If I create a schedule on a Saturday that repeats on Mondays and Thursdays, I expect it to remind me about it only on Mondays and Thursdays and not on the Saturday it was created.

title: Calendula app crashes when accessing notification settings
body:
#### Expected behavior
The app should remain stable and should not crash when notifications sound is changed. 


#### Actual behavior

The app crashes when a new notification sound is selected and continues subsequent crashes that ensures users can no  longer  notification settings.



#### How to reproduce

Download and install latest app version

Click on settings (This can be accessed by clicking on the 3 lines in the top left corner of the app)
 
Rotate phone to landscape mode

Click on notifications 

Change notification sound  to an mp3 song preferably


The app crashes and notification sound  cannot be accessed again until it is re-installed




* Browser/App version: Calendula app version 2.5.5
*Operating system: Android 6.0


#### Recording Of The Bug
This is the recording showing the crash when trying to set the notifications settings
https://youtu.be/Mq7dT0daAIA

This is the recording showing subsequent crashes
https://youtu.be/RPYrDiEuX4U
