title: [UR: 4] Allow users to choose backup location
body:
Closing in favour of #1078.

Should seriously consider using a SQLite (Free)/Firestore (Pro) hybrid model. This will be the most reliable data backup method, and includes device data sync as an added bonus.

For free users, we can include instructions on setting up automatic device backups.

-----------------------------------------------
Background:
* The app showed my last backup as "Sunday at X" (today is April 30th, 2025).
* I added a catch. The new "last backup" value updated to the current time.

In preparation for testing 2.7.5, I uninstalled and reinstalled the app, then restored from Google Drive. After the restoration, the app said the last backup was on March 4th. As a result, I lost all data between March 4th and today (it was only 2 trips). But what happened here?

Need to look into:
* Automatic backup behaviour. Why did the last backup date update, but the actual backed up data did not?
* Could this have anything to do with Google Drive's "24 hour delay" for private app data? 
* Is this "24 hour delay" applicable if we were to use a non-private folder?

If we can't rely on the Google Drive backup, what do we do?

Considerations:
* Related to #743 
* Should we not use a private Google Drive folder, and instead package everything in an archive with custom extension (similar to Quicken)? Private app data isn't accessible, so if we ever needed a user's data for debugging, there's no way to get it. This would have been helpful multiple times.
* Perhaps we scrap Google Drive altogether, and implement #743 where the user can choose their backup location, which could include _any_ cloud solution they use.
* Really, having any kind of delay after a backup is a poor user experience.
* ChatGPT related to folder picking using Flutter: https://chatgpt.com/share/68125d98-78a8-800b-abe6-35934d43b7b9
  * Possible in Android (possibly with path_provider)
  * Possible in iOS by [bookmarking a directory](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/providing-access-to-directories?changes=_8) (no _recent_ Flutter plugins)
  * We've had several people contact support with backup/restoration issues, including a recent 1 star review because they couldn't restore their data on a new device (reasons are unknown at the moment. Update: After talking with the user, I'm convinced they never backed up their data in the first place).

**AC**
- [ ] Users should be able to get their hands on their backed up data (i.e. a `backup.anglerslog` package).
- [ ] Users can _choose_ their backup location.
- [ ] Automatic backups are immediate and reliable. 
- [ ] Manual backup reminders. 
- [ ] Include device backup recommendations on backup page.

title: Error during backup/restore: "Software caused connection abort"
body: Closing in favour of #1078. **Reproduction Steps:** 1. Add a few catches with photos (so a backup takes a bit of time). 2. Start a backup. 3. Put the app in to the background. 4. After a few seconds, `D/AL-BackupRestoreManager: Unknown backup or restore error: ClientException - ClientException: Software caused connection abort` will show in the console. **Solution:** First, we want to improve UX around this issue. #1077 greatly improved the speed at which backups and restores are done, which will already help tremendously. - [ ] Investigate loading widget lag (may need to move backup/restore to an isolate). - [ ] Add a "for best results, keep the app in the foreground" dialog from which the user confirms to continue with the backup/restore. - [ ] When an auto-backup starts, show a snack bar to indicate progress and to incentivize keeping the app in the foreground. - [ ] If the backup or restore stops due to the "connection abort" error: - [ ] Update the notification and backup/restore page error message to something like "Backup was paused due to network interruption. Tap here to continue." - [ ] The "error" on the backup/restore page should be downgraded to a warning label, and the "Send Report" button should be removed. - [ ] Resume backup when app is foregrounded - [ ] Hide unreliability warning for backup/restore features added in 913e915f4dc0d5ac78e5161b4f5980c384c700bc and 651cbfbbb2000ca5c5d4b781587a2848e0ddfb4d. - [ ] Create ticket for removing dead code, assuming the solution here works. > Note that using `WorkManager` to create background tasks is overkill for our use case, given that 99% of the time, we're uploading a couple images immediately after a user action. ------------------------- This has been reported twice now by different users, both on Android. ``` App version: 2.7.7 OS version: Android (35) Device: CPH2653 Device ID: AP3A.240617.008 Device Locale: en_GB RevenueCat ID: REDACTED User Type: Pro Feedback type: Bug Error: ClientException: Software caused connection abort, uri=https://u25455220.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.3Km3Zvj39Gyc-2FxvIAhDKUjLlnOcODVAbXo6dupz4kzPvPowYKM-2FW-2FEFGq7n9rfQoZTYqtFNHYcwFZPKsUG78qzlq5F8Gu1HSxSD7dPMrRXySRoCvjGxS1lxvJFSkpZP0UO82_wmc-2BViHeaSAOdjPZKiSJ-2FV0nSDncX6xPM7j95V2FO98WH5mZ8fl4-2F66eziZ2Pi0dOplxuFYKdEW1Ux929u9EgXfb-2FIC0NWb0XW7YoKpBEkFVgOApztDwf1ai4mo-2F0a8yamJdJmJxpogG48SbvxuU-2FpKpVK7nv-2B8F-2Fb3a2Y6DCXViFvNcdB7s71WLUTcFWmrr2iA7Svh019p9KBr5mm78vw-3D-3D Name: REDACTED Email: REDACTED Message: Happens each time I add a catch.but always backups up when I re run it. Thanks. Attachment: BackupRestorePage - The network may have been interrupted. Verify your internet connection and try again. If the issue persists, please send Anglers' Log a report for investigation. ``` ``` App version: 2.7.8 OS version: Android (35) Device: Pixel 9 Pro Device ID: BP1A.250505.005 Device Locale: en_US RevenueCat ID: REDACTED User Type: Pro Feedback type: Bug Error: ClientException: Software caused connection abort, uri=https://u25455220.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.3Km3Zvj39Gyc-2FxvIAhDKUjLlnOcODVAbXo6dupz4kzPvPowYKM-2FW-2FEFGq7n9rfQoZTYqtFNHYcwFZPKsUG78qzlq5F8Gu1HSxSD7dPMrRXySRoCvjGxS1lxvJFSkpZP0Vd0O_wmc-2BViHeaSAOdjPZKiSJ-2FV0nSDncX6xPM7j95V2FO99s-2FRoE5zT18Zgui-2BaYmE3-2BBH7sAkF6XL9Epe9JUKncI1wCG9MqZZva2SFZD4yoPoP2-2F7-2F-2Fp-2BX5AqmEkzNfxGpjTl55IuEYMjXLF-2FrJDhHJuv5fIp-2BfSO3ikI8hcTlysOPfRn6nCYCJvjgXqYCtjn79SAhW6oHPJyj4UNZbnt5UPQ-3D-3D Name: REDACTED Email: REDACTED Message: This is the error report from the backup error. title: Backup error after adding photo from WhatsApp
body:
From a user:
> I got an error with the backup after adding a photo (the cooked fish) from whatsapp. There were two existing photos before I added the whatsapp Pic. Thanks

It's unclear what "adding a photo from WhatsApp" means, since WhatsApp isn't a photo app. Will have to investigate. In the meantime, I've requested the user to elaborate.

**Considerations**
* "Software caused connection abort" means something on the app side caused the backup to fail. We need to catch the actual error earlier, likely the result of an uncaught `Future` error, to get the actual line of code causing problems.
* This user's data was included in the bug report, so we can try to use that to reproduce the problem. Data file has been uploaded to iCloud Drive.
* Should mess around with WhatsApp on Android to see if I we can figure out how to add a photo to Anglers' Log.

title: Unknown number format: #’###’###.##
body:
Uses a different apostrophe character than what we support in the app. From ChatGPT:
```
Yes, #'###'###.## and #’###’###.## are different due to the type of apostrophe used:
	•	#'###'###.## uses the straight apostrophe: ' (Unicode U+0027)
	•	#’###’###.## uses the right single quotation mark: ’ (Unicode U+2019)

While they may look similar in many fonts, they are distinct characters and will not be treated the same by functions like NumberFormat, which expects exact string matches for formats.
```

```
https://console.firebase.google.com/u/0/project/anglers-log/crashlytics/app/android:com.cohenadair.anglerslog/issues/19a30124278c8628da69060bb0323100?time=last-seven-days&sessionEventKey=68485385023D00016C36A35B53E7F91E_2092796630884807126
```

title: Fix exceptions in AppManager initializing
body:
If an exception is thrown while `AppManager` is initializing, the app just freezes on the launch screen (there's a TODO item in `app_manager.dart:361`). This hasn't been a huge issue until recently, when a user reported not being able to use the app at all. He's based in the Ukraine, and both him and his wife (who never had the app installed before) get stuck on the launch screen. I'm unable to reproduce the issue, but my suspicion is the app initializing code is throwing an exception. This needs to be fixed so that, at the very least, the issue gets logged to Crashlytics.

**Reproduction Steps:**
1. Set device timezone to Ukraine/Kyiv (#1006)
2. Kill and restart the app
3. Verify app freezes

**AC:**
* When reproduced, app crashes shows an error (or crashes) instead of doing nothing.

title: values-x-invalidLanguageCode: Invalid resource directory name
body:
A recent commit from TranslateWiki added a `app/src/main/res/values-x-invalidLanguageCode` directory, which results in this build error:

app/src/main/res/values-x-invalidLanguageCode: Resource and asset merger: Invalid resource directory name

Maybe `ks` got deprecated as a language code and we should edit our TranslateWiki configuration? I believe we had similar issues in the past:

- https://github.com/commons-app/commons-app-documentation/blob/master/android/Translating-the-app.md#fixing-language-mappings-between-translatewiki-and-android
- https://gerrit.wikimedia.org/r/c/translatewiki/+/826587

**Workaround**: Until the problem is fixed, when creating pull requests, feel free to remove that directory.

title: Nearby can not track current location or it takes too long to do
body:
**Summary:** 
This issue is created based on a feedback in our mailing list. They say usually the Commons App fails to follow them. They must flick the app out of memory and restart it, for it to know their location. It also takes much longer than any other app in their phone ( Strava, Google Maps, MSN Weather) to find their location. Thus they still must use WikiShootMe despite its disadvantages. 
Most often they use Commons App to snap and upload, after using WSM to find a target in whatever place they are visiting. 

So the issue is making sure current location is followed by the app and checking if there are any improvements that we can made to make it quicker.

title: [Bug]: Unable to restart failed uploads
body:

I would expect in step `6.` that I am able to upload pictures that I've painstakingly taken, described and categorized!

also (less importantly) one would expect that in step `4.` queued uploads would start automatically

Unable to upload pictures in any way I can think of. Fear complete data and effort loss, very demotivating.

title: [BUG]: Can't handle mutiple artists of opus tag
body: ### Bug Description It `` when using opus tags' native multiple artist. You can specify multiple artist by repeating a comment. E.g. ```ini ARTISTS=First ARTISTS=First ``` For this following track it only shows `NF` as artist (Probably using fallback to `ALBUMARTIST`): ```bash $ -> opustags "5. SORRY.opus" | rg ARTIST ALBUMARTIST=NF ALBUMARTISTSORT=NF ARTISTSORT=NF & Arthur, James ARTISTS=NF ARTISTS=James Arthur ``` But this is showing ``: ```bash $ -> opustags "1. Clarity (BUNT. remix).opus" | rg ARTIST ALBUMARTISTSORT=Zedd ARTISTSORT=Zedd ARTISTS=Zedd ARTISTS=Foxes ``` I believe this is due to how `retriever.extractMetadata(android.media.MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_ARTIST)` handles metadata. > [!IMPORTANT] > I've removed the `ARTIST` tag because that is used by default. Thanks for making this app! ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Install `ffmpeg` and `opustags` ```bash # Convert an audio file to opus ffmpeg -hide_banner -i soundfile.opus ffprobe soundfile.opus # Set Mutiple ARTISTS by opustags CLI opustags --set ARTISTS=first --set ARTISTS=second -i soundfile.opus # Verify tags opustags soundfile.opus ffprobe -hide_banner soundfile.opus ``` ```ini encoder=Lavc62.11.100 libopus ARTISTS=first ARTISTS=second ``` 2. Copy the `soundfile.opus` to your phone: ### Expected vs Actual Behavior It should respect `ARTISTS` fields and fallback to `ARTIST` field. ### App Version 4.7.364.975-fdroid ### Android Version 13 ### Device Realme Narzo 50 ### Installation Method F-Droid ### Additional Context _No response_ ### Logs ```shell ``` ### Attach screenshots here if necessary. _No response_

I tried to change various of font but it still not changes ### Steps to Reproduce ### Expected vs Actual Behavior Expected: Đằng sau sân khấu Actual: ??ng sau s�n kh?u ### App Version 4.6.361.969-gh ### Android Version Android 16 ### Device Oneplus Ace 5 ### Installation Method GitHub Releases ### Additional Context _No response_ ### Logs ```shell ``` ### Attach screenshots here if necessary. _No response_

title: [BUG]: The drop-down menu does not switch the screen by default
body:
### Bug Description

duplicatе
The drop-down menu does not switch the screen by default

sry for the bug report over such a minor issue :o 

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Launch the app for the first time
2. Click "Continue Full Tour"
3. Try to change default start screen

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

Expected: Change default startscreen
Actual: Not changed 

### App Version

4.6.361.969-gh

### Android Version

_No response_

### Device

_No response_

### Installation Method

F-Droid

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [BUG]: Unselected artists splitters are still being applied
body:
### Bug Description

As of Rhythm version 4.5.353.95-fdroid it appears the artist name splitter no longer 'cares' about the splitter blacklist.

Previously, I had disabled/unselected the ampersand (&) from the artist splitters, cause I listen to music from the artist named "For King & Country" for example.
On the previously installed version, this worked fine, it did not split that name and it was fine.

However since above mentioned version, even when the ampersand is set to be excluded, it does still split on it!

*Also the save and cancel buttons are out of view, at least on my screen. Consider adding a scrollbar to that popup*

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Download a song from an artist with an ampersand in their name (or rename a songs artist name to one with an ampersand)
2. Open Rhythm and check the library artists list
3. It should split right now
4. Go to settings -> artists and then change the splitter settings to exclude ampersand and save
5. Go back to the library and see it still splits

### Expected vs Actual Behavior

Expected the artist name to not get split on the excluded splitters just like previous versions of the app

but in reality it does still split the artist name on the excluded splitters

### App Version

4.5.353.95-fdroid

### Android Version

Android 8

### Device

Samsung A52

### Installation Method

title: Cross-swipe on ABC key2_2 with layout NOThumbKeyDataDriven unresponsive .
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Apply layout NOThumbKeyDataDriven.
2. Attempt to swipe left or up on the bottom right key.
3. Expected letters will not be typed.

### Expected behavior

Swiping left should type "x", swiping up should type "q".

### Actual behavior

"f" or "e" is typed instead.

### version of the program

5.1.7

### Android version

Android 16

### Device

Pixel 9 Pro

### Other details

As far as I can tell, the issue seems to be that the swipeType is explicitly set to FOUR_WAY_DIAGONAL. Changing this to EIGHT_WAY with YAML fixes the issue.

title: USB device visible but permission never becomes usable for M5Stack CoreS3
body:
## Classification
Bug, Device

## Summary
When connecting an M5Stack CoreS3 to me.things on Android, the device is successfully enumerated but never reaches a usable USB permission state.

Although the device appears in `usb.status`, the permission state remains `has_permission: false`, preventing serial communication.

## Environment
Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate:

- me.things version: v0.4.2
- git SHA: cb79ca3607985e59b4cf7eb0f3c50d01817b8de0
- Android version: 15
- Device model: Sony XQ-DQ44
- Brain provider/model: openai / gpt-5.4
- Relevant permission state:
  - CAMERA: granted
  - RECORD_AUDIO: not granted
  - ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION: not granted
  - ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION: not granted
  - Bluetooth-related permissions: granted
  - notifications: granted
- External hardware involved:
  - M5Stack CoreS3

## Goal
Use a USB-connected M5Stack CoreS3 from me.things, starting with device recognition and then establishing serial communication.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Connect M5Stack CoreS3 to the Android device via USB.
2. Check whether me.things enumerates the device.
3. Attempt to grant permission and re-check device state.
4. Inspect `usb.status` after reconnection or re-enumeration.

## Observed Behavior

me.things successfully enumerates the USB device and reports:

- manufacturer: `Espressif`
- product: `USB JTAG/serial debug unit`
- vendor_id: `12346`
- product_id: `4097`

Example device path observed:

- `/dev/bus/usb/001/006`

However, the USB permission state remains:

```
has_permission: false
pending_permission_requests: []
```

As a result, serial communication cannot be started from me.things.

## Expected Behavior

After enumeration and user approval, me.things should:

1. obtain USB permission
2. open the USB device
3. allow serial communication with the M5Stack CoreS3

## Success Criteria

- USB permission becomes usable after device enumeration.
- `usb.open` succeeds for the enumerated device.
- Serial communication can be established with the board.

## Impact
Blocks communication with USB-connected hardware devices such as M5Stack CoreS3.

## Additional Observations

A control test was performed using the Android app **Serial USB Terminal** with the same device, cable, and Android phone.

That app was able to start serial communication successfully.

This suggests:

- the cable is functioning correctly
- the board itself is functioning
- Android can communicate with the device
- the issue likely lies in me.things' USB permission handling or device open logic

## Notes
Possible causes include:

- USB permission request not being triggered correctly
- permission state not being reflected after approval
- stale device identifiers after re-enumeration
- unsupported handling of the `Espressif USB JTAG/serial debug unit` interface

Improving USB permission diagnostics and device matching may help identify the root cause.

title: Severe UI slowdown and device heating during WebView polling
body:
## Classification
Bug, Performance

## Summary
While experimenting with UI customization, the app UI became extremely slow and the device became noticeably hot.

Even after resetting the UI back to default and reloading it, the interface still felt heavy and sluggish.

The behavior suggests that frequent polling in the WebView UI may cause excessive CPU usage.

## Environment
Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate:

- me.things version: v0.4.0
- git SHA: 2e4e8fc940ecec87709e4163a823891ba3d1103b
- Android version: 15
- Device model: Sony XQ-DQ44
- Brain provider/model: openai / gpt-5.4

## Goal
Verify that UI customization and normal interaction remain responsive without causing excessive CPU usage or device heating.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Perform minimal UI customization tests.
2. Use the UI normally after customization.
3. Observe responsiveness and device temperature.

## Observed Behavior
After performing minimal UI customization tests:

- the UI became extremely slow
- typing lag increased
- navigation felt sluggish
- the device became hot
- resetting the UI to default did not fully restore responsiveness

Eventually the next step was to reinstall the app.

During investigation, several frequent polling loops appeared to exist in the UI logic, roughly around:

- ~700 ms
- ~1000 ms
- ~1200 ms

These polling loops appeared related to:

- permission state checks
- secret field lock refresh
- sync / progress refresh
- pin / authentication timing refresh
- status banner updates

When multiple timers run continuously in a WebView, it may cause:

- high idle CPU usage
- device heating
- laggy UI interaction

## Expected Behavior
The UI should remain responsive and not significantly heat the device during idle or light interaction.

## Success Criteria
- The UI remains responsive during normal usage.
- Device temperature remains within normal operating range.
- Background polling does not cause excessive CPU usage.

## Impact
Severely degrades usability due to laggy UI interaction and device heating.

## Additional Observations
Frequent polling loops in the WebView UI may contribute to unnecessary CPU usage.

## Notes
This issue was discovered while investigating UI customization behavior.

Related:
- #21

title: Custom UI elements disappear after restart when modifying `www/index.html`
body:
## Classification
Bug, UX

## Summary
When experimenting with UI customization in me.things, a custom UI element added to `www/index.html` appeared and worked correctly at first but disappeared after restarting the app.

This suggests that custom UI modifications may not persist reliably across restart or reload cycles.

## Environment
Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate:

- me.things version: v0.4.0
- git SHA: 2e4e8fc940ecec87709e4163a823891ba3d1103b
- Android version: 15
- Device model: Sony XQ-DQ44
- Brain provider/model: openai / gpt-5.4

## Goal
Verify whether lightweight UI extensions can be added by modifying `www/index.html`.

Specifically:
- add a document icon (`📄`) near the gear icon
- open a Notes screen when tapped
- allow memo input using a textarea

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Modify `www/index.html` to add a custom document icon and Notes screen.
2. Launch the app.
3. Confirm the Notes UI appears and works.
4. Restart the app.
5. Observe whether the custom UI remains.

## Observed Behavior

Initially:

- the document icon appeared in the top-right
- tapping it opened the Notes screen
- text input worked

After restarting the app:

- the custom document icon disappeared
- the Notes UI was no longer accessible

## Expected Behavior
Custom UI changes to `www/index.html` should remain stable across restart unless explicitly reset.

## Success Criteria
- Custom UI elements added to `www/index.html` remain visible after app restart.
- The Notes UI remains accessible after restart.

## Impact
Prevents reliable UI customization because changes do not persist across restart.

## Additional Observations
The initial UI behavior confirmed that the concept of lightweight UI extension works, but persistence across restart appears unreliable.

## Notes
This issue was originally observed while investigating UI customization instability.

Related:
- #21

title: Duplicate rendering of a single user message in chat UI
body: ## Classification Bug, UX ## Summary An identical user message was rendered twice consecutively in the chat UI. The duplication occurred without any intentional double-send action. ## Environment Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate: - me.things version: v0.3.3 - git SHA: 85e4334 - Android version: 15 (SDK 35) - Device model: Sony XQ-DQ44 ## Goal Send a single chat message and have it appear exactly once in the chat UI. ## Steps to Reproduce Reproduction conditions are currently unclear. ## Observed Behavior A single user message: > agents.md や tools.md に記録することはできますか？ was displayed twice in succession as separate chat blocks. There was no manual re-send, retry, or visible network error at the time of occurrence. ## Expected Behavior A user message should be rendered exactly once per send action. ## Success Criteria - Each message send action results in exactly one rendered chat block. - No duplicate message rendering occurs without a second send event. ## Reproducibility Rare ## Impact Causes UI confusion and reduces trust in message delivery behavior. ## Additional Observations The issue was observed during normal interactive use. This may indicate duplication in message dispatch, rendering, or retry logic within the UI layer. ## Attachments Screenshot evidence: ## Notes Reproduction conditions are currently unclear.

title: Image preview UX gaps and instability when using Select Text on image blocks
body:
## Classification
Bug, UX

## Summary
Multi-image capture and preview behavior works well and closely matches LINE-style interaction. Multiple images can be attached in a single chat block and browsed with swipe gestures.

However, several UX gaps and one critical instability were observed during testing.

## Environment
Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate:

- me.things version: v0.2.13+

## Goal
Capture multiple photos in a chat block and review them using the built-in preview system.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Capture multiple images in a single chat message.
2. Tap one of the images to enter preview mode.
3. Swipe left or right to browse images.
4. Long-press an image and use the available menu options.

## Observed Behavior

### Multi-image preview (working well)

The following behavior works correctly and closely matches LINE UX:

- Multiple photos can be attached within one chat block.
- Tapping an image opens preview mode.
- Swiping left/right browses images in the same set.
- Swiping beyond the set moves to images in adjacent chat blocks.

### Preview screen UX gaps

Compared to typical messaging UI:

- No timestamp is shown in preview (e.g., `YYYY.MM.DD HH:MM`)
- No position indicator such as `1/3`, `2/3` for the current image

### Long-press menu behavior

When long-pressing an image inside a chat block, the following menu appears:

- Copy All
- Select Text
- Reference

#### Copy All

Result example:

```
Here's the photo I captured.
rel-path: captures/photo1.jpg
rel-path: captures/photo2.jpg
rel-path: captures/photo3.jpg
```

This behavior may be acceptable for advanced users but differs from typical messaging UX where Copy All is not shown for image blocks.

#### Select Text (critical issue)

Selecting **Select Text** on an image-containing chat block causes:

- the entire chat block to turn black
- the UI to become unstable
- recovery often requires restarting the app

#### Reference

Using **Reference** results in:

```
Here's the photo I captured.
rel-path: captures/photo2.jpg
```

The referenced image is represented only by its relative path rather than a visual thumbnail.

## Expected Behavior

- Preview mode should optionally display timestamps.
- Preview mode should display an image index indicator (e.g., `1/3`).
- Selection mode should not cause UI instability.
- Referencing an image should include a visual thumbnail rather than only a file path.

## Success Criteria

- Image preview displays optional timestamp and position indicators.
- Selecting **Select Text** on image blocks does not cause UI instability.
- Referenced images appear with visual context rather than only text paths.

## Impact

Selection mode instability affects usability and can interrupt normal chat interaction.

## Additional Observations

The current multi-image preview interaction is already well designed and close to a polished UX. Addressing the remaining issues would significantly improve the overall experience.

## Notes

The image preview system itself is already strong; the primary concern is the instability triggered by text selection in image-containing blocks.

title: Select Text prevents scrolling of the overall chat view
body:
## Classification
Bug, UX

## Summary
After the long-press menu (Copy All / Select Text / Reference) was introduced, text selection initially works as expected. However, once **Select Text** mode is activated, the overall chat screen becomes non-scrollable.

This prevents extending text selection beyond the currently visible portion of the chat.

## Environment
Only the minimum needed to reproduce or evaluate:

- me.things version: v0.2.13+

## Goal
Select and copy long messages using the **Select Text** feature.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Long-press a chat message.
2. Select **Select Text** from the menu.
3. Attempt to extend the selection beyond the currently visible message area.

## Observed Behavior
When Select Text mode is activated:

- Text selection handles appear.
- Selection handles can be dragged.
- Internal text scrolling inside the message block works.

However:

- The **overall chat screen cannot be scrolled**.
- Selection cannot be extended beyond the currently visible message region.

## Expected Behavior
While in text selection mode:

- The entire chat view should still be scrollable.
- Selection handles should allow extending selection across the full message.
- The interaction should match typical Android behavior for selectable text inside scrollable containers.

## Success Criteria
- The user can scroll the chat view while Select Text mode is active.
- Selection handles can extend the selection beyond the initially visible text.
- Long messages can be fully selected.

## Impact
Prevents users from selecting the full text of long messages, making the Select Text feature partially unusable.

## Additional Observations
The Select Text feature itself is very useful, but this scrolling limitation prevents it from working correctly for longer messages.

## Notes
This may be related to touch-event interception between the chat container (ScrollView / RecyclerView), the selectable TextView, and Android’s selection handle interaction layer.

title: Auto-expand input area stops permanently after manual resize (drag handle)
body:
## Environment

- App: me.things **v0.3.2**
- Android device (on-device usage)
- On-screen keyboard

---

## Summary

In v0.3.2, the message input area now auto-expands when adding new lines, which is very usable.

However, once I manually resize the input area using the draggable handle at the top of the input field, the auto-expand behavior stops working and does not return.

This state persists even after sending a message.

This behavior appears related to the auto-expand improvement discussed in

- #36 

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open a chat and type multi-line text.
2. Press Enter several times.
   - Confirm the input area auto-expands as expected.
3. Drag the resize handle (the knob at the top of the input area) to manually change the input field height.
4. Press Enter again to add more lines.
5. Send the message, then start composing a new message and press Enter again.

---

## Expected Behavior

- Auto-expand should continue to work even after manual resizing, or
- Auto-expand should reset after sending a message / starting a new message, or
- There should be a clear and intentional rule (e.g., manual resize temporarily overrides auto-expand, with an easy way to return to auto mode).

---

## Actual Behavior

- After manually resizing once, auto-expand stops working.
- The input area no longer grows automatically on newline.
- The behavior remains disabled even after sending messages.

---

## Notes

This feels like the input area enters a persistent "manual mode" after any resize action, without a way to return to "auto-expand mode".

title: Sent image messages are rendered as text (rel_path only) instead of visual thumbnails
body:
## Environment

- App: me.things v0.2.13+
- Android device (on-device usage)

---

## Summary

When I attach images and send a message, the outgoing chat block is rendered as text only, for example:

```
I attached the image.
rel_path:
uploads/chat/20260226_152950.JPG
rel_path:
uploads/chat/20260226_152956.JPG
rel_path:
uploads/chat/20260226_153002.JPG
```

or

```
I attached the image.
rel_path:
uploads/chat/20260226_153148.JPG
```

The images are functional and accessible, but visually the sent message contains only file paths and no thumbnails.

---

## Expected Behavior

In common messaging UI patterns (e.g., LINE):

- Sent images are displayed as visual thumbnails
- Multiple images appear grouped in a single message block
- The sender can visually confirm what was sent

Even if the internal representation uses `rel_path`, the UI layer should render image previews rather than raw paths.

---

## Why This Matters

Without visual thumbnails:

- The sender cannot easily confirm what was sent
- The message history becomes less intuitive
- The UI feels inconsistent with received image rendering

Rendering sent images as thumbnails would significantly improve usability and visual clarity.

---

## Note

This is not a functional defect (images are correctly stored and accessible), but a UI rendering improvement request.

title: 严重的隐私问题：在解锁时显示剪贴板内容/Serious Privacy Issue: Clipboard Content Displayed Upon Unlock
body:
### 摘要 / Summary

将小企鹅输入法设置为默认应用后，在初次解锁设备后再次解锁设备，剪贴板会默认打开，使他人可以不解锁手机即可阅读相关内容。

---
After setting the Little Penguin Keyboard as the default app, when unlocking the device for the first time and then unlocking it again, the clipboard will automatically open by default, allowing others to view its contents without unlocking the phone.

### 重现步骤 / Steps to Reproduce

1. 设置小企鹅输入法为默认输入法
2. 在重启手机后解锁手机。
3. 再次锁定手机（无论是直接按下电源键还是通过Android系统内置的“锁定”按钮）

---
1. Set Little Penguin Keyboard as the default input method.
2. Unlock the phone after restarting it.
3. Lock the phone again (either by pressing the power button directly or using the Android system's built-in "Lock" button).

### 预期行为 / Expected Behavior

剪贴板内容会被显示。

如何查看？
通过九键数字菜单即可查看。

---
The clipboard content will be displayed.
How to view?
It can be viewed through the nine-key numeric menu.

title: Crash when asjusting max-temp on coolant temp gauge
body:
# Describe the bug

App crashes upon pressing OK on the screen where the limits of the gauge can be adjusted.  Once it crashes it then crashes on every launch.  Only way out is to uninstall and reinstall.

## To Reproduce

Install app from F-Droid (v2.6.12).
Launch app.
Deny BlueTooth to get to demo mode (crash happens in both demo and using an ODB adapter mode).
Pick 0x10 as ECU address for demo mode.
Tap menu - pick "OBD Data".
Scroll down to "Coolant Temperature" and long press to select.
Tap "show gauge" icon in menu bar to get the graphical "speedometer" display.
Initial default units are metric.  But note that max temp on gauge is 120C.
Return to settings and pick Imperial units.
Return to coolant temperature gauge view.
Note that gauge reads degrees F, but maximum on gauge continues to read 120 (which would be 120F in imperial units mode).
120C (the original view) would be 248F, so I would have expected the gauge to update to show -40 to 248F in imperial mode.
So, long press the gauge to get to the gauge config panel.
Drag the very finicky slider to extend the gauge range to about 250F (and never being able to get exactly 250F because of the finicky slider.
Press "ok".
Android reports "AndrOBD has stopped".
Try to relaunch the app, get "AdrOBD keeps stopping".
The app must now be uninstalled to get out of this crash loop.

## Expected behavior

Updating the upper range of the gauge shows a new gauge display with the new upper range (and having two entries into which a numbers could be typed would be much more useful than the much too finicky slider).

## AndrOBD Debug log files

[AndrOBD.log.0.txt.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16462178/AndrOBD.log.0.txt.gz)

### Log detail level

Since I had to reinstall to get the app to work, I preset the log level to All.

## Important details on your environment:

- Android OS version: 7.1.1
- AndrOBD version: 2.6.12 (from F-Droid)
- OBD adapter type: ODBLink LX OBD2 Bluetooth adapter
- Connected vehicle make, type, year, engine size: Ford, Crown Victoria, 2002, 281ci/4.6l

## Additional context

title: AndrOBD crashes when establishing USB connection to ELM327 adapter
body:
# Describe the bug

When establishing the USB connection in the usual way, recent versions of AndrOBD crash as follows:

Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. plug USB cable from the adapter into the cellphone
2. confirm to open AndrOBD
3. The proposed USB connection is displayed. Click on it to confirm.
4. Android displays that AndrOBD has crashed.

## Expected behavior

Instead of crashing according to item 4, AndrOBD should open and communicate with the ELM327 adapter.

## AndrOBD Debug log files

## Additional context
Note: When no USB connection is established, e. g. with no cable connected to the cell phone, AndrOBD 2.6.13 will start. 

The last version which worked OK with the same adapter was  2.6.7.

We encountered an identically looking issue already in February 2021; it was fixed after a bug report.

title: False values O2-Lambda-Sensor
body:
![Untitled 1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84518891/133781151-8789f3ac-1bfe-488d-b2a6-780e9a65b6c8.png)


Hi, how is the lambda value O2 sensor calculated? I think the app miscalculates this value (Service 01 PID 24). The values that AndrODB shows me do not match a turbo diesel and other.

It looks like AndrOBD calculates the lambda value (Service 01 PID 24) like this:

EQ_RAT = 2/65535 * Byte_A_B_PID24

However, if Byte_A_PID4F (Service 01 PID 4F) is set, then this must be included in the calculation:

EQ_RAT = Byte_A_PID4F / 65535 * Byte_A_B_PID24

For me the Byte_A_PID4F = 32_ (10) and therefore has to multiply the AndrOBD values by 16 so that it fits.


![photo_2021-09-17_14-17-38](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84518891/133781376-ee6dcb58-e323-4a96-9d8c-7129320229a0.jpg)
![photo_2021-09-17_14-17-46](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84518891/133781401-15ab9ee3-0022-434e-bf6b-45deb328d54f.jpg)

**Important details on your environment:**
- Android OS version 10
- AndrOBD version 2.2.4
- OBD adapter type 
- Audi A3 Sportsback (8PA) 2005 2L TDI 140 PS

title: Mode 09 PID 00  ISO Standard is different
body:
In picture the blue byte should be a Message_Counter_Byte. AndrOBD interprets this byte as data and thinks that PIDs 08, 09, 0a, 0b, 0c are supported.
My car only supports PIDs 01, 02, 03, 04. That should be the first data byte F0 after the Message_Count_Byte. 

![screen-2021-09-21-23-36-03](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84518891/134251703-edad6b7b-4537-441e-adc4-d9a20e923366.png)


**Important details on your environment:**
- Android OS version 10
- AndrOBD version 2.4.2
- OBD adapter type 
- Audi A3 (8PA) 2005 2L 140PS


[Mode09PID00_ByteMisinterpretation.txt](https://github.com/fr3ts0n/AndrOBD/files/7206454/Mode09PID00_ByteMisinterpretation.txt)

title: No Fault Codes, no Vehicle Info, but there is OBD Data
body:
**Describe the bug**
 
After successfully pairing with OBD BT device, ECU detected, and connection is confirmed.  Only one screen is displaying data, that's _OBD Data_ screen.
 
On that screen you can see "Number of Fault Codes" is listed as zero.  However, the SRS light is staying on, on the instrument panel. 
 
I know live data is being read as I can see changes in values for some sensors.  As well, the _OBD fault codes_ screen is showing as "Connected"
 
Without the engine running but ignition On, I've tried the "Clear fault codes" feature anyway, but the SRS light never does turn off.  It seems the attempt to clear fault codes is processing, although never does confirm, as the car is running a little more rough the next time I start it.
 
I have tried a few different setting changes within AndrOBD but none of the change has improved the results  e.g. Secure connection, Preferred OBD protocol, ELM adaptive timing, Use last settings, Keep main screen on

title: When moving backwards is disabled and drafts are enabled, resuming at saved question fails
body:
#### ODK Collect version
2024.2.1 (from Play Store)

#### Device used
emulated Pixel 3 XL (Android 13), Samsung Galaxy A13 (Android 14)

#### Problem description
When moving backwards is disabled and drafts are enabled, resuming at saved question fails and the summary screen is opened instead. This means the user can navigate back to previous questions.

On both devices I've tried, I get an exception like 
```
java.io.FileNotFoundException: /storage/emulated/0/Android/data/org.odk.collect.android/files/projects/DEMO/.cache/All question types_2024-08-15_11-40-55.xml.index: open failed: ENOENT (No such file or directory)
```

I can verify with `adb shell` that the file exists.

#### Steps to reproduce the problem
1. Disallow moving backwards and don't have Collect change other related settings.
2. Fill out a few questions, exit the form
3. Reopen the form
4. See the hierarchy view

#### Expected behavior
Instead of the hierarchy view, Collect should resume at the last question visited.

#### Other information 

Could this be some kind of permission issue?

@getodk/testers it would be helpful to see whether it reproduces on any of your devices. It's not urgent.

title: Images are cached incorrectly, probably by glide
body:
#### ODK Collect version
https://github.com/getodk/collect/commit/66e9c1f10f707b803654bc57f7930b805edf2742 but I believe this affects all recent versions

#### Android version
5.1

#### Device used
Moto G5

#### Problem description
When media is updated for a Central draft, that update is not reflected on Collect.

#### Steps to reproduce the problem
1. Start a Central draft for a form with attached media such as All Widgets.
2. scan in the draft QR code to Collect
3. change one of the media files in Central
4. Refresh the form in Collect

#### Expected behavior
The new media file should show in Collect.

#### Other information 
Things I've verified:
* Central changes the md5 hash
* Central changes the etag
* Collect does download the new file and updates the "updated at" timestamp for the form
* The media file is correctly written to storage! I verified this through adb and the files app. Yes, this means that the file that is being used by Collect does not seem to exist in storage.
* The problem persists even after killing and reopening Collect 
* If I delete the image from storage, Collect shows a missing image error. Then if I download the form update, the expected (new) image is saved to disk but the unexpected (old) image is still shown when filling a form!
* If I delete the blank form from the UI and download it again, the unexpected (old) image is still shown
* If I delete the project and create a new one from the same QR code, I get the expected (new) image
* If I scan in the QR code again and create a duplicate project, the duplicate project shows the expected (new) image. The original project still shows the old image

https://forum.getodk.org/t/select-one-multiple-with-images-for-choices-big-image-choice-filtering-labels-images-not-shown-enketo-images-repeated-collect/40655/6?u=ln

I suspect this is related to glide disk caching: https://github.com/bumptech/glide/issues/624#issuecomment-140117332, https://github.com/bumptech/glide/wiki/Caching-and-Cache-Invalidation

I found this issue during testing PR from Central: https://github.com/getodk/central/issues/746, but it is probably not related with changes in Central, because I checked also the Test instance where this PR has not been introduced yet and I was able to reproduce the issue. 

When I have 2 or more projects in Collect and there is a form in one of them, which causes an error when sending, when Collect is trying to resend this form, I suddenly see the number of forms in “Draft”, “Ready to send” and “Sent” tabs from other project. Check the video below if something is unclear.

**Steps to reproduce the problem**

1. Scan the first QR code:
![Screenshot(206)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1231a043-53fe-45e5-b0e1-875ac75f0185)
2. Scan the second QR code:
![Screenshot(207)](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1796b8c5-3aa7-441d-be7c-ddcc1e96824e)

3. In the first project create some Drafts, send some forms and the most important send the: "random" form (there should be an error while sending)
4. Move to the second project and wait for match exactly/resend from the first project
5. You'll see the number of forms from the first project.
6. After going into e.g. Draft tab the forms will be shown correctly


**Expected behavior**

- User should not see number of forms from other projects after resending forms with issues or match exactly. 

**Other information**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3d58336b-cf67-4a3d-a967-782eb24b70ee

title: Dates saved to offline Entity properties have the wrong format when used in XPath expressions
body:
#### ODK Collect version
v2024.1.0 beta 8

#### Problem description
When an offline update is made to an Entity and this update represents a date, the date is converted to DD/MM/YY HH:MM format instead of remaining in ISO 8601 format. This means that any expressions trying to use that date crashes. This is a local-only issue: once the Entity is updated on the server and synced back to the device, everything works as expected.

#### Steps to reproduce the problem
1. Create a form that saves a date to an Entity property and then later uses that date such as [this one](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1_VsrftvmLgvuldUFtz2C1uxuUVSrMKRK8pKdZBZvNHw/edit?gid=1068911091#gid=1068911091)
2. Make sure that there is the `offline` column on the entities sheet and that its value is set to `yes`
3. Fill out and finalize a submission
4. Open the form again and see the crash
5. Open the Entities browser and the property has been incorrectly re-formated
![Screenshot_20241114_154559](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1dc4f34a-ecc7-44b3-82e4-227b13424b2d)

#### Expected behavior
Entity properties should always be treated as opaque strings. They should be saved and retrieved exactly as entered into the form.

#### Other information 

Something along the way is detecting a date and reformatting it. I don't think it's sqlite because I'm pretty sure sqlite stores dates in iso8601 format.

title: Don't require a label for Entity updates
body:
#### ODK Collect version
v2024.3.0 beta 8

#### Problem description
Entity updates without an explicit label fail silently.

#### Steps to reproduce the problem
1. Remove the value for the `label` Entity property for a form that updates Entities such as [this one](https://github.com/getodk/collect/issues/6453)
2. Make sure that there is the `offline` column on the entities sheet and that its value is set to `yes`
3. Fill out and finalize a submission
4. See that the local Entity update was not applied

#### Expected behavior
Labels should be optional for Entity updates. Just like with other properties, if no value is specified in the update, the existing value should be carried forward. Label is required at creation time so there should always be one at update time.

#### Other information 

I think this likely came from a misunderstanding around https://github.com/getodk/collect/issues/6453 Labels are required for Entity creation but optional for updates.

title: Constraint violation message is not dismissed when value from external app changes
body:
#### ODK Collect version
v2024.2.0

#### Android version
Android 14

#### Device used
Galaxy A13

#### Problem description
Using an external app question, when I get a different value from the external app, I may still see a stale error message that does not apply to the new value.

#### Steps to reproduce the problem
Use a form with an external app call and a constraint on it such as [this one](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1XCPbItGnIxBDuQgAT4uhC5sYkd2cxiYDq3EpjIBhBu0/edit?gid=0#gid=0)
Violate the constraint, see that a message is shown as expected
Launch the app again, see that the message is still shown

#### Expected behavior
The error message is dismissed when the constraint condition is met

#### Other information 

I did some initial debugging and noticed that `ExStringWidget.hideError` overrides `QuestionWidget.hideError` without calling `super`. I added the `super` call and I believe that results in the currently-expected behavior: the error message is dismissed whenever the value changes.

~My original expectation was that values from external apps (+ barcode, etc) would be validated on return to Collect. I guess that would be different from other question types so maybe we don't want that? I also expected that fields in field lists would get their validation messages updated dynamically but it looks like it's only done on swipe, right?~ We can split this out into a separate issue if we eventually want but it's probably not that important.

title: QuickNote content synchronization issue: Text is not cleared after deleting QuickNote.md from the file list.
body:
### ⚠️ This issue respects the following points: ⚠️

- [x] This is a **bug**. Not a [question or feature request](https://github.com/gsantner/markor/issues/new/choose).
- [x] The topic is **not** already reported at [Issues](https://github.com/gsantner/markor/issues?q=#js-issues-search). _(I've searched it)_.
- [x] Markor **is** up to date. See [Releases](https://github.com/gsantner/markor/tags) for the latest version. Updates are available from [F-Droid](https://f-droid.org/en/packages/net.gsantner.markor/) and GitHub.
- [x] The bug is still present in the latest development version (git master). (Please [download](https://nightly.link/gsantner/markor/workflows/build-android-project/master) and try the test version of Markor, named **Marder**. Don't worry; Markor and Marder appear as completely separate applications. You can install both side-by-side, and Markor settings are not touched. In case the issue is resolved there, you don't need to create a bug report. The change will be part of the next Markor update.)

### Description

The Bug: When QuickNote.md is deleted directly from the home screen (file list), the content remains visible when navigating to the "QuickNote" page via the bottom/side navigation bar. The UI does not stay in sync with the filesystem changes.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4a530d9-ae21-40e1-9de6-790dcd10fe94

Correct Behavior: Deleting the QuickNote.md file should trigger a refresh or clear the internal cache so that the QuickNote editor appears empty (or reverts to its default state).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0de667f4-e904-49e7-9953-64ec72ae8aa3

Observation: Interestingly, if the user opens the QuickNote.md editor at least once before deleting the file from the home list, the synchronization works correctly, and the text is removed. This suggests the issue is related to how the editor fragment initializes or observes file changes when it hasn't been "activated" in the current session.

### Steps to reproduce

**Initialize a QuickNote.md file  with some text content.**

### Scenario A (Triggering the Bug):

1. Turn to the Main/Home file list.

2. Long-press QuickNote.md and select Delete.

3. Click the QuickNote icon in the navigation bar.

4. Result: The old text content is still present in the editor.

### Scenario B (Normal/Correct Sync):

1. Turn to the Main/Home file list.

2. Open the QuickNote.md editor first, then go back to the list.

3. Perform the deletion.

4. Click the QuickNote navigation icon.

5. Result: The content is successfully cleared/synced.

### Information

Android version:  14 (SDK 34)
Device: HONOR | HONOR | HNFNE | FNE-AN00 | FNE-AN00 | qcom 
App Version: v2.15.2

GitHub Releases

title: Bug: The navigation button disappears if repositioned and on a device without navbar (with hardware buttons)
body:
Steps to reproduce:

0. Use a device without navbar/soft-touch buttons (i.e. hardware buttons for back, home and change app. If I go to the Android settings and enable the soft-touch buttons this bug stops immediatly, I don't even need to restart the app)
1. Activate on-screen navigation for materialistic
2. Re-position the navigation button (otherwise it works perfectly, but I hate the default position because scrolling down makes me hit the home button quite often.)
3. Open a random story's comments, go back to stories list, repeat multiple times (or different stories, doesn't matter). There is no discernible pattern but sometimes the navigation button will appear and sometimes it won't. After about 3 to 5 attempts it will almost certainly have disappeared at least once already.

Devices I have tested already:
- A BQ M5 Marshmallow phone with hardware buttons that allows me to disable or enable soft buttons
- An older KitKat phone with hardware buttons, but that does not allow me to enable the navbar
- A Nexus 7 with Marshmallow which has no hardware buttons and so does not let me disable the navbar
- A Jelly Bean phone which has no hardware buttons and so does not let me disable the navbar

With any of those four devices, if there is a navbar the bug does not happen, if there is no navbar, the bug does happen.

If you want any help tracking down this bug please let me know, I love the application and this seems like a very interesting bug, I have no idea what it could be :p

title: Unuseable On Android 10, sql error -> un
body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Have Android 10 installed with latest (Nov) patchlevel
2. Start app
4. See error - multiple popups with the following error message

`Invalid SQL - use default instead 
SQL-error 'Invalid column max(width, height) as col_width' 

SELECT _id._data AS disp_text, max(width, height) AS col_width, longitude, datetaken, _data, orientation
FROM content://media/external/file
WHERE (( media_type=1)) `

The app has to be killed with the app switcher. There is no log written. 


**Expected behavior**
Start of App showing map with photos. 

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Android version [e.g. Android-7.1] : 10
 - [A Photo Manager Version](https://github.com/k3b/APhotoManager/releases) (i.e. 0.4.6.160304) : latest Version from fdroid

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

**Crash Report**
If you report an app crash: Can you add the crash logfile to this ticket?

When APhotoManager crashes it tries to write a crash log file in the [**Error Log Folder**](https://github.com/k3b/APhotoManager/wiki/settings#logfolder).

* [ExternalStorageDirectory]/copy/log/androFotofinder.logcat-2017....txt
  * i.e. /storage/sdcard0/copy/log/androFotofinder.logcat-20170728-135704.txt
    * crash data from 2017-07-28 13:57
    
For more details on Error Loging see [diagnostic settings](https://github.com/k3b/APhotoManager/wiki/settings#Diagnostics).

-------
[Update 2020-04-13]

**Technical Problem**

Android-10 (api29) prevents apps from using sql-functions on media-content-provider colums where APhotoManager heavily depends on.

To achive android-10 compatibility i have seperated all database access logic into a seperate layer where there are two implementations for:

* A Contentprovider-implementation for android-9 and below 
** is used since APhotoManager-0.8.1
* A database implementation that works with a data clone of media-contentprovider
** will be used in android-10 and up, when finished
** done: code to one-time copy all data from media-contentprovider to database
** done: code to query database
** done: datachanges from within APhotoManager are written to media-contentprovider and to database
** open todo: when changes are done to media-contentprovider outside APhotoManager (i.e. when camera shoots a photo) the changes must be transfered to database, too

Currently work on this ticket is stopped, because i work on sd-card-support #169 to make APhotoManager run on my new android-9 device.

[Update k3b 20210122]

since android-10 it is not allowed to query the media database  for latitude/longitude any more ( https://developer.android.com/reference/android/provider/MediaStore.Images.ImageColumns#LATITUDE ) :-(

As consequence APhotoManager needs 

* its own local copy of the media database
* a full media rescan (or the import of the gps data through a csv file).

title: AsyncTask rembering owning Activity may cause memory leak
body:
According to our research, there are misuses about the following two AsyncTask classes:
1. `de.k3b.android.widget.AsyncTaskWithProgressDialog`
2. `de.k3b.android.androFotoFinder.directory.DirectoryLoaderTask`

The problems are:
1. They hold strong reference to the GUI element of Activity, which can lead to memory leak when the Activity is destroyed and the AsyncTask did not finish
2. Their instances are not cancelled before the Activity is destroyed, which can lead to the wrong invocation of onPostExecute

I think we can make following changes to fix the misuse problems:
1. I think the GUI-related fields should be wrapped into WeakReference. Take `protected Activity parent` as example, it can be changed tp `private final WeakReference<Activity> parent`.
2. Add a AsyncTask field in the corresponding Activities which use AsyncTask. These fields refer to the annoymous AsyncTask objects. Then invoke `cancel()` in the onDestroy() method of Activities.

These are my suggestions above, thank you.

title: On recent android versions processed photos may be removed after app uninstall
body:
In https://developer.android.com/training/camera/photobasics

i read

	> Note: Files you save in the directories provided by 
	> `getExternalFilesDir()` or `getFilesDir()` are deleted 
	> when the user uninstalls your app.

Solution: from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/9726990/keep-files-after-uninstallation-of-android-app

You can put files in a directory derived from Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory(). These files will persist after an uninstall. However, a user can delete those files any time they like, regardless of whether your app is installed or not.

Other than that, there is no place on the device that you can place files that will survive an uninstall.
-----

see also

* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/56468539/getexternalstoragepublicdirectory-deprecated-in-android-q
* https://commonsware.com/blog/2019/04/22/death-external-storage-more-story.html
* https://stackoverflow.com/questions/54516769/how-to-write-files-to-sdcard-that-will-not-be-deleted-if-app-is-uninstalled

Report on notification issues shows that a number of notification failures potentially related to syncing app state with push notification state, which could be tied to the same issue. A delay between the server and local state could be related to inconsistent states. 

Could be related to [this v16.1 issue](https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10713), though we'd have to investigate if the inclusion of the message list widget changes the problem. Could also be due to multiple processes attempting to load remote notifications, creating inconsistent states.  One user reported that the issue was not reliant on the message list:

title: Potential main-thread blocking issue in KeyChainKeyManager
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [x] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App

Thunderbird for Android

### App version

Trunk / `main`

---

Hi Thunderbird for Android Team,

I’m a PhD student researching Android performance issues. My research group recently ran a static analysis scan for thread-affinity and main-thread blocking bugs, and our prototype flagged a potential issue in Thunderbird for Android / K-9 Mail.

## Checked source-level target

I checked the latest upstream source and found one app-owned source path in:

```text
legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/KeyChainKeyManager.java
```

Verified commit:

```text
416859a7f080db8ff0be7038a2aa43ed95f23dfc
```

The relevant constructor synchronously retrieves both the certificate chain and private key from Android `KeyChain`:

```java
63:  public KeyChainKeyManager(Context context, String alias) throws MessagingException {
64:      mAlias = alias;
65:      try {
66:          mChain = fetchCertificateChain(context, alias);
67:          mPrivateKey = fetchPrivateKey(context, alias);
68:      } catch (KeyChainException | InterruptedException e) {
69:          throw new ClientCertificateException(ClientCertificateError.RetrievalFailure, e);
70:      }
71:  }
```

The helper methods then reach the sensitive Android APIs:

```java
fetchCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getCertificateChain(context, alias)

fetchPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getPrivateKey(context, alias)
```

## What I found

`KeyChainKeyManager` performs Android KeyChain credential retrieval synchronously during construction.

This is risky if the constructor is ever reached from a UI-thread path, because Android documents both of the underlying platform APIs as potentially blocking operations that must not be called from the main thread:

```java
KeyChain.getCertificateChain(Context, String)
KeyChain.getPrivateKey(Context, String)
```

These calls can block while Android waits for a connection to another process, resolves credential access, or performs KeyChain-related work. The Android documentation also states that these methods may take several seconds to complete and should only be called from a worker thread.

I did not see an asynchronous boundary inside `KeyChainKeyManager` itself. Therefore, the safety of this code currently depends on every caller constructing `KeyChainKeyManager` only from a background thread.

## Verified source-level traces

```text
Trace 1: Certificate-chain retrieval

<com.fsck.k9.helper.KeyChainKeyManager: void <init>(android.content.Context, java.lang.String)>
  -> fetchCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> Android platform KeyChain service
  -> documented as blocking / worker-thread-only
  -> possible UI freeze, StrictMode violation, or IllegalStateException if reached from the main thread
```

```text
Trace 2: Private-key retrieval

<com.fsck.k9.helper.KeyChainKeyManager: void <init>(android.content.Context, java.lang.String)>
  -> fetchPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> Android platform KeyChain service
  -> documented as blocking / worker-thread-only
  -> possible UI freeze, StrictMode violation, or IllegalStateException if reached from the main thread
```

## Why this matters

Client-certificate retrieval is usually part of account setup, mail transport setup, or TLS/client-authentication initialization. If this path is triggered from an Activity, Fragment, setup wizard, account settings screen, or any other UI-facing flow, the app may temporarily freeze while waiting for Android KeyChain.

On slower devices, locked/slow credential backends, or devices with unusual certificate providers, this could become visible jank or a delayed UI response. On Android versions that enforce the documented restriction, calling these APIs from the main thread may also throw `IllegalStateException`.

I am reporting this as a source-level main-thread blocking risk because I verified the current source pattern, but I have not yet produced an on-device runtime reproduction.

## Possible fix

The safest fix is to ensure that `KeyChainKeyManager` is only constructed from a worker thread, or to make the KeyChain retrieval path explicitly asynchronous.

For example, the credential retrieval could be moved behind an executor or existing background mail/account setup pipeline:

```java
ExecutorService executor = Executors.newSingleThreadExecutor();
Context appContext = context.getApplicationContext();

executor.execute(() -> {
    try {
        KeyChainKeyManager keyManager =
                new KeyChainKeyManager(appContext, alias);

        // Continue TLS / mail setup on the background path,
        // or post only the final UI result back to the main thread if needed.

    } catch (MessagingException e) {
        // Handle retrieval failure.
        // If UI feedback is needed, post that part back to the main thread.
    }
});
```

Another option is to document and enforce the threading contract at the call boundary, for example by asserting that `KeyChainKeyManager` is never constructed on the main thread and by moving all callsites into existing background setup code.

One additional nuance: the Android documentation recommends using a long-lived context such as `getApplicationContext()` for these KeyChain calls, because Activity and Service contexts can be short-lived.

## References

- Android platform API: `android.security.KeyChain#getCertificateChain(Context, String)`
- Android platform API: `android.security.KeyChain#getPrivateKey(Context, String)`

- Link: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/security/KeyChain#getCertificateChain(android.content.Context,%20java.lang.String)

Relevant Android documentation wording:

> This method may block while waiting for a connection to another process, and must never be called from the main thread.

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.

title: Importing settings fails to add avatar monogram
body:
When importing older settings like the QR code (version 99), the settings migration 104 should add the avatar monogram based on the user name or email. This fails as the `name` and `email` settings are not accessible during the settings upgrade.

The issue is caused by using the wrong type of migration that just migrates account settings but not account information like name, email, identities etc.

To fix the issue we need to use the `CombinedSettingsUpgrader` instead.

This is the source of users importing settings from Desktop or from previously exported settings will end up with a `XX` avatar monogram.

title: Various issues in attachments since v18: images no longer visible, bad UX
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [x] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App

Thunderbird for Android

### App version

18

### Where did you get the app from?

None

### Android version

15

### Device model

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

Open a message with attachements

### Expected behavior

Previously you could see the list of attachments at the end of the message body.

This was not the best place but you could preview images, and click on each image one by one. A single click was required to open an attachment.

### Actual behavior

Now the attachment UI is difficult to use: you need to click on a very small "View all" button (which doesn't look like a button at all, and is way too small, I struggle to click it with my finger), clicking on the "4 attachments" text does nothing.

This opens a menu at the bottom of the screen (meaning you need to move your finger).

There is no preview of images, so when the file name is a UUID, you don't know what the image is.

If you click on an attachment name in that menu, the menu disappears, meaning you have to re-click the small "view all" button which reopens the attachments menu. Now you don't know where you were when you have 20 attachments with similar file names.

It has become very hard to view attachments now because of that "redesign".

Good UX principles should always be followed:

* text buttons should look like buttons, not like text
* click zones should be large enough
* the interface should not "jump" off when you open an attachment
* image attachments should have a largish preview

The new UX is a regression and should have been tested before deployment. I understand that having attachments at the end of the body is not the best (as you have to scroll), but the new UI is worse in every aspect.

### Logs

_No response_

title: ExtraTranslation error
body:
I get some ExtraTranslation lint errors. Please either fix them or disable them with  `android { lintOptions { disable 'ExtraTranslation' } }` in your build.gradle:

```
   Explanation for issues of type "ExtraTranslation":
   If a string appears in a specific language translation file, but there is
   no corresponding string in the default locale, then this string is probably
   unused. (It's technically possible that your application is only intended
   to run in a specific locale, but it's still a good idea to provide a
   fallback.).

   Note that these strings can lead to crashes if the string is looked up on
   any locale not providing a translation, so it's important to clean them
   up.
```

title: Rewrite to use content URI functionality
body:
A really great improvement since Android Q (or P, not sure) is that Google added a file manager.
I suspect that they found out that handling files on Android is not even that bad so they decided to make it a real pain. 
To solve this, this app has to be rewritten to use the annoying `content://`/`DocumentContract` wrapper around the File API instead of dealing with them directly.

body:
**Describe the bug**
It seems like the `SettingsActivity` doesn't set the GUI elements according to current preference state.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to any folder containing an archive
2. Click on the menu button next to the file
3. Click on extract
4. Select "Yes, always"
5. Go to Settings
6. Scroll down to "Always extract in current dir"
7. It will be `false` while archives are being extracted in the current dir.

**Expected behavior**
Setting should show it's actual state.

title: Keep current folder and operation context when switching apps
body:
**Describe the bug**
When switching to another app and returning, or when going to the homescreen the app resets the file operation context and current folder (ie. does a "reboot").

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open lrkFM
2. Add file to operation context (ie. copy but don't paste)
3. Switch to another app
4. Switch back to lrkFM
5. The app will be reset

**Expected behavior**
The app should "remember" the last current folder and op context.

title: Potential main-thread blocking issue in LibreTube shortcut update flow
body:
Hi LibreTube Team,

I’m a PhD student researching Android performance issues. My research group recently ran a static analysis scan for thread affinity and main-thread blocking bugs, and our prototype flagged a potential issue in LibreTube.

This could be similar to https://github.com/bitfireAT/davx5-ose/issues/2194.

## Checked target

+ Source-level caller: `com.github.libretube.LibreTubeApp.onCreate() -> ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(Context)`
+ Detected APIs: `ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(...)` and `ShortcutManagerCompat.setDynamicShortcuts(...)`
+ Underlying platform APIs: `ShortcutManager#getDynamicShortcuts()` and `ShortcutManager#setDynamicShortcuts(List<ShortcutInfo>)`
+ Observed context: app startup / `Application.onCreate()` main thread
+ Expected context: worker/background thread

## What I found

The current `LibreTubeApp.onCreate()` initializes dynamic app shortcuts during application startup:

```kotlin
class LibreTubeApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        ...

        /**
         * Dynamically create App Shortcuts
         */
        ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this)

        NewPipeExtractorInstance.init()
    }
}
```

`ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(...)` then checks existing dynamic shortcuts and publishes the app shortcuts synchronously:

```kotlin
object ShortcutHelper {
    fun createShortcuts(context: Context) {
        if (ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(context).isEmpty()) {
            val dynamicShortcuts = TopLevelDestination.entries.map { createShortcut(context, it) }
            ShortcutManagerCompat.setDynamicShortcuts(context, dynamicShortcuts)
        }
    }
}
```

The concern is that the underlying Android platform APIs for retrieving and publishing dynamic shortcuts are documented as potentially taking several seconds and should only be called from a worker thread. Since this code is called directly from `Application.onCreate()`, it can run on the main thread during cold start.

## Verified bug trace

```text
App process starts
  -> LibreTubeApp.onCreate()
  -> ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this)
  -> ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(context)
  -> if no dynamic shortcuts exist:
       ShortcutManagerCompat.setDynamicShortcuts(context, dynamicShortcuts)
  -> underlying Android ShortcutManager query/publish operation
  -> Android documentation marks these operations as potentially slow and worker-thread-only
  -> possible cold-start delay / startup jank / ANR risk
```

I did not dynamically reproduce an ANR or crash, so this should be treated as a source-confirmed main-thread blocking risk rather than a reproduced runtime failure. The relevant call pattern is still present in the current source.

## Why this matters

This path runs during application startup. Shortcut queries and publishing go through Android system services and may be slower on some devices, launchers, user states, or Android versions. Running them on the main thread can delay startup and make the app feel unresponsive.

## Possible fix

Move the shortcut initialization to a worker context. For example, if using coroutines:

```kotlin
class LibreTubeApp : Application() {
    private val appScope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.Default)

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        ...

        appScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this@LibreTubeApp)
        }
    }
}
```

Alternatively, use a small executor dedicated to startup background work. The important point is that `getDynamicShortcuts(...)` and `setDynamicShortcuts(...)` should not run synchronously on the main startup path.

## Reference

Android API: `android.content.pm.ShortcutManager`

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/pm/ShortcutManager

Relevant Android documentation wording for the affected shortcut APIs:

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.


## Source references

+ Current app startup source: https://github.com/libre-tube/LibreTube/blob/master/app/src/main/java/com/github/libretube/LibreTubeApp.kt
+ Current shortcut helper source: https://github.com/libre-tube/LibreTube/blob/master/app/src/main/java/com/github/libretube/helpers/ShortcutHelper.kt


### LibreTube version

trunk

title: Incorrect equals() implementation in BaseItem
body:
## About this issue

Incorrect `equals()` implementation in `BaseItem`.

Here's how the implementation looks like
```
override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
    if (this === other) return true
    if (other == null || javaClass != other.javaClass) return false
    val that = other as? AbstractItem<*>?  
    return identifier == that?.identifier
}
```

The line `val that = other as? AbstractItem<*>?` should be replaced with `val that = other as? BaseItem<*>?`, as not all subclasses of `BaseItem` extend `AbstractItem`.

As an example `AbstractBindingItem` extends `BaseItem` directly, thus the equals() fails on it.

## Details
- [5.1.0] Used library version

## Checklist

- [x] Searched for [similar issues](https://github.com/mikepenz/FastAdapter/issues)

title: Drawables with ratio different than 1:1 (swipe)
body:
https://github.com/mikepenz/FastAdapter/blob/494df3d7aac31e5d34836b77a5ac8adb1ba4da1b/library-extensions-swipe/src/main/java/com/mikepenz/fastadapter/swipe/SimpleSwipeCallback.kt#L125

this line should be:

`val intrinsicHeight = drawable.intrinsicHeight `

otherwise canvas drawing would be stretched for lets say drawables with ratio 3:1.

title: Paged list - selectExtension.getSelectedItems() loads _all_ the placeholders
body:
## About this issue

I'm using FastAdapter with `PagedModelAdapter` to display a large list of books.

__Repro steps__
1. Initialize a `PagedList` on a library with 70 books, using `PagedList.Config.Builder` and 
- `enablePlaceholders = true`
- `initialLoadSizeHint = 40`
- `pageSize = 20`

2. View the first 10 items on a `RecyclerView`

3. Call `selectExtension.getSelectedItems()`


__Expected behaviour__
The selected items are returned; the DAO is never called
Only the first 40 items are loaded in the `PagedList`; the 30 others still are placeholders

__Actual behaviour__
As soon as `selectExtension.getSelectedItems()` is called, the DAO loads books 41 to 60 and 61 to 70.
All 70 items are loaded in the `PagedList`; no placeholder is left even though the `RecyclerView` has never gone beyond position 10.

__Note__
Loading all placeholders has both a performance impact and super weird side effects on my app.

If the current behaviour works as designed, could you at least add an option to _avoid_ loading placeholders when collecting selected items ? Thanks a bunch !

## Details
- [x] Used library version v4.1.2
- [x] Used support library version v1.0.0 (AndroidX)
- [x] Used gradle build tools version v3.5.3
- [x] Used tooling / Android Studio version AS v3.5.3
- [x] Other used libraries, potential conflicting libraries None that I know of

title: Crash with PagedModelAdapter
body:
## About this issue

I'm using `PagedModelAdapter` to handle the display of a book library. I'm fetching data using `LivePagedListBuilder` with placeholders enabled.

When working on large libraries (100+ books), I noticed that, when changing the ordering of the list (e.g. A->Z to Z->A, which implies everything on screen gets instantly recycled to display the other end of the list) :
 - `PagedItemListImpl` crashes very often after calling `PagedModelAdapter.submitList` (see stacktrace below)
 - While debugging, the position where the crash happens has no obvious issue (i.e. not out of bounds by any means)
 - The crash is completely avoided when disabling placeholders in `LivePagedListBuilder`

title: Mixing expandable and non expandable items
body:
## About this issue
When I mix some expandable items with simple items, I got a crash due to predicate trying to cast my non-expandable item to `ISubItem` (code below).
https://github.com/mikepenz/FastAdapter/blob/b716ba353855ed8f2ad9cb8d3ee43637c33236ad/library-extensions-expandable/src/main/java/com/mikepenz/fastadapter/expandable/ExpandableExtension.kt#L76-L83
I think it can be avoid just by checking the cast and let the null check breaks the recursive apply.

Or maybe I miss something?
