title: ClassCastException with DefaultItemAnimator and adapter.set()
body:
## About this issue

- Briefly describe the issue

Setting an adapter repeatedly during a search with 3 types of classes of that implement IItem identical to RealmSampleUserItem, crashes the app due to animations in the DefaultItemAnimator (Please check the stack trace below).

Based on the following solution that i've found, I cannot resolve the issue that is directly caused from within the FastAdapter library. What happens is that the adapter is set again when an animation is in progress, and the onAnimationFinished callback in the RecyclerView class notifies the FastAdapter of this for a specific item index, but the item at that index is now of another IItem class. 
Setting `recyclerView.setItemAnimator(null);` resolves the issue.

A possible resolution would be to end all animations in the recyclerview when calling `adapter.set()`, but calling `recyclerView.clearAnimation();` before setting the new list does not resolve the issue.

With this ticket I hope to get more information and possibly other alternatives how to resolve the issue, or if this can be resolved from within the FastAdapter library.

- How can the issue be reproduced / sample code

1. Create 3 classes similar to RealmSampleUserItem
2. Set up a recyclerview with fast item adapter
3. Implement a search bar that will filter the items
4. Create a list of items (mixed classes) from step 1
5. Run the app and search.

Please note, when i search, i use `adapter.clear()` and then `adapter.set(filteredList)` instead of the adapter's filter function, because of filtering mechanics other than a string search.
Also, Before modifying the adapter or recyclerview, i am using `recyclerView.setLayoutFrozen(true/false)`. 

Crash log should show as follows:
[Open logcat log](https://pastebin.com/raw/07BpAxG9)


## Details
- [x] Used library version 3.2.5
- [x] Used support library version 27.1.1
- [x] Used gradle build tools version 3.1.3
- [x] Used tooling / Android Studio version (MacOS) Android Studio 3.1.3 #AI-173.4819257
- [x] Other used libraries, potential conflicting libraries (mike, I will PM you if you need this list)

title: Automatic determination of plot-boundaries fails for negative interval endpoints
body: ## Description of issue When leaving the axis endpoints blank for the boundaries of a plot to be automatically determined from the input interval(s) and function range, the plot will be filled with `NaN` if any endpoint is negative. This is the case for both function plots and 3D plots. ## Steps to reproduce Attached [EndpointBug.mmt.html](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24501466/EndpointBug.mmt.html) is a worksheet with problematic plot. (Remove `.html` from extension. Was added for GitHub upload compatibility.) Otherwise... 1. Create an interval with a negative boundary. 2. Create a function. 3. Attempt to plot the function applied to that interval, leaving the axis endpoints blank. ## Expected behaviour Endpoints are automatically determined and the plot should work. ## Actual behaviour The automatically determined endpoints 'look' like correct values, but the plot is malformed and contains no useful data or rendering. ## Screenshots ## Environment information - **OS:** Graphene OS release 2025122500, Android 16 Security update Dec 5, 2025 - **Software version:** microMathematics-v2.23.2-release.apk via Obtainium ## Additional context I read on another issue that there are two different kinds of minus symbols in the system. It appears to me from the length of the character in the automatic boundaries that the wrong symbol is being used, causing a malformed input to be produced.

title: Interval defined from another interval gives inconsistent plotting behaviour
body: ## Description of issue In a 3D plot using two intervals as inputs to the function to be graphed, if one of the intervals is defined using the first, the plot will not be rendered as expected. This as compared to the rendering of the same function of the same mathematical intervals where each interval is defined independently. ## Steps to reproduce Attached [IntervalBug.mmt.html](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24501894/IntervalBug.mmt.html) is a worksheet with problematic plot. (Remove `.html` from extension. Was added for GitHub upload compatibility.) Otherwise... 1. Create an interval. 2. Create another interval dependent on the first. 3. Create a function of two variables. 4. Plot the function applied to the two intervals in a 3D plot. ## Expected behaviour Contours or surface is displayed over the whole region defined by the two intervals. ## Actual behaviour Only the section of the surface or contours along the line `y=x` is rendered, regardless of the values in the second interval. ## Screenshots ## Environment information - **OS:** Graphene OS release 2025122500, Android 16 Security update Dec 5, 2025 - **Software version:** microMathematics-v2.23.2-release.apk via Obtainium ## Additional context I read in another issue that parameterised-curve-like behaviour was added for when the same interval is supplied for each argument in the function to be plotted. The above bug appears to be an extension of that behaviour to unintended situations.

title: When a text fragment is selected manually and copied, a magnifier overlay still be visible
body: Observed on Android 16

title: Problem opening file from documents after installation
body:
How to reproduce:
- Delete the app
- Install the app again
- Run the app: welcome document is shown as expected
- Three-dot menu-> Open file -> Document -> Select a folder with file to be opened: Android storage permission dealog appears
- Confirm the folder selection: app file selection dialog re-appears
- Select a file to open
*Expected behaviour*: selected file is opened
*Observed behaviour*: welcome document still be shown, file content is loss

**Workaround**: after storage permission confirmation, when app file selection dialog appears again, press CANCEL and close the app.

**Analysis:**
- It seems to be the problem is introduced by #137 
- When storage permission are confrmed, a new app fragment is created since onResume is called after SAF fragment is closed. 
- However, the app file selection dialog that re-appears after storage permission confirmation still work with the previous deprecated fragment
- The file is than read into the invalid fragment, but opened file name is changed
- After any operations, the app (new fragment) stores welcome document using new file name that results the data lost in the original document

title: File content sometime is lost after file saving in Android emulator
The app content may disappear after rotating on an emulator where a big document is open
body:
Follow-up issue after #136:
After rotating an emulator where square_wave_oscillator example is open, the content disappears and app is not more usable.

Analysis: in the onCreate method, also check whether the input bundle is empty:
```
instanceStateEmpty = savedInstanceState == null || savedInstanceState.isEmpty();
```
instead of 
```
instanceStateEmpty = savedInstanceState == null;
```

Note: this problem is only observed on an Android 14 emulator, not on a real device.

title: No possibility to input a file name after the save menu is selected when the app is started at the first time
body: When the "File save" menu is selected for a newly created document, the file name selection dialog is opened. However, if the app is started at the first time and no storage permissions are yet granted, file name selection dialog does not appear.

title: App crashes when a document with huge embedded image is open and app settings are selected from app menu
body:
How to reproduce:
- Create a document with huge embedded image (image size greater that 300KB)
- Select "App settings" from the main menu
_Observed result_: app crashes with TransactionTooLargeException runtime exception

Analysis:
https://developer.android.com/guide/components/activities/parcelables-and-bundles
In Android 7.0 (API level 24) and higher, the system throws a TransactionTooLargeException
as a runtime exception. In lower versions of Android, the system only shows a warning in logcat.
Recommended state size: 50k or less.

After https://github.com/mkulesh/microMathematics/issues/68, the only one case where the instant state is collected is the transition to the app settings. However, the collected state is not used by the back transition to the main screen. 

Proposed solution: do not collect the state if it is greater that 50KB.

title: Scroll stuttering of huge documents on Android 13 and above
body:
Starting from Android 13, a scroll stuttering (laggy animations) of huge documents like release_notes or function_overview is observed. The reason is currently unknown.

Seems to be a common problem:

body:
## Steps to reproduce
1. Load wikipedia.org, or any other page.
2. Tap the toolbar, and type a different address, like cnn.com.

### Expected behavior
The page from step 2 loads replacing the page from step 1.

### Actual behavior
The page from step 1 is still displayed, on the top of the screen, leaving the bottom of the screen blank.

### Device information
* Android device: Xiaomi mi4i (Android 5.0.2)
* Focus version: 12/3 Focus Nightly
* Also reproducible on 12/3 Fenix Nightly
* Not reproducible on the GeckoView Example from 12/3
* Not reproducible on Samsung Galaxy Tab A6 (Android 5.1.1), or any other device

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40694158/144615489-900d9ced-b8e2-4ffa-8bf6-26b15b5d7d42.mp4

[logcat.txt](https://github.com/mozilla-mobile/focus-android/files/7650136/logcat.txt)

title: [L10N] "Block cross-site cookies" has a missing space in Dutch
body:
## Steps to reproduce
1. Set the device in Dutch.
2. Open Focus - Settings - Privacy & Security - Cookies and Site Data and observe the "Block cross-site cookies" text layout.

### Expected behavior
The words are separated by spaces.

### Actual behavior
The "cross-site cookies" has no space between them - please see the attached screenshot.

### Device information
* Android device: HTC 10 (Android 8)
* Focus version: RC 96.1.1
![cross site](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/40694158/149088678-cb0f8f23-fbe4-4d07-ab60-b1c4297741b5.png)

title: [Bug] Shopping search crash on first-time use with Redmi Note7
body:
**Describe the bug**
As title. Redmi Note 7 crash when using Shopping Search for the first time.

**Steps To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Firefox Lite
2. Click on Shopping Search Button on the homepage (bottom left corner)
3. Type anything to search
4. See the overlay onboarding message about Shopping Search preference

**Expected behavior**
See shopping search results in multiple sites.

**Actual behavior**
After seeing the overlay onboarding message the app crash.

**Device info (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Redmi Note 7
 - OS: Android 9 PKQ1.180904.001
 - MIUI version: 11.0.4
 - Version: 2.1.6 (18084)

**Reproduce rate**
10/10

title: New "edit times" screen harder to use than old screen
body:
**Describe the bug**
The new edit times screen which appears looks nice, but using it to actually edit times is a much greater pain than the irritants that were present on the old screen (the version with the round 'clock' time selector wheels).

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Long press a task with time values
2. Scroll the popup menu (if necessary) and select "show times"
3. Tap a time range

**Expected behavior**
An easy and fast UI to use to make changes to start/end time points for an event.

**Actual behavior**
The UI for selecting a time value has changed from a circular clock face to a side-by-side pair of vertically scrolling linear lists of time values.  This layout is significantly more time consuming to use to select a specific time value because of the amount of 'scroll' through the lengthy linear lists that is necessary (24 entries for the hours, 60 entries for minutes).  If one has, say, a value of 09:45 and needs to run back to 09:15, one has to scroll the minutes list across 30 numerals to get to the right value.

With the old clock layout, selecting an hours value and a minutes value was a direct access operation (sometimes with a little tweaking when the ui picked 16 instead of 15).

But the two UI's are like the difference between an O(1) lookup vs. an O(n) lookup algorithm.  The old clock face was O(1) access time.  The new UI is O(n) access time.

Please either bring back the old UI, or offer a setup option to switch to the old UI.  It was way superior from a time taken to make a change perspective than the new UI.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Phone: Moto E4
 - OS: Android 7.1.1

body:
Since the change of SDK level in version 0.50 it is not possible to restore a backup (at least not with Android 6).

It seems we now need to explicitly ask permission before accessing the external storage.

Same issue is likely also when trying to perform a backup

title: mislabled dialog (delete time)
body:
when you want to delete a time in time view inside a task, the are-you-sure-dialog is titled "Delete Task", which is confusing as you want to delete a time range (as stated correctly in the dialog text).
"Delete Time(range)" would be better.

title: Vibration is initially enabled, but preferences screen says it is disabled
body: Just after installing the app, or after wiping its data, if you press on a task to start or stop it, the device will vibrate. However, if you look in the preferences, it will say vibration is disabled.

title: Initial help reappears when rotating etc.
body:
Thanks for picking up this app from Sean Russel and working on it. I just discovered it via the What's New part of F-Droid and have some issues to report of my first impressions.

Regarding the initial help window that appears, I like the way that it appears whenever the app starts when there are no tasks listed. I think that is better than other apps where the help only appears on the actual first use. With your method if you run the app once but don't really use it, the help will appear again, until the user gets to the point of having added a task.

However, I think this method may have introduced a bug, which is that if the help is dismissed then the device is rotated, the help will reappear. It will also reappear if a sliding physical keyboard is slid open, or slid closed.

title: Export view to CSV success message states wrong filename (often, not always)
body:
Export view to CSV often says "Successfully exported CSV to memory card as timetracker.db" when in fact it exported it as a file named something like 2015-02-23.csv or 2015-02-23_0.csv.

I'm using version 0.19 of A Time Tracker, from F-Droid.org repository, on CyanogenMod-7.2.0-glacier (Android 2.3.7).

title: Call to static SimpleDateFormat
body:
Code smells:
- SimpleDateFormat is not thread safe
- Weird initialization of date format from one class to static field of an other and duplicated in two places

Instead:
- Move out the initialization from TimeRange and let the calling class handle that
- Remove the initialization of static fields
- For other cases: do not have static initialization of SimeDateFormat

title: [Bug] Space following . ! ? results in shift key being glitched (in Bengali layout)
body:
**Describe the bug**
Issue:
Pressing the shift key makes the upper case words show for a second & goes back to lower case immediately. (In Bengali layout only)

How to recreate:
Type '.' or '?' or '!' after a word, press space & then press shift.

What's causing it:
Auto-capitalization feature which capitalizes every first word of a sentence, since any sentence after . ! ? is a new sentence, the glitch is only visible when these characters are being used.

{Note: After the space, the keys are shown in lower case but the blue light on the shift key is visible; meaning it is actually on the upper case mode, then when we press shift intended to get upper case, the keyboard registers it as 'disable-shift' (since it's automatically on 'enable-shift'), even though the layout shows otherwise.}

Suggested solution:
Maybe force to turn this feature (auto capitalization) off for Bengali layout, so that it doesn't mess up the state of the keys & keep it on only for English layout

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 N/A, tested on multiple device vendors

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Strange, unpredictable backspace behavior; causes words to merge
body:
I notice this occurring at random most often in the Proton email client/app where I type most frequently, though I believe it occurs in various other text fields.
I'll have to give it more attention to identify specific locations and any behavioral patterns within specific environmental contexts. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

Perhaps investigate with a Pixel with GrapheneOS and without.
1. Go to Proton email
2. Submit text into a text field such as a new email or a reply email
3. Begin to backspace each letter of each word
4. Notice the space before the current word being edited will be removed before the word is entirely deleted, thus causing the current partial-word to merge into the previous word.
5. Continue to backspace letter by letter to observe recurrences at each word.


**Screenshots**
Screen recording provided below.

**Smartphone
 - Device: Pixel 7 Pro
 - OS: Graphene
 - Android Version: 14 and prior with 13


**Additional context**
* OpenBoard app version is up to date as current.

* Issue has been ongoing since at least Android 13 and still into 14.

* Issue may be particular to specific apps, web apps, websites, web portals. Issue is not ubiquitous everywhere, only specific or else random applications. I'll update this with any observable patterns or commonalities pertaining to usage sites in case that proves relevant.

* I suspect this as a compatibility issue involving Graphene in combination with incompatible/discriminative/underdeveloped apps/web portals, but that's just a guess.

* Issue may/may not reproduce according to content size within a text field, such as amidst 1000+ words or so, maybe not.

* Issue may/may not reproduce amidst maximized memory usage; inanely excessive internet tabs + dozens of open apps + media apps running in background + 1k—2k+ words in a text field.

```

the space appears when the next character is typed, unless that next character is also a normal double-quote.

all autocorrect-type of features are disabled.

title: App crashes when long-pressing a symbol to reveal more characters
body:
**Describe the bug**
After having edited the symbols layout (via the "customize symbols layout" menu, I was able to consistently reproduce and then fix an app crash.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. open the openboard app
2. click advanced
3. under experimental, click "customize symbols layout" (or the other one, it should be the same)
4. add a space to the end of any key (remember which one it is, i used the line where the `_` underscore character was)
5. go to another app with a text input
6. long press the key with the modified symbol map (the underscore)

**Expected behavior**
keyboard crashes consistently when steps 5 and 6 repeated until step 4 is un-done 

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Google Pixel 6
 - OS: Android 14, LineageOS 21

**Additional context**
undoing this process fixed the issue. redoing it to a different key caused it to come back

Suggested solution (maybe its not viable): Trim whitespace from the end of each line of the key customization file upon saving (assuming non-newline whitespace characters are not allowed in this file)

title: Openboard breaks cursor location
body:
**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

When jumping between different parts of typed text while my phone is unfolded, my cursor becomes broken. I will select a new place to type in (I jump around a lot while typing), and the second I start typing, my cursor will instantly jump to the last word I was typing, delete a portion of it, and resume typing from the middle of that word. This makes longer-form typing in apps like Samsung Notes or Outlook Email a nightmare with openboard, as this happens multiple times while typing. It seems to happen more frequently when scrolling is needed to get from one paragraph to the next.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Type longer paragraphs, ideally enough that you have to scroll to get from one to another.
2. Attempt to jump around add sentences to different paragraphs.
3. After a few tries, the cursor issue should occur. It doesn't happen every time, but it is frequent.

**Expected behavior**
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

When moving the cursor and starting to type in a different location, the cursor should immediately jump to one of the last words you were typing, delete part of it, and resume your current typing in the middle of the word it jumped to.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Smartphone (please complete the following information):**
 - Device: Samsung Fold 5
 - OS: Android 14

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

 I haven't tested if this happens on my phone while folded, as I never type longer things that way. 

This bug has led me to switch back to the standard samsung keyboard for the time being.

The issue has not happened while typing this, even while jumping around a lot. I don't know if I was just really lucky, or if it only affects certain text boxes. But I normally would have experienced the issue by now in other text boxes.

title: When service is running and you open the notification, it fails to check if the service is running
body:
Hi,

I've noticed that when you start the service, the notification is created and the service is actually running, when you click on the notification to return to the main activity, it fails to check if the service is actually running even though it is running.

So if (service != null && service.isRunning()) always returns a false even though it is still running in the background but the main activity thinks it's not and behaves that way, resulting in no communication between the service and activity anymore.

title: Multithreading or Disable Stats
body:
I would like to be able to disable the automatic stats that have to calculate each and every time an activity is changed.

The reason for this is, while the app may seem okay when you first test it, it becomes horrendously slow when you have 6k+ entries.  To top that off, if you don't wait the entire time for it to do the tediously slow calculations and rearrange everything before you either close the app or "minimize" it, the last activity change won't even save.  You'll have to set it again, and manually edit the time.

Wait times have become anywhere between 10 to 60 sec.  It's not worth using the app if I need to set my phone down and wait that long.  I'd like to be able to just press a button, close the app, and know for 100% sure that it saved, without concerns of having to redo it.  The whole interaction with the app should last no more than a second, not a minute.

Thus, I propose a setting to end this automatic recalculation thing.  Also, simply arrange the activities according to the last one used.  I find this weighed calculation thing to not work as expected, anyway.

There is no need to recalculate stats for nearly 7,000 entries, every time, if it renders the app useless.

title: fail to update an active activity's pic when this activity is terminated or another activity is started
body:
- I have searched the history bug list, and have not found a similar bug.

- The bug was found on the latest version 1.4.0. The bug can be reproduced on an Android emulator 6.0 and a Google Pixel 3 XL phone.

 - Bug description

1. start an activity ``Cinema`` and take a pic
2. switch to the picture column where a pic exists (correct function)
3. click ``Cinema`` to terminate it (we can see the pic is still there, is this correct?)
3. click another activity ``Sleeping`` to start it (we can see the pic is still there, this should be incorrect)

- Video of incorrect functionality

![20200227_112456](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3156978/75447110-02b3d700-5969-11ea-978e-b46cd67ab250.gif)

title: Fail to load the pic after start-and-terminate an activity
body:
- I have searched the history bug list, and have not found a similar bug.

- The bug was found on the latest version 1.4.0. The bug can be reproduced on an Android emulator 6.0 and a Google Pixel 3 XL phone.

- Bug description

1. start an activity ``Cinema`` and terminate it
2. switch to the picture column
3. restart the activity ``Cinema`` (the issue also exists if starting any other activity, e.g., ``Cleaning``).

title: select addressbooks
body:
Present list of address books in the preferences so user can select them with checkmarks.
Lack of this causes #18 and should fix it properly.

title: crash on illegal input in preferences
body: Birthdroid doesn't correctly sanitize input in the prefs dialog and keeps crashing. As workaround, one can delete all app-data and restart the app.

title: remove widget upon uninstall
body: widget keeps on launcher but fails to load upon uninstall

title: Missing year causes the app to not display any birthdays
body:
In my (Google) contact list I have some contacts where the birthday date consists only of day and month (e.g. 16 January) because I don't know the exact birth year.

This causes the app to throw an error (Birthdays.findDateFormat:371) Could not find DateFormat for "--01-16". At least Google allows to have the year missing, so I guess it is a valid case. In this situation the year could be substituted with the upcoming year, or more easily Date formats of MM-dd could be allowed.

title: Once enabling "Auto-disable the eraser" and disabling it after never actually disables it
body:
### Describe the bug

So I had the option "Auto-disable the eraser" enabled, but I turned it off. But whenever I erase something, it auto-disables the eraser.

### To reproduce

1. Enable "Auto-disable the eraser"
2. Disable "Auto-disable the eraser"
3. Use eraser

### Expected behavior

The eraser still should be selected.

### Saber version

v1.29.5 Apple App Store

### Device

- Device: iPad 10th Gen
- OS: iPadOS 26.2.1 (problem already existed before that update)

### Anything else?

_No response_

title: When having PDF as background pasting an image turns the background white after saving!
body:
### Describe the bug

After pasting an image drawing a bit and saving multiple times the background turns white and my PDF page is gone!!


### To reproduce

1. Open Saber
2. Import a PDF as background
3. Insert an Image on top of one of the pages
4. Draw some stuff 
5. Save the Note
6. Repeat 4 and 5 a few times and the page turns white :/

### Expected behavior

I wanted the background to stay my PDF. I almost rely on the PDF not suddenly being gone and all of that error being synced to other devices.

### Saber version

version 133020 on Windows

### Device

- Device: Lenovo Thinkpad T490
- OS: Win11

title: [Storage/Performance] PDF Import duplicates full PDF binary for every page (Code Analysis included)
body:
### Describe the bug

I previously opened a discussion about file size, but after analyzing the source code, I believe this is a structural issue in how `EditorPage` handles backgrounds.

When importing a multi-page PDF, Saber generates a separate asset file (`.sbn0`, `.sbn1`, etc.) for each page. Instead of referencing the original file, it seems to duplicate the entire binary or creating a heavy rasterized copy for every single page.

Looking at `lib/data/editor/page.dart` and `lib/data/editor/editor_exporter.dart`, it appears that:

1. The `backgroundImage` field in `EditorPage` is treated as a standard `EditorImage` which gets serialized directly into the storage structure via `toJson`.
2. The reliance on `screenshotPage` in `editor_exporter.dart` suggests the app captures/stores a static bitmap for the background rather than rendering the PDF page from the source file on-the-fly.
3. The result is that a 2MB PDF with 100 pages doesn't occupy `~2MB + annotations`. It occupies `~200MB` because the background data is replicated 100 times.

### To reproduce

1. Import a PDF file (e.g., `document.pdf`, size: 2.17 MB, 452 pages).
2. Navigate to the Saber storage directory.
3. Observe the generated files:
- You will see numbered files (`.sbn0`, `.sbn1`... `.sbn103`).
- Check the file size of each `.sbnX` file.
- Result: Each individual `.sbnX` file is approximately the same size (2.17 MB) as the *entire* original PDF.

### Expected behavior


The app should implement a Linked/Layered approach (similar to Xournal++ or standard PDF annotators):

1. Store only the file path/URI of the source PDF.
2. The `.sbn2` file (or internal structure) should only contain the **vector metadata** (strokes, text) and a pointer to the page number.
3. The background should be rendered dynamically from the source PDF using a `pdf_renderer` library, without duplicating the binary data to disk.

- Goal: Storage usage should be `Size of Original PDF + Size of Vector Strokes` (kB), not exponential.

### Saber version

v1.29.5 F-Droid (129050)

### Device

- Android: Redmi Note 14
- Linux: IdeaPad 3 15ALC6 (Debian 13, KDE Plasma 6.3.6)

### Anything else?

First of all, thank you for this amazing project. I tested several handwritten note-taking apps and Saber was the best for me – and the fact that it's open source is a huge plus for the community.

Currently, I'm using it for my Phd and law studies, and I'd love for it to handle large PDFs more efficiently so I could use it as my main app for long reading sessions.

Keep up the great work! 🙂

title: Multiple saves of PDFs imported into Notes
body:
### Describe the bug

Whenever a PDF is added to a Saber note, Saber generates a duplicate of the original PDF for each page it contains with the file name  'name of note'.snb2.'number' . The File size and checksum is identical. Especially for long PDFs this generates massive amounts of data both local and in the Nextcloud. For example adding a 20 MB PDF with 100 pages to a note generates 2 GB of Data.

### To reproduce

Create a new Note. Add a multi page PDF to it. Save the Note.

### Expected behavior

Saving only one copy of each PDF added to a note.

v1.33.1, v1.31.1

title: Setting the sidewalk to separate doesn't remove sidewalk surface tags
body:
**How to Reproduce**
Given a road that has the following existing tags:

```
highway=residential
sidewalk=both
sidewalk:both:surface=asphalt
```

If I use the sidewalk overlay to indicate that the sidewalk has been separately mapped on both sides, the end result is:


```
highway=residential
sidewalk:both=separate
sidewalk:both:surface=asphalt
```

**Expected Behavior** 

For it to remove `sidewalk:both:surface` ie

```
highway=residential
sidewalk:both=separate
```

**Versions affected**
v63.1

title: Crash in MapDataWithEditsSource when a way in a bbox update has missing nodes
body:
(I'm posting this here because the file hasn't been modified in SCEE.)
SCEE crashes with a `NullPointerException` in `MapDataWithEditsSource.modifyBBoxMapData(...)` when processing map data updates. The crash happens at the point where the code assumes that all nodes of a way are available and uses `getWayNodes(way)!!`.

From the logcat, the app logs:

```FATAL EXCEPTION: DefaultDispatcher-worker-16 (Fix with AI)
Process: de.westnordost.streetcomplete.expert.debug, PID: 12152
java.lang.NullPointerException
at de.westnordost.streetcomplete.data.osm.edits.MapDataWithEditsSource.modifyBBoxMapData(MapDataWithEditsSource.kt:442)
at de.westnordost.streetcomplete.data.osm.edits.MapDataWithEditsSource.getMapDataWithGeometry(MapDataWithEditsSource.kt:273)
at de.westnordost.streetcomplete.screens.main.map.StyleableOverlayManager$setStyledElements$mapData$1$1.invokeSuspend(StyleableOverlayManager.kt:246)
at 
...
```

and then later crashes in the same code path. This suggests that a way can end up in the bbox-update handling with incomplete node data. The issue may be triggered by an edit state that can happen in SCEE but not in normal StreetComplete, so this may be a compatibility issue between the two codebases rather than a general StreetComplete workflow.

**How to Reproduce**

1. Open the app with local edits present.
2. Trigger a map data refresh / bbox update.
3. In my case, the app reaches `MapDataWithEditsSource.modifyBBoxMapData(...)`.
4. A way is processed whose nodes are not all available.
5. The app crashes with a `NullPointerException`.

**Expected Behavior**

The app should not crash if a way in the bbox update does not have all nodes available. It should handle the incomplete way gracefully, for example by skipping it, logging the problem, or rebuilding the affected map data state safely.

**Possible solution / workaround**
The issue could be fixed by removing the unsafe !! and handling incomplete ways gracefully, e.g. by skipping ways whose nodes are not fully available. Alternatively, ensure upstream that modifyBBoxMapData is only called with consistent map data where all referenced nodes are present. A defensive check with fallback behavior (skip or remove the way) would prevent crashes without breaking the flow.

This local workaround worked to get the app running again without loosing data:
```
for (way in addWays) {
            val nodes = getWayNodes(way)
            if (nodes == null) {
                Log.w(TAG, "Skipping way ${way.id} because not all nodes are available")
                continue
            }
            val geometries = nodes.map { ElementGeometryEntry(it.type, it.id, ElementPointGeometry(it.position)) }
            mapData.putAll(nodes, geometries)
        }
```

**Versions affected**

* SCEE: v63.0
* Android: 16

title: Android denies running the "ionice" command in non-root environments
body:
Problem
Android OS denies running the "ionice" command from shell returing exit code "127" "permission denied" unless we use a root shell.

Reproducer
a) Use syncthing app **without** the root option checked > Log says "ionice returned with exit code 127". Temporarily attaching the log()-dumper for this subshell process reveals a "permission denied" error message.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16361913/42750743-a344be6c-88e8-11e8-9c0b-6471240d86cf.png)

b) Use syncthing app **with** the root option checked > Log says "ionice returned with exit code 0". Temporarily attaching the log()-dumper for this subshell process just doesn't bring up an error message. Exit code 0 means "successfully io'niced process".
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16361913/42750780-c379f922-88e8-11e8-9d1d-69ee1ab6cd07.png)

### Version Information
    App Version: 0.10.11 (and earlier versions)
    Syncthing Version: v0.14.48 (not relevant)
    Android Version: Android 7.1.2

title: Mismatched/Incorrect Input Prompts
body:
Hi, there. Thank you for creating this app, first of all.
I just tried it out for the first time today and encounted a pretty serious issue on my first Hiragana Writing session. お was the first one and went fine. But, when I tried い (the next one shown), it kept rejecting my attempts to draw the first stroke. I thought it was just being very strict on following the nuances in the font/style used, but then it gave a red "hint" to draw a short dtroke straight across the top. I did that and it accepted it as the first stroke. The second stroke was the same deal, except it prompted a vertical stroke down the middle. That worked...
When I tried to draw い for the second stage, I completely missed what the first stroke was "supposed" to be, but it accepted what I tried to draw, and the second one was the second stroke of お!

I'll continue and let you know which other ones it does this for and how often.

The device I'm using is a Galaxy Note 3 running Lineage OS. Android version is 11.

It's getting really annoying. ||| Before: A really great app. Well done. Thank you. // Update after one week: The app is so great. Thank you, Mozilla ❤️❤️❤️

Report on notification issues shows that a number of notification failures potentially related to syncing app state with push notification state, which could be tied to the same issue. A delay between the server and local state could be related to inconsistent states. 

Could be related to [this v16.1 issue](https://github.com/thunderbird/thunderbird-android/issues/10713), though we'd have to investigate if the inclusion of the message list widget changes the problem. Could also be due to multiple processes attempting to load remote notifications, creating inconsistent states.  One user reported that the issue was not reliant on the message list:

> I don't use the widget but this affects me too. Am on A16, app version 17, from F-Droid, using since years ago back on A12 as "K-9 mail", move to A16 included device wipe so only shows recent date of install, but data/cache were restored from back then.

title: Potential main-thread blocking issue in KeyChainKeyManager
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [x] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App

Thunderbird for Android

### App version

Trunk / `main`

---

Hi Thunderbird for Android Team,

I’m a PhD student researching Android performance issues. My research group recently ran a static analysis scan for thread-affinity and main-thread blocking bugs, and our prototype flagged a potential issue in Thunderbird for Android / K-9 Mail.

## Checked source-level target

I checked the latest upstream source and found one app-owned source path in:

```text
legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/KeyChainKeyManager.java
```

Verified commit:

```text
416859a7f080db8ff0be7038a2aa43ed95f23dfc
```

The relevant constructor synchronously retrieves both the certificate chain and private key from Android `KeyChain`:

```java
63:  public KeyChainKeyManager(Context context, String alias) throws MessagingException {
64:      mAlias = alias;
65:      try {
66:          mChain = fetchCertificateChain(context, alias);
67:          mPrivateKey = fetchPrivateKey(context, alias);
68:      } catch (KeyChainException | InterruptedException e) {
69:          throw new ClientCertificateException(ClientCertificateError.RetrievalFailure, e);
70:      }
71:  }
```

The helper methods then reach the sensitive Android APIs:

```java
fetchCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getCertificateChain(context, alias)

fetchPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getPrivateKey(context, alias)
```

## What I found

`KeyChainKeyManager` performs Android KeyChain credential retrieval synchronously during construction.

This is risky if the constructor is ever reached from a UI-thread path, because Android documents both of the underlying platform APIs as potentially blocking operations that must not be called from the main thread:

```java
KeyChain.getCertificateChain(Context, String)
KeyChain.getPrivateKey(Context, String)
```

These calls can block while Android waits for a connection to another process, resolves credential access, or performs KeyChain-related work. The Android documentation also states that these methods may take several seconds to complete and should only be called from a worker thread.

I did not see an asynchronous boundary inside `KeyChainKeyManager` itself. Therefore, the safety of this code currently depends on every caller constructing `KeyChainKeyManager` only from a background thread.

## Verified source-level traces

```text
Trace 1: Certificate-chain retrieval

<com.fsck.k9.helper.KeyChainKeyManager: void <init>(android.content.Context, java.lang.String)>
  -> fetchCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getCertificateChain(context, alias)
  -> Android platform KeyChain service
  -> documented as blocking / worker-thread-only
  -> possible UI freeze, StrictMode violation, or IllegalStateException if reached from the main thread
```

```text
Trace 2: Private-key retrieval

<com.fsck.k9.helper.KeyChainKeyManager: void <init>(android.content.Context, java.lang.String)>
  -> fetchPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> android.security.KeyChain.getPrivateKey(context, alias)
  -> Android platform KeyChain service
  -> documented as blocking / worker-thread-only
  -> possible UI freeze, StrictMode violation, or IllegalStateException if reached from the main thread
```

## Why this matters

Client-certificate retrieval is usually part of account setup, mail transport setup, or TLS/client-authentication initialization. If this path is triggered from an Activity, Fragment, setup wizard, account settings screen, or any other UI-facing flow, the app may temporarily freeze while waiting for Android KeyChain.

On slower devices, locked/slow credential backends, or devices with unusual certificate providers, this could become visible jank or a delayed UI response. On Android versions that enforce the documented restriction, calling these APIs from the main thread may also throw `IllegalStateException`.

I am reporting this as a source-level main-thread blocking risk because I verified the current source pattern, but I have not yet produced an on-device runtime reproduction.

## Possible fix

The safest fix is to ensure that `KeyChainKeyManager` is only constructed from a worker thread, or to make the KeyChain retrieval path explicitly asynchronous.

For example, the credential retrieval could be moved behind an executor or existing background mail/account setup pipeline:

```java
ExecutorService executor = Executors.newSingleThreadExecutor();
Context appContext = context.getApplicationContext();

executor.execute(() -> {
    try {
        KeyChainKeyManager keyManager =
                new KeyChainKeyManager(appContext, alias);

        // Continue TLS / mail setup on the background path,
        // or post only the final UI result back to the main thread if needed.

    } catch (MessagingException e) {
        // Handle retrieval failure.
        // If UI feedback is needed, post that part back to the main thread.
    }
});
```

Another option is to document and enforce the threading contract at the call boundary, for example by asserting that `KeyChainKeyManager` is never constructed on the main thread and by moving all callsites into existing background setup code.

One additional nuance: the Android documentation recommends using a long-lived context such as `getApplicationContext()` for these KeyChain calls, because Activity and Service contexts can be short-lived.

## References

- Android platform API: `android.security.KeyChain#getCertificateChain(Context, String)`
- Android platform API: `android.security.KeyChain#getPrivateKey(Context, String)`

- Link: https://developer.android.com/reference/android/security/KeyChain#getCertificateChain(android.content.Context,%20java.lang.String)

Relevant Android documentation wording:

> This method may block while waiting for a connection to another process, and must never be called from the main thread.

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.

title: Importing settings fails to add avatar monogram
body:
When importing older settings like the QR code (version 99), the settings migration 104 should add the avatar monogram based on the user name or email. This fails as the `name` and `email` settings are not accessible during the settings upgrade.

The issue is caused by using the wrong type of migration that just migrates account settings but not account information like name, email, identities etc.

To fix the issue we need to use the `CombinedSettingsUpgrader` instead.

This is the source of users importing settings from Desktop or from previously exported settings will end up with a `XX` avatar monogram.

title: Various issues in attachments since v18: images no longer visible, bad UX
body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report.
- [x] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible.

### App

Thunderbird for Android

### App version

18

### Where did you get the app from?

None

### Android version

15

### Device model

_No response_

### Steps to reproduce

Open a message with attachements

### Expected behavior

Previously you could see the list of attachments at the end of the message body.

This was not the best place but you could preview images, and click on each image one by one. A single click was required to open an attachment.

### Actual behavior

Now the attachment UI is difficult to use: you need to click on a very small "View all" button (which doesn't look like a button at all, and is way too small, I struggle to click it with my finger), clicking on the "4 attachments" text does nothing.

This opens a menu at the bottom of the screen (meaning you need to move your finger).

There is no preview of images, so when the file name is a UUID, you don't know what the image is.

If you click on an attachment name in that menu, the menu disappears, meaning you have to re-click the small "view all" button which reopens the attachments menu. Now you don't know where you were when you have 20 attachments with similar file names.

It has become very hard to view attachments now because of that "redesign".

Good UX principles should always be followed:

* text buttons should look like buttons, not like text
* click zones should be large enough
* the interface should not "jump" off when you open an attachment
* image attachments should have a largish preview

The new UX is a regression and should have been tested before deployment. I understand that having attachments at the end of the body is not the best (as you have to scroll), but the new UI is worse in every aspect.

### Logs

_No response_

title: Weird behaviour and system crash
body:
## Problem description

Disclaimer: I don't know if this is an issue with this app or with the OS. I don't see this issue on a different phone running stock Android.

I recently noticed a stability issue happening when I play back ogg music. At first songs play fine (mostly the first one), then the app starts skipping songs in the playlist after a brief pause on each song, as if it's unable to play it. Sometimes the whole playlist is unplayable and sometimes it resumes playback from one of the following songs.
If I click back on an already played song (for example the first), the app skips it and it's unable to play it back again even if it just worked fine. It mostly seems to work on freshly downloaded songs only, but it's hard to tell because the behaviour is really erratic.

Clicking on "unplayable" songs enough times leads to the app freezing, the UI restarting or even the phone crashing entirely and producing a core dump.

I don't know since when this issue started because I had set my music server (Navidrome) to transcode to opus, and playback is mostly fine there. Yesterday I removed the transcoding rule and now I'm just streaming the original ogg files, which caused the problem I described above. Switching back to opus seems to mitigate this issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. get a Navidrome server with some ogg files on it
2. make sure it's got no transcoding setting for the Ultrasonic player
3. from Ultrasonic pick an album and play it
4. wait for the first song to finish
5. alternative 1: click on the progress bar to skip part of the song
6. alternative 2: while the first song is playing click on the second one in the playlist
7. the second song won't play, most of the times the third will, but just once, then the fifth and so on (this is the overall behaviour I observed, but it's not very consistent)

## System information

 * **Ultrasonic version**: *2.24.0*
 * **Android version**: *LineageOS 18.1 24th Dec 2021*
 * **Device info**: *Asus Zenfone 8*

## Additional notes

I don't know if this is a problem with some systems components provided by the OS.
From my laptop everything works fine (browser and Strawberry player) and I don't see any error on the Navidrome side.

After the problem starts occurring, even if the system doesn't crash I still have to reboot it as sound is completely gone. For example youtube videos in Newpipe will have no sound, and sometimes play in a laggy/slow way.

Sometimes closing Ultrasonic becomes impossible without a full reboot, ie I click on the `X` button in the play widget from the drop down system menu and nothing happens.

title: Fragment injection does not occur. NullPointerException for Android versions 22 (Lollipop 5.1) and below.
body:
## Crash

The following crash occurs on devices running API 22 (Lollipop 5.1) and below.

```
   java.lang.NullPointerException: Attempt to invoke interface method 'void com.vestrel00.daggerbutterknifemvp.ui.main.view.MainFragmentListener.onExample1Clicked()' on a null object reference
       at com.vestrel00.daggerbutterknifemvp.ui.main.view.MainFragment.onExample1Clicked(MainFragment.java:63)
      ...
```

The crash does not occur for API 23 (Marshmallow 6.0) and above.

#### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the app
2. Click on Example 1 button

Observe the crash. Note that clicking on any of the example buttons will cause the crash.

## Analysis

The issue is that the `MainFragmentListener listener` in `MainFragment` is null for some reason. This suggests a bug with Dagger 2.11. 

The issue is that the injection is currently happening in [onAttach(Context)](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/Fragment.html#onAttach(android.content.Context)), which is added in API level 23. This means that API level 22 and below will not invoke `onAttach(Context)`, thereby bypassing the injection. 

## Solutions

1. Perform fragment injection in [onAttach(Activity)](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/Fragment.html#onAttach(android.app.Activity)).
2. Use AppCompat Activity and support Fragment with *dagger.android.support* injection frameworks.

title: Fatal exception error occurred while trying to end screen recording
body:
#### Expected behavior Screen cam should be able to save the screen recordings even if they are small. #### Actual behavior I started recording a screen video by shaking my phone and wanted to end it immediately with another shake but the video was not saved displaying a message "fatal error occurred". #### How to reproduce - Start recording on screen cam by shaking your phone - Immediately end it with another shake and the error message will be displayed and video will not be saved. * Application : Screen Cam screen recorder v1.8.5 * Operating system: Android 6.0.1 #### Recording Of The Bug https://youtu.be/QrLld58SUlw
Posted on Utopian.io - Rewarding Open Source Contributors

title: crash when editing video
body: hey i have a problem and that is after building project when i try to edit the saved video, app crashesh on multiple devices (m above)

title: Floating bar
body:
1. It is way too big, at least on devices like mine < 720p.
2. I can't deactivate it, every time I start a recording it switches on by itself.
3. It should be invisible on the recording itself, like the simulated screen (which you activate in the developer settings) is.

This app is still very cool.

title: App crashes
body:
[crash.txt](https://github.com/vijai1996/screenrecorder/files/602390/crash.txt)

Hey bro, thanks a lot for this useful app, and thanks even more for having it on f-droid! Unfortunately I the app crashes very frequently on my phone. It's an Android 6.0.1. It seems to be a problem with switch to and from the app with the recent apps menu. After switching back to screenrecorder the rec button shows the stop symbol and the app crashes when I press it. I also get notified that the app crashed if I swipe it away in the recent apps menu.
I attached a log, hope it can help...

title: Establish embassy not working for mods where embassies aren't required for diplomacy
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues

### Game Version

4.20.4

### Describe the bug

For the purposes of mods which want embassies for the capital information they grant it seems like the current implementation makes that impossible.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a mod without the unique "Requires establishing embassies to conduct advanced diplomacy", but with the unique "Enables establishment of embassies". 
2. Expected result: Embassies can be established, but do not lock out other diplomacy options
Actual result: embassies cannot be established


### Screenshots

_No response_

_No response_

### Operating System

Windows

### Additional Information

_No response_

title: Building with a gradle daemon under Java 25 fails
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues

### Game Version

3f2a181d2d4d15455a47cdeb56b99d04f5716cdd

### Describe the bug

Seems a recent Java 25 update is incompatible with Gradle 8.11.1.
Running, debugging, etc Unciv from Android Studio using the Jetbrains JBR - no problem.
Building a JAR or running any gradlew command from terminal, using system Java, fails.

### Steps to Reproduce

- Update your system Java to adoptium temurin 25.0.3
- Run `./gradlew check` from terminal


### Screenshots

```bash
~/Work/Unciv git:[ReplaceAudio]
./gradlew check
WARNING: A restricted method in java.lang.System has been called
WARNING: java.lang.System::load has been called by net.rubygrapefruit.platform.internal.NativeLibraryLoader in an unnamed module (file:/home/bob/.gradle/wrapper/dists/gradle-8.11.1-bin/bpt9gzteqjrbo1mjrsomdt32c/gradle-8.11.1/lib/native-platform-0.22-milestone-26.jar)
WARNING: Use --enable-native-access=ALL-UNNAMED to avoid a warning for callers in this module
WARNING: Restricted methods will be blocked in a future release unless native access is enabled


FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
25.0.3

* Try:
> Run with --stacktrace option to get the stack trace.
> Run with --info or --debug option to get more log output.
> Run with --scan to get full insights.
> Get more help at https://help.gradle.org.

BUILD FAILED in 2s
```

### Link to save file

n/a

### Operating System

Linux

### Additional Information

See also: #13976
See also: https://github.com/gradle/gradle/issues?q=net.rubygrapefruit.platform.internal.NativeLibraryLoader - looks like chaos to me

title: Current master shows file location quirks on Android
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues

### Game Version

3f2a181d2d4d15455a47cdeb56b99d04f5716cdd

### Describe the bug

Commit 3f2a181d2d4d15455a47cdeb56b99d04f5716cdd changed stuff in AndroidLauncher. At first I was only suspicious because change and title didn't match. Now I tested on a device...
- Settings are lost, it starts over from language picker
- Installed Mods aren't displayed in Mod manager's left side, with one exception in my case, the one I transferred from desktop via usb.
- Plays music from mods mod manager doesn't show
- mod manager's online listing never fills

### Steps to Reproduce

- Pull current master
- run the "android" run config

_No response_

_No response_

Android

Still LOS 19 = Android 12 on my device

title: Game elements hidden under camera notch - Android 15
body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [x] I have searched the existing issues

### Game Version

4.15.11

### Describe the bug

On my phone, Unciv seems to be using the entire screen, without considering the camera notch. This leaves some UI elements hidden under the notch. I couldn't find any display settings to change this behaviour. It occurs both in portrait and in landscape mode.

I have previously played Unciv on the same phone without this bug. However, that was on an older OS version _and_ an older Unciv version. After recently upgrading my OS, I decided to install Unciv again. It's not clear if the OS upgrade or a recent Unciv version introduced this behaviour.

### Steps to Reproduce

I guess this is device specific, so it's probably not easy to reproduce on a different device.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Link to save file

_No response_

### Operating System

Android

### Additional Information

LineageOS 22.1 (Android 15) on a Xiaomi Poco F1

title: Potential main-thread blocking issue in ConnectYou shortcut update flow
body:
Hi ConnectYou Team,

I’m a PhD student researching Android performance issues. My research group recently ran a static analysis scan for thread-affinity and main-thread blocking bugs, and our prototype flagged a potential issue in ConnectYou.

## Checked target

+ Source-level caller: `com.bnyro.contacts.App.onCreate() -> ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(Context)`
+ Detected APIs: `ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(...)` and `ShortcutManagerCompat.pushDynamicShortcut(...)`
+ Related platform APIs: `ShortcutManager#getDynamicShortcuts()` / dynamic-shortcut publishing APIs
+ Observed context: app startup / `Application.onCreate()` main thread
+ Expected context: worker/background thread

## What I found

The current `App.onCreate()` initializes shortcuts during application startup:

```kotlin
class App : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this)
        DatabaseHolder.init(this)
        Preferences.init(this)
        BackupWorker.enqueue(this)
        NotificationHelper.createChannels(this)
        initSmsRepo()
    }
}
```

`ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(...)` checks existing dynamic shortcuts and then creates the app shortcuts synchronously:

```kotlin
fun createShortcuts(context: Context) {
    ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(context).takeIf { it.isEmpty() } ?: return

    AppShortcut.entries.forEach { appShortcut ->
        createShortcut(context, appShortcut, false)
    }
}
```

The helper eventually publishes each shortcut through `ShortcutManagerCompat.pushDynamicShortcut(...)`:

```kotlin
private fun createShortcut(
    context: Context,
    appShortcut: AppShortcut,
    pin: Boolean,
) {
    ...

    if (!pin) {
        ShortcutManagerCompat.pushDynamicShortcut(context, shortcut)
    } else {
        ShortcutManagerCompat.requestPinShortcut(context, shortcut, null)
    }
}
```

The concern is that the Android platform shortcut APIs for querying and publishing shortcuts are documented as potentially slow and worker-thread-only. Since this startup path is called directly from `Application.onCreate()`, the query and shortcut publication can run on the main thread.

## Verified bug trace

```text
App process starts
  -> App.onCreate()
  -> ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this)
  -> ShortcutManagerCompat.getDynamicShortcuts(context)
  -> if the dynamic shortcut list is empty:
       createShortcut(..., pin = false)
       -> ShortcutManagerCompat.pushDynamicShortcut(context, shortcut)
       -> underlying Android shortcut-manager operation
  -> Android documentation marks shortcut query/publish operations as potentially slow and worker-thread-only
  -> possible cold-start delay / startup jank / ANR risk
```

I did not dynamically reproduce an ANR or crash, so this should be treated as a source-confirmed main-thread blocking risk rather than a reproduced runtime failure. The relevant call pattern is still present in the current source.

## Why this matters

This code runs during application startup. Shortcut-manager calls go through Android system services and may take longer depending on launcher state, user state, device speed, or Android version. Performing this work synchronously on the startup main thread can delay the first screen and create avoidable startup jank.

## Possible fix

Move shortcut initialization to a worker context. For example:

```kotlin
class App : Application() {
    private val appScope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.Default)

    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()

        appScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            ShortcutHelper.createShortcuts(this@App)
        }

        DatabaseHolder.init(this)
        Preferences.init(this)
        BackupWorker.enqueue(this)
        NotificationHelper.createChannels(this)
        initSmsRepo()
    }
}
```

Alternatively, a small `Executor` could be used instead of coroutines. The key point is to avoid calling shortcut query/publish APIs synchronously from `Application.onCreate()`.

## Reference

Android API: `android.content.pm.ShortcutManager`

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/pm/ShortcutManager

Relevant Android documentation wording for the affected shortcut APIs:

> This method may take several seconds to complete, so it should only be called from a worker thread.


## Source references

+ Current app startup source: https://github.com/you-apps/ConnectYou/blob/main/app/src/main/java/com/bnyro/contacts/App.kt
+ Current shortcut helper source: https://github.com/you-apps/ConnectYou/blob/main/app/src/main/java/com/bnyro/contacts/util/ShortcutsHelper.kt


### Connect You version

Trunk


### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have searched the existing issues and this is a new ticket, **NOT** a duplicate or related to another open issue.
- [x] I have written a short but informative title.
- [x] I will fill out all of the requested information in this form.

title: Editing contact and then going back decrements Event dates by 1
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create contact with an event set to some date. 
2. (Also note that the intended date is not the one that is selected. Probably a related bug but different situation)
3. Save contact. 
4. Press edit contact button
5. Observe decremented event date in edit contact details view. 
6. Input back gesture (or press). 
7. Observe that the decremented date is now saved. 
8. Repeat until your contact’s birthday is now in 800 CE. 

### Expected behavior

Event dates should not decrement. 

### Actual behavior

The date of the event decrements. 

### Connect You version

2.1

### Android version

Android 13

### Other details

What’s interesting is that going back shouldn’t save any edited contact details but the event date still decrements without saving.

title: Stops changing local source wallpaper after a time
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Select local source for wallpaper from a directory for my home screen
2. Set interval to 4 hours
3. Click OK
4. Enable Wallpaper changer
5. Exit

### Expected behavior

I expect the wallpaper to change every 4 hours, choosing a random picture from a local directory.



### Actual behavior

It works for a day or two, but then it suddenly stops switching to a new photo for some unknown reason. I have had the same wallpaper now for about 3 days. Sometimes, setting the interval to a different value fixes the issue, but again, for a day or two, then it's just stuck on one image. Enabling and disabling the changer usually doesn't fix the issue.


### Wall You version

13.1 (35)

### Android version

Android 13

### Other details

- Battery usage is set to unrestricted for the app
- I don't switch off my phone, reboot seldomly. Not sure if that's relevant, though.

### Acknowledgements

title: Favorites accidentally removed while trying to scroll
body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Open favorites
2. With your finger starting on a favorite item, scroll vertically.

### Expected behavior

I expect to scroll vertically with relative ease.

### Actual behavior

Instead, the item box catches my thumb and often swipes to the side instead. This regularly results in accidentally removing favorited items.

### Wall You version

13.1 (35)

### Android version

Android 16

### Other details

I haven't spent much time on this. I went in and flipped some booleans to disable the feature for my build. It's hard to say from a user perspective if the 'swipe to remove'  implementation has problems or if may the motion thresholds need adjustment. For my part, I wouldn't mine the option to disable the functionality entirely. Simply tapping the heart to unfavorite seems fine to me and reduces complexity and problems.

### Acknowledgements

- [x] I have searched the existing issues and this is a new ticket, **NOT** a duplicate or related to another open issue.
